title: Overview of BayesianQDM
text: BayesianQDM package provides Bayesian Quantitative Decision-Making (QDM) framework proof--concept (PoC) clinical trials. framework supports three-zone Go/NoGo/Gray decision rule, Go decision recommends proceeding next development phase, NoGo decision recommends stopping development, Gray decision indicates insufficient evidence make definitive recommendation. primary use case evaluation operating characteristics PoC trial specified decision rule sample size. Specifically, package can answer following practical questions: probability making Go, NoGo, Gray decision given true treatment effect? decision thresholds best control false Go rate false NoGo rate simultaneously?

title: Overview of BayesianQDM
text: package supports wide range PoC trial configurations combination following dimensions.

title: Overview of BayesianQDM
text: Binary endpoint: e.g., response rate, treatment effect difference response rates θ=πt−πc\theta = \pi_t - \pi_c. Continuous endpoint: e.g., biomarker clinical score, treatment effect difference means θ=μt−μc\theta = \mu_t - \mu_c. single-endpoint two co-primary endpoint settings available.

title: Overview of BayesianQDM
text: Two complementary metrics implemented quantify evidence PoC data: Posterior probability: probability, given observed PoC data, true treatment effect exceeds clinically meaningful threshold. metric directly reflects evidence accumulated PoC trial. Posterior predictive probability: probability future (e.g., Phase III) trial achieve pre-specified success criterion, given observed PoC data. metric forward-looking accounts uncertainty future trial outcomes.

title: Overview of BayesianQDM
text: Three study designs supported: Controlled design: standard parallel-group trial concurrent treatment control groups. Uncontrolled design: single-arm trial control distribution specified prior knowledge rather observed data. External design: design incorporates historical external data treatment group, control group, , via power prior user-specified borrowing weight α0e∈(0,1]\alpha_{0e} \(0, 1].

title: Overview of BayesianQDM
text: Vague prior (Jeffreys prior): non-informative prior allows observed PoC data dominate posterior. Conjugate prior: informative prior specified hyperparameters, combined likelihood closed form.

title: Overview of BayesianQDM
code: # P(pi_treat - pi_ctrl > 0.05 | data) # Observed: 7/10 responders (treatment), 3/10 (control) pbayespostpred1bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled', theta0 = 0.05,   n_t = 10, n_c = 10, y_t = 7, y_c = 3,   a_t = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_c = 0.5,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL, z = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, ye_t = NULL, ye_c = NULL,   alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE ) #> [1] 0.941619

code: # Find gamma_go  : smallest gamma s.t. Pr(Go)   < 0.05 under Null (pi_t = pi_c = 0.15) # Find gamma_nogo: smallest gamma s.t. Pr(NoGo) < 0.20 under Alt  (pi_t = 0.35, pi_c = 0.15) gg_res <- getgamma1bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled',   theta_TV = 0.30, theta_MAV = 0.10, theta_NULL = NULL,   pi_t_go = 0.15, pi_c_go = 0.15,   pi_t_nogo = 0.35, pi_c_nogo = 0.15,   target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,   n_t = 12L, n_c = 12L,   a_t = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_c = 0.5,   z = NULL, m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, ye_t = NULL, ye_c = NULL,   alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL ) plot(gg_res, base_size = 20)

title: Overview of BayesianQDM
text: vignette("single-binary") — detailed examples single binary endpoint, covering three designs prior specifications. vignette("single-continuous") — detailed examples single continuous endpoint, covering three designs calculation methods. vignette("two-binary") — detailed examples two binary co-primary endpoints. vignette("two-continuous") — detailed examples two continuous co-primary endpoints.

title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: use hypothetical Phase IIa proof--concept (PoC) trial moderate--severe rheumatoid arthritis (RA). investigational drug compared placebo 1:1 randomised controlled trial nt=nc=12n_t = n_c = 12 patients per group. Endpoint: ACR20 response rate (proportion patients achieving ≥20%\ge 20\% improvement ACR criteria). Clinically meaningful thresholds (posterior probability): Target Value (TV) = 0.20 (treatment–control difference) Minimum Acceptable Value (MAV) = 0.05 Null hypothesis threshold (predictive probability): θNULL=0.10\theta_{\mathrm{NULL}} = 0.10. Decision thresholds: γgo=0.80\gamma_{\mathrm{go}} = 0.80 (Go), γnogo=0.20\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}} = 0.20 (NoGo). Observed data (used Sections 2–4): yt=8y_t = 8 responders nt=12n_t = 12 (treatment); yc=3y_c = 3 responders nc=12n_c = 12 (control).

title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: Let πj\pi_j denote true response rate group jj (j=tj = t: treatment, j=cj = c: control). model number responders yj∣πj∼Binomial(nj,πj),y_j \mid \pi_j \;\sim\; \mathrm{Binomial}(n_j,\, \pi_j), place independent Beta priors rate: πj∼Beta(aj,bj),\pi_j \;\sim\; \mathrm{Beta}(a_j,\, b_j), aj>0a_j > 0 bj>0b_j > 0 shape parameters. Jeffreys prior corresponds aj=bj=0.5a_j = b_j = 0.5, giving weakly informative, symmetric prior (0,1)(0, 1). uniform prior corresponds aj=bj=1a_j = b_j = 1.

title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: conjugacy Beta-Binomial model, posterior observing yjy_j responders among njn_j patients πj∣yj∼Beta(aj*,bj*),\pi_j \mid y_j \;\sim\; \mathrm{Beta}(a_j^*,\, b_j^*), posterior shape parameters aj*=aj+yj,bj*=bj+nj−yj.a_j^* = a_j + y_j, \qquad b_j^* = b_j + n_j - y_j. posterior mean variance E[πj∣yj]=aj*aj*+bj*,Var(πj∣yj)=aj*bj*(aj*+bj*)2(aj*+bj*+1).E[\pi_j \mid y_j] = \frac{a_j^*}{a_j^* + b_j^*}, \qquad   \mathrm{Var}(\pi_j \mid y_j) =   \frac{a_j^*\, b_j^*}{(a_j^* + b_j^*)^2\,(a_j^* + b_j^* + 1)}.

title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: treatment effect θ=πt−πc\theta = \pi_t - \pi_c. Since two groups independent, θ∣yt,yc\theta \mid y_t, y_c follows distribution difference two independent Beta random variables: πt∣yt∼Beta(*,bt*),πc∣yc∼Beta(ac*,bc*).\pi_t \mid y_t \;\sim\; \mathrm{Beta}(a_t^*,\, b_t^*), \qquad   \pi_c \mid y_c \;\sim\; \mathrm{Beta}(a_c^*,\, b_c^*). closed-form CDF θ=πt−πc\theta = \pi_t - \pi_c, posterior probability P(θ>θ0∣yt,yc)=P(πt−πc>θ0∣yt,yc)P(\theta > \theta_0 \mid y_t, y_c) = P(\pi_t - \pi_c > \theta_0   \mid y_t, y_c) evaluated convolution integral implemented pbetadiff(): P(πt−πc>θ0)=∫01FBeta(ac*,bc*)(x−θ0)fBeta(*,bt*)(x)dx,P(\pi_t - \pi_c > \theta_0) =   \int_0^1   F_{\mathrm{Beta}(a_c^*, b_c^*)}\!\left(x - \theta_0\right)\,   f_{\mathrm{Beta}(a_t^*, b_t^*)}\!(x)\; dx, FBeta(α,β)F_{\mathrm{Beta}(\alpha,\beta)} Beta CDF fBeta(α,β)f_{\mathrm{Beta}(\alpha,\beta)} Beta PDF. one-dimensional integral evaluated adaptive Gauss-Kronrod quadrature via stats::integrate().

title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: Let ỹj∣πj∼Binomial(mj,πj)\tilde{y}_j \mid \pi_j \sim \mathrm{Binomial}(m_j, \pi_j) future trial counts mjm_j future patients. Integrating πj\pi_j Beta posterior gives Beta-Binomial predictive distribution: P(ỹj=k∣yj)=(mjk)B(aj*+k,bj*+mj−k)B(aj*,bj*),k=0,1,…,mj,P(\tilde{y}_j = k \mid y_j) =   \binom{m_j}{k}   \frac{B(a_j^* + k,\; b_j^* + m_j - k)}{B(a_j^*,\; b_j^*)},   \quad k = 0, 1, \ldots, m_j, B(⋅,⋅)B(\cdot, \cdot) Beta function.

what: 2.2 Posterior Predictive Probability of Future Success
title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: posterior predictive probability future treatment difference exceeds θNULL\theta_{\mathrm{NULL}} P(ỹtmt−ỹcmc>θNULL|yt,yc)=∑kt=0mt∑kc=0mc𝟏[ktmt−kcmc>θNULL]P(ỹt=kt∣yt)P(ỹc=kc∣yc).P\!\left(\frac{\tilde{y}_t}{m_t} - \frac{\tilde{y}_c}{m_c}   > \theta_{\mathrm{NULL}} \;\Big|\; y_t, y_c\right)   = \sum_{k_t=0}^{m_t} \sum_{k_c=0}^{m_c}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\frac{k_t}{m_t} - \frac{k_c}{m_c} >   \theta_{\mathrm{NULL}}\right]\,   P(\tilde{y}_t = k_t \mid y_t)\,   P(\tilde{y}_c = k_c \mid y_c). double sum (mt+1)(mc+1)(m_t + 1)(m_c + 1) outcome combinations evaluated exactly pbetabinomdiff().

title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: Standard parallel-group RCT observed data groups. posterior shape parameters group jj aj*=aj+yj,bj*=bj+nj−yj,a_j^* = a_j + y_j, \qquad b_j^* = b_j + n_j - y_j, posterior probability P(πt−πc>θ0∣yt,yc)P(\pi_t - \pi_c > \theta_0 \mid y_t, y_c) computed via pbetadiff(). Posterior probability TV MAV: Posterior predictive probability (future Phase III: mt=mc=40m_t = m_c = 40): Vectorised computation possible outcomes: function accepts vectors y_t y_c, enabling efficient computation posterior probability every possible outcome pair (yt,yc)∈{0,…,nt}×{0,…,nc}(y_t, y_c) \\{0,\ldots,n_t\} \times \{0,\ldots,n_c\}.
code:
# P(pi_t - pi_c > TV  | data) p_tv <- pbayespostpred1bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled', theta0 = 0.20,   n_t = 12, n_c = 12, y_t = 8, y_c = 3,   a_t = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_c = 0.5,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL, z = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, ye_t = NULL, ye_c = NULL,   alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE )  # P(pi_t - pi_c > MAV | data) p_mav <- pbayespostpred1bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled', theta0 = 0.05,   n_t = 12, n_c = 12, y_t = 8, y_c = 3,   a_t = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_c = 0.5,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL, z = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, ye_t = NULL, ye_c = NULL,   alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE )  cat(sprintf("P(theta > TV  | data) = %.4f  -> Go  criterion (>= %.2f): %s
",             p_tv,  0.80, ifelse(p_tv  >= 0.80, "YES", "NO"))) #> P(theta > TV  | data) = 0.8517  -> Go  criterion (>= 0.80): YES cat(sprintf("P(theta <= MAV | data) = %.4f  -> NoGo criterion (>= %.2f): %s
",             1 - p_mav, 0.20, ifelse((1 - p_mav) >= 0.20, "YES", "NO"))) #> P(theta <= MAV | data) = 0.0347  -> NoGo criterion (>= 0.20): NO cat(sprintf("Decision: %s
",             ifelse(p_tv >= 0.80 & (1 - p_mav) < 0.20, "Go",                    ifelse(p_tv < 0.80 & (1 - p_mav) >= 0.20, "NoGo",                           ifelse(p_tv >= 0.80 & (1 - p_mav) >= 0.20, "Miss", "Gray"))))) #> Decision: Go p_pred <- pbayespostpred1bin(   prob = 'predictive', design = 'controlled', theta0 = 0.10,   n_t = 12, n_c = 12, y_t = 8, y_c = 3,   a_t = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_c = 0.5,   m_t = 40, m_c = 40, z = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, ye_t = NULL, ye_c = NULL,   alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE ) cat(sprintf("Predictive probability (m_t = m_c = 40) = %.4f
", p_pred)) #> Predictive probability (m_t = m_c = 40) = 0.9053 grid <- expand.grid(y_t = 0:12, y_c = 0:12) p_all <- pbayespostpred1bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled', theta0 = 0.20,   n_t = 12, n_c = 12, y_t = grid$y_t, y_c = grid$y_c,   a_t = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_c = 0.5,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL, z = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, ye_t = NULL, ye_c = NULL,   alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE ) # Show results for a selection of outcome pairs sel <- data.frame(y_t = grid$y_t, y_c = grid$y_c, P_gt_TV = round(p_all, 4)) head(sel[order(-sel$P_gt_TV), ], 10) #>    y_t y_c P_gt_TV #> 12  11   0  1.0000 #> 13  12   0  1.0000 #> 26  12   1  1.0000 #> 11  10   0  0.9998 #> 39  12   2  0.9998 #> 25  11   1  0.9997 #> 10   9   0  0.9992 #> 52  12   3  0.9992 #> 24  10   1  0.9982 #> 38  11   2  0.9982

title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: concurrent control group enrolled, hypothetical control responder count zz specified historical knowledge. control posterior πc∣z∼Beta(ac+z,bc+nc−z),\pi_c \mid z \;\sim\; \mathrm{Beta}(a_c + z,\; b_c + n_c - z), ncn_c hypothetical control sample size zz assumed number hypothetical responders (0≤z≤nc0 \le z \le n_c). treatment group data yty_t observed; ycy_c set NULL. Effect hypothetical control count: varying zz shows sensitivity assumed control rate.
code: # Hypothetical control: z = 2 responders out of n_c = 12 p_unctrl <- pbayespostpred1bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'uncontrolled', theta0 = 0.20,   n_t = 12, n_c = 12, y_t = 8, y_c = NULL,   a_t = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_c = 0.5,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL, z = 2L,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, ye_t = NULL, ye_c = NULL,   alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE ) cat(sprintf("P(theta > TV | data, uncontrolled, z=2) = %.4f\n", p_unctrl)) #> P(theta > TV | data, uncontrolled, z=2) = 0.9338 z_seq <- 0:12 p_z   <- sapply(z_seq, function(z) {   pbayespostpred1bin(     prob = 'posterior', design = 'uncontrolled', theta0 = 0.20,     n_t = 12, n_c = 12, y_t = 8, y_c = NULL,     a_t = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_c = 0.5,     m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL, z = z,     ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, ye_t = NULL, ye_c = NULL,     alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,     lower.tail = FALSE   ) }) data.frame(z = z_seq, P_gt_TV = round(p_z, 4)) #>     z P_gt_TV #> 1   0  0.9968 #> 2   1  0.9787 #> 3   2  0.9338 #> 4   3  0.8517 #> 5   4  0.7297 #> 6   5  0.5760 #> 7   6  0.4099 #> 8   7  0.2558 #> 9   8  0.1350 #> 10  9  0.0571 #> 11 10  0.0177 #> 12 11  0.0034 #> 13 12  0.0002

title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: Historical external data incorporated via power prior borrowing weight α0ej∈(0,1]\alpha_{0ej} \(0, 1]. augmented prior shape parameters aj*=aj+α0ejyej,bj*=bj+α0ej(nej−yej),a_j^* = a_j + \alpha_{0ej}\, y_{ej}, \qquad   b_j^* = b_j + \alpha_{0ej}\,(n_{ej} - y_{ej}), nejn_{ej} yejy_{ej} external sample size responder count group jj (corresponding ne_t/ne_c, ye_t/ye_c, alpha0e_t/alpha0e_c). serve prior current PoC data, yielding closed-form posterior: πj∣yj∼Beta(aj*+yj,bj*+nj−yj).\pi_j \mid y_j \;\sim\;   \mathrm{Beta}\!\left(a_j^* + y_j,\; b_j^* + n_j - y_j\right). Setting α0ej=1\alpha_{0ej} = 1 corresponds full borrowing (external data treated came current trial); α0ej→0\alpha_{0ej} \0 recovers result current data alone original prior. , external data groups (external design): net=15n_{et} = 15, yet=5y_{et} = 5, nec=15n_{ec} = 15, yec=4y_{ec} = 4, borrowing weights α0et=α0ec=0.5\alpha_{0et} = \alpha_{0ec} = 0.5: Effect borrowing weight: varying α0ec\alpha_{0ec} (control group ) fixed α0et=0.5\alpha_{0et} = 0.5:
code: p_ext <- pbayespostpred1bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'external', theta0 = 0.20,   n_t = 12, n_c = 12, y_t = 8, y_c = 3,   a_t = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_c = 0.5,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL, z = NULL,   ne_t = 15L, ne_c = 15L, ye_t = 5L, ye_c = 4L,   alpha0e_t = 0.5, alpha0e_c = 0.5,   lower.tail = FALSE ) cat(sprintf("P(theta > TV | data, external, alpha0e=0.5) = %.4f\n", p_ext)) #> P(theta > TV | data, external, alpha0e=0.5) = 0.6874 # alpha0e must be in (0, 1]; avoid alpha0e = 0 ae_seq <- c(0.01, seq(0.1, 1.0, by = 0.1)) p_ae   <- sapply(ae_seq, function(ae) {   pbayespostpred1bin(     prob = 'posterior', design = 'external', theta0 = 0.20,     n_t = 12, n_c = 12, y_t = 8, y_c = 3,     a_t = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_c = 0.5,     m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL, z = NULL,     ne_t = 15L, ne_c = 15L, ye_t = 5L, ye_c = 4L,     alpha0e_t = 0.5, alpha0e_c = ae,     lower.tail = FALSE   ) }) data.frame(alpha0e_c = ae_seq, P_gt_TV = round(p_ae, 4)) #>    alpha0e_c P_gt_TV #> 1       0.01  0.6735 #> 2       0.10  0.6766 #> 3       0.20  0.6797 #> 4       0.30  0.6825 #> 5       0.40  0.6851 #> 6       0.50  0.6874 #> 7       0.60  0.6896 #> 8       0.70  0.6916 #> 9       0.80  0.6934 #> 10      0.90  0.6951 #> 11      1.00  0.6967

title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: given true response rates (πt,πc)(\pi_t, \pi_c), operating characteristics computed exact enumeration (nt+1)(nc+1)(n_t + 1)(n_c + 1) possible outcome pairs (yt,yc)∈{0,…,nt}×{0,…,nc}(y_t, y_c) \\{0,\ldots,n_t\} \times \{0,\ldots,n_c\}: Pr(Go)=∑yt=0nt∑yc=0nc𝟏[gGo(yt,yc)≥γgo gNoGo(yt,yc)<γnogo](ntyt)πtyt(1−πt)nt−yt(ncyc)πcyc(1−πc)nc−yc,\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) =   \sum_{y_t=0}^{n_t} \sum_{y_c=0}^{n_c}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[g_{\mathrm{Go}}(y_t, y_c) \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{go}}     \;\text{ }\; g_{\mathrm{NoGo}}(y_t, y_c) < \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\right]\,   \binom{n_t}{y_t} \pi_t^{y_t}(1-\pi_t)^{n_t-y_t}\,   \binom{n_c}{y_c} \pi_c^{y_c}(1-\pi_c)^{n_c-y_c}, gGo(yt,yc)=P(πt−πc>θTV∣yt,yc),g_{\mathrm{Go}}(y_t, y_c) = P(\pi_t - \pi_c > \theta_{\mathrm{TV}} \mid y_t, y_c),gNoGo(yt,yc)=P(πt−πc≤θMAV∣yt,yc),g_{\mathrm{NoGo}}(y_t, y_c) = P(\pi_t - \pi_c \le \theta_{\mathrm{MAV}} \mid y_t, y_c), decision outcomes classified : Go: gGo≥γgog_{\mathrm{Go}} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{go}} gNoGo<γnogog_{\mathrm{NoGo}} < \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}} NoGo: gGo<γgog_{\mathrm{Go}} < \gamma_{\mathrm{go}} gNoGo≥γnogog_{\mathrm{NoGo}} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}} Miss: gGo≥γgog_{\mathrm{Go}} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{go}} gNoGo≥γnogog_{\mathrm{NoGo}} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}} Gray: neither Go NoGo criterion met

title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: function supports design = 'uncontrolled' (argument z), design = 'external' (power prior arguments ne_t, ne_c, ye_t, ye_c, alpha0e_t, alpha0e_c), prob = 'predictive' (future sample size arguments m_t, m_c theta_NULL). function signature output format identical across combinations.

title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: getgamma1bin() function finds optimal pair (γgo*,γnogo*)(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}^*, \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}^*) grid search Γ={γ(1),…,γ(K)}⊂(0,1)\Gamma = \{\gamma^{(1)}, \ldots, \gamma^{(K)}\} \subset (0, 1) null scenario (πtnull,πcnull)(\pi_t^{\mathrm{null}}, \pi_c^{\mathrm{null}}). two-stage precompute--sweep strategy used: Stage 1 (Precompute): posterior/predictive probabilities gGo(yt,yc)g_{\mathrm{Go}}(y_t, y_c) gNoGo(yt,yc)g_{\mathrm{NoGo}}(y_t, y_c) computed (nt+1)(nc+1)(n_t + 1)(n_c + 1) outcome pairs — independently γ\gamma. Stage 2 (Sweep): candidate γ∈Γ\gamma \\Gamma, Pr(Go∣γ)\Pr(\mathrm{Go} \mid \gamma) Pr(NoGo∣γ)\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo} \mid \gamma) obtained weighted sums indicator functions: Pr(Go∣γ)=∑yt,yc𝟏[gGo(yt,yc)≥γ]w(yt,yc),\Pr(\mathrm{Go} \mid \gamma) = \sum_{y_t, y_c}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[g_{\mathrm{Go}}(y_t, y_c) \ge \gamma\right]\,   w(y_t, y_c), Pr(NoGo∣γ)=∑yt,yc𝟏[gNoGo(yt,yc)≥γ]w(yt,yc),\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo} \mid \gamma) = \sum_{y_t, y_c}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[g_{\mathrm{NoGo}}(y_t, y_c) \ge \gamma\right]\,   w(y_t, y_c), w(yt,yc)=Binom(yt;nt,πt)Binom(yc;nc,πc)w(y_t, y_c) = \mathrm{Binom}(y_t; n_t, \pi_t)\,\mathrm{Binom}(y_c; n_c, \pi_c) binomial weights null scenario. calls pbayespostpred1bin() required stage. sweep vectorised using outer(), making full grid search computationally negligible Stage 1 complete. optimal γgo*\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}^* smallest value Γ\Gamma Pr(Go∣γgo*)\Pr(\mathrm{Go} \mid \gamma_{\mathrm{go}}^*) satisfies criterion target (e.g., Pr(Go)<0.05\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) < 0.05). Analogously γnogo*\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}^*.

title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: function supports design = 'uncontrolled' (pi_t_go, pi_t_nogo, z), design = 'external' (power prior arguments), prob = 'predictive' (theta_NULL, m_t, m_c). calibration plot returned gamma_go/gamma_nogo values available combinations.
code: res_gamma <- getgamma1bin(   prob      = 'posterior',   design    = 'controlled',   theta_TV  = 0.30, theta_MAV = 0.15, theta_NULL = NULL,   pi_t_go   = 0.10, pi_c_go   = 0.10,   pi_t_nogo = 0.30, pi_c_nogo = 0.10,   target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,   n_t = 12L, n_c = 12L,   a_t = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_c = 0.5,   z = NULL, m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, ye_t = NULL, ye_c = NULL,   alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   gamma_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01) ) plot(res_gamma, base_size = 20)

title: Single Binary Endpoint
text: vignette covered single binary endpoint analysis BayesianQDM: Bayesian model: Beta-Binomial conjugate posterior explicit update formulae aj*=aj+yja_j^* = a_j + y_j bj*=bj+nj−yjb_j^* = b_j + n_j - y_j; Jeffreys prior (aj=bj=0.5a_j = b_j = 0.5) recommended weakly informative default. Posterior probability: evaluated via convolution integral pbetadiff() — closed-form CDF exists Beta difference. Posterior predictive probability: evaluated exact enumeration Beta-Binomial double sum pbetabinomdiff(). Three study designs: controlled (groups observed), uncontrolled (hypothetical control count zz fixed prior knowledge), external design (power prior borrowing weight α0ej\alpha_{0ej}, augmenting prior α0ej\alpha_{0ej} times external sufficient statistics). Operating characteristics: computed exact enumeration (nt+1)(nc+1)(n_t+1)(n_c+1) outcome pairs binomial probability weights — simulation required binary endpoint. Optimal threshold search: two-stage precompute--sweep strategy getgamma1bin() using outer() fully vectorised gamma sweep. See vignette("single-continuous") analogous continuous endpoint analysis.

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
text: use hypothetical Phase IIa proof--concept (PoC) trial moderate--severe rheumatoid arthritis (RA). investigational drug compared placebo 1:1 randomised controlled trial nt=nc=15n_t = n_c = 15 patients per group. Endpoint: change baseline DAS28 score (larger decrease indicates greater improvement). Clinically meaningful thresholds (posterior probability): Target Value (θTV\theta_{\mathrm{TV}}) = 1.5 (ambitious target) Minimum Acceptable Value (θMAV\theta_{\mathrm{MAV}}) = 0.5 (lower bar) Null hypothesis threshold (predictive probability): θNULL=1.0\theta_{\mathrm{NULL}} = 1.0. Decision thresholds: γgo=0.80\gamma_{\mathrm{go}} = 0.80 (Go), γnogo=0.20\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}} = 0.20 (NoGo). Observed data (used Sections 2–4): y‾t=3.2\bar{y}_t = 3.2, st=2.0s_t = 2.0 (treatment); y‾c=1.1\bar{y}_c = 1.1, sc=1.8s_c = 1.8 (control).

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
Single Continuous Endpoint
text: treatment effect θ=μt−μc\theta = \mu_t - \mu_c. Since two groups independent, posterior θ\theta distribution difference two independent non-standardised tt-variables: Tj∼tνnj(μnj,σnj†),j=t,c,T_j \;\sim\; t_{\nu_{nj}}(\mu_{nj},\, \sigma_{nj}^{\dagger}), \qquad   j = t, c, posterior scale σnj†=σnj/κnj\sigma_{nj}^{\dagger} = \sigma_{nj} / \sqrt{\kappa_{nj}}. posterior probability θ\theta exceeds threshold θ0\theta_0, P(θ>θ0∣𝐲)=P(Tt−Tc>θ0),P(\theta > \theta_0 \mid \mathbf{y}) =   P(T_t - T_c > \theta_0), computed one three methods described Section 3.

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
text: Let ỹ‾j\bar{\tilde{y}}_j denote future sample mean based mjm_j future patients. N-Inv-χ2\chi^2 posterior, predictive distribution ỹ‾j\bar{\tilde{y}}_j non-standardised tt-distribution: ỹ‾j∣𝐲j∼tνnj(μnj,σnj1+κnjκnjmj).\bar{\tilde{y}}_j \mid \mathbf{y}_j \;\sim\;   t_{\nu_{nj}}\!\!\left(\mu_{nj},\;   \sigma_{nj}\sqrt{\frac{1 + \kappa_{nj}}{\kappa_{nj}\, m_j}}\right). vague prior (κnj=nj\kappa_{nj} = n_j, σnj=sj\sigma_{nj} = s_j) becomes ỹ‾j∣𝐲j∼tnj−1(y‾j,sjnj+1njmj).\bar{\tilde{y}}_j \mid \mathbf{y}_j \;\sim\;   t_{n_j - 1}\!\!\left(\bar{y}_j,\;   s_j\sqrt{\frac{n_j + 1}{n_j\, m_j}}\right).

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
text: posterior predictive probability future treatment effect exceeds null threshold θNULL\theta_{\mathrm{NULL}} P(ỹ‾t−ỹ‾c>θNULL∣𝐲),P\!\left(\bar{\tilde{y}}_t - \bar{\tilde{y}}_c > \theta_{\mathrm{NULL}}   \mid \mathbf{y}\right), computed three methods posterior probability predictive scale replacing posterior scale.

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
Single Continuous Endpoint
text: nMCn_{\mathrm{MC}} independent samples drawn marginal: Tt()∼tνt(μt,σt),Tc()∼tνc(μc,σc),=1,…,nMC,T_t^{()} \;\sim\; t_{\nu_t}(\mu_t, \sigma_t), \quad   T_c^{()} \;\sim\; t_{\nu_c}(\mu_c, \sigma_c), \quad   = 1, \ldots, n_{\mathrm{MC}}, probability estimated P̂(D>θ0)=1nMC∑=1nMC𝟏[Tt()−Tc()>θ0].\widehat{P}(D > \theta_0) =   \frac{1}{n_{\mathrm{MC}}}   \sum_{=1}^{n_{\mathrm{MC}}}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[T_t^{()} - T_c^{()} > \theta_0\right]. called pbayesdecisionprob1cont() nsimn_{\mathrm{sim}} simulation replicates, draws generated single nMC×nsimn_{\mathrm{MC}} \times n_{\mathrm{sim}} matrix avoid loop overhead.

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
Single Continuous Endpoint
code:
# Observed data (nMC excluded from base list; passed individually per method) args_base <- list(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled', prior = 'vague',   theta0 = 1.0,   n_t = 15, n_c = 15,   bar_y_t = 3.2, s_t = 2.0,   bar_y_c = 1.1, s_c = 1.8,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   kappa0_t = NULL, kappa0_c = NULL, nu0_t = NULL, nu0_c = NULL,   mu0_t = NULL, mu0_c = NULL, sigma0_t = NULL, sigma0_c = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL, se_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = NULL, se_c = NULL )  p_ni <- do.call(pbayespostpred1cont,                 c(args_base, list(CalcMethod = 'NI', nMC = NULL))) p_mm <- do.call(pbayespostpred1cont,                 c(args_base, list(CalcMethod = 'MM', nMC = NULL))) set.seed(42) p_mc <- do.call(pbayespostpred1cont,                 c(args_base, list(CalcMethod = 'MC', nMC = 10000L)))  cat(sprintf("NI : %.6f  (exact)
",      p_ni)) #> NI : 0.069397  (exact) cat(sprintf("MM : %.6f  (approx)
",     p_mm)) #> MM : 0.069397  (approx) cat(sprintf("MC : %.6f  (n_MC=10000)
", p_mc)) #> MC : 0.073400  (n_MC=10000)

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
text: Standard parallel-group RCT observed data treatment (ntn_t, y‾t\bar{y}_t, sts_t) control (ncn_c, y‾c\bar{y}_c, scs_c) groups. posteriors updated PoC data. Vague prior — posterior probability: N-Inv-χ2\chi^2 informative prior: Historical knowledge suggests treatment mean ∼3.0\sim 3.0 control mean ∼1.0\sim 1.0 prior pseudo-sample size κ0t=κ0c=5\kappa_{0t} = \kappa_{0c} = 5 (kappa0_t, kappa0_c) degrees freedom ν0t=ν0c=5\nu_{0t} = \nu_{0c} = 5 (nu0_t, nu0_c). Posterior predictive probability (future Phase III: mt=mc=60m_t = m_c = 60):
code:
# P(mu_t - mu_c > theta_TV | data) and P(mu_t - mu_c <= theta_MAV | data) p_tv <- pbayespostpred1cont(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled', prior = 'vague',   CalcMethod = 'NI', theta0 = 1.5, nMC = NULL,   n_t = 15, n_c = 15,   bar_y_t = 3.2, s_t = 2.0,   bar_y_c = 1.1, s_c = 1.8,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   kappa0_t = NULL, kappa0_c = NULL, nu0_t = NULL, nu0_c = NULL,   mu0_t = NULL, mu0_c = NULL, sigma0_t = NULL, sigma0_c = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL, se_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = NULL, se_c = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE )  p_mav <- pbayespostpred1cont(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled', prior = 'vague',   CalcMethod = 'NI', theta0 = 0.5, nMC = NULL,   n_t = 15, n_c = 15,   bar_y_t = 3.2, s_t = 2.0,   bar_y_c = 1.1, s_c = 1.8,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   kappa0_t = NULL, kappa0_c = NULL, nu0_t = NULL, nu0_c = NULL,   mu0_t = NULL, mu0_c = NULL, sigma0_t = NULL, sigma0_c = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL, se_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = NULL, se_c = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE )  cat(sprintf("P(theta > theta_TV  | data) = %.4f  -> Go  criterion: >= 0.80? %s
",             p_tv, ifelse(p_tv >= 0.80, "YES", "NO"))) #> P(theta > theta_TV  | data) = 0.7940  -> Go  criterion: >= 0.80? NO cat(sprintf("P(theta <= theta_MAV | data) = %.4f  -> NoGo criterion: >= 0.20? %s
",             1 - p_mav, ifelse((1 - p_mav) >= 0.20, "YES", "NO"))) #> P(theta <= theta_MAV | data) = 0.0178  -> NoGo criterion: >= 0.20? NO cat(sprintf("Decision: %s
",             ifelse(p_tv >= 0.80 & (1 - p_mav) < 0.20, "Go",                    ifelse(p_tv < 0.80 & (1 - p_mav) >= 0.20, "NoGo",                           ifelse(p_tv >= 0.80 & (1 - p_mav) >= 0.20, "Miss", "Gray"))))) #> Decision: Gray p_tv_inf <- pbayespostpred1cont(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled', prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq',   CalcMethod = 'NI', theta0 = 1.5, nMC = NULL,   n_t = 15, n_c = 15,   bar_y_t = 3.2, s_t = 2.0,   bar_y_c = 1.1, s_c = 1.8,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   kappa0_t = 5, kappa0_c = 5,   nu0_t    = 5, nu0_c    = 5,   mu0_t    = 3.0, mu0_c  = 1.0,   sigma0_t = 2.0, sigma0_c = 1.8,   r = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL, se_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = NULL, se_c = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE ) cat(sprintf("P(theta > theta_TV | data, N-Inv-Chisq prior) = %.4f
", p_tv_inf)) #> P(theta > theta_TV | data, N-Inv-Chisq prior) = 0.8274 p_pred <- pbayespostpred1cont(   prob = 'predictive', design = 'controlled', prior = 'vague',   CalcMethod = 'NI', theta0 = 1.0, nMC = NULL,   n_t = 15, n_c = 15, m_t = 60, m_c = 60,   bar_y_t = 3.2, s_t = 2.0,   bar_y_c = 1.1, s_c = 1.8,   kappa0_t = NULL, kappa0_c = NULL, nu0_t = NULL, nu0_c = NULL,   mu0_t = NULL, mu0_c = NULL, sigma0_t = NULL, sigma0_c = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL, se_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = NULL, se_c = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE ) cat(sprintf("Predictive probability (m_t = m_c = 60) = %.4f
", p_pred)) #> Predictive probability (m_t = m_c = 60) = 0.9966

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
text: concurrent control group enrolled, control distribution specified external knowledge: Hypothetical control mean: μ0c\mu_{0c} (mu0_c, fixed scalar) Variance scaling: σc=r⋅σt\sigma_c = \sqrt{r} \cdot \sigma_t, r=1r = 1 assumes equal variances posterior control mean updated observed data; treatment posterior updated.
code: p_unctrl <- pbayespostpred1cont(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'uncontrolled', prior = 'vague',   CalcMethod = 'MM', theta0 = 1.5, nMC = NULL,   n_t = 15, n_c = NULL,   bar_y_t = 3.2, s_t = 2.0,   bar_y_c = NULL, s_c = NULL,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   kappa0_t = NULL, kappa0_c = NULL, nu0_t = NULL, nu0_c = NULL,   mu0_t = NULL, mu0_c = 1.0,   # hypothetical control mean   sigma0_t = NULL, sigma0_c = NULL,   r = 1.0,                     # equal variance assumption   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL, se_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = NULL, se_c = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE ) cat(sprintf("P(theta > theta_TV | data, uncontrolled) = %.4f\n", p_unctrl)) #> P(theta > theta_TV | data, uncontrolled) = 0.8184

text: external design, historical data prior study incorporated via power prior borrowing weight α0e∈(0,1]\alpha_{0e} \(0, 1]. two prior specifications yield different posterior update formulae.

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
text: group jj ne,jn_{e,j} external patients, external mean y‾e,j\bar{y}_{e,j}, external SD se,js_{e,j}, posterior parameters combining power prior PoC data given Theorem 4 Huang et al. (2025): μnj*=α0e,jne,jy‾e,j+njy‾jα0e,jne,j+nj,\mu_{nj}^* = \frac{\alpha_{0e,j}\, n_{e,j}\, \bar{y}_{e,j} + n_j\, \bar{y}_j}   {\alpha_{0e,j}\, n_{e,j} + n_j}, κnj*=α0e,jne,j+nj,\kappa_{nj}^* = \alpha_{0e,j}\, n_{e,j} + n_j, νnj*=α0e,jne,j+nj−1,\nu_{nj}^* = \alpha_{0e,j}\, n_{e,j} + n_j - 1, (σnj*)2=α0e,j(ne,j−1)se,j2+(nj−1)sj2+α0e,jne,jnj(y‾e,j−y‾j)2α0e,jne,j+njα0e,jne,j+nj.(\sigma_{nj}^*)^2 = \frac{\alpha_{0e,j}(n_{e,j}-1)\, s_{e,j}^2 +   (n_j-1)\, s_j^2 +   \dfrac{\alpha_{0e,j}\, n_{e,j}\, n_j\, (\bar{y}_{e,j} - \bar{y}_j)^2}         {\alpha_{0e,j}\, n_{e,j} + n_j}}   {\alpha_{0e,j}\, n_{e,j} + n_j}. marginal posterior μj\mu_j μj∣𝐲j∼tνnj*(μnj*,σnj*κnj*).\mu_j \mid \mathbf{y}_j \;\sim\;   t_{\nu_{nj}^*}\!\!\left(\mu_{nj}^*,\;   \frac{\sigma_{nj}^*}{\sqrt{\kappa_{nj}^*}}\right). Setting α0e,j=1\alpha_{0e,j} = 1 corresponds full borrowing; α0e,j→0\alpha_{0e,j} \0 result recovers vague-prior posterior current data alone.

code: p_ext <- pbayespostpred1cont(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'external', prior = 'vague',   CalcMethod = 'MM', theta0 = 1.5, nMC = NULL,   n_t = 15, n_c = 15,   bar_y_t = 3.2, s_t = 2.0,   bar_y_c = 1.1, s_c = 1.8,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   kappa0_t = NULL, kappa0_c = NULL, nu0_t = NULL, nu0_c = NULL,   mu0_t = NULL, mu0_c = NULL, sigma0_t = NULL, sigma0_c = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = 20L, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = 0.5,   bar_ye_t = NULL, se_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = 0.9, se_c = 1.8,   lower.tail = FALSE ) cat(sprintf("P(theta > theta_TV | data, external control design, alpha=0.5) = %.4f\n",             p_ext)) #> P(theta > theta_TV | data, external control design, alpha=0.5) = 0.8517 # alpha0e_c must be in (0, 1]; start from 0.01 to avoid validation error alpha_seq <- c(0.01, seq(0.1, 1.0, by = 0.1)) p_alpha   <- sapply(alpha_seq, function(a) {   pbayespostpred1cont(     prob = 'posterior', design = 'external', prior = 'vague',     CalcMethod = 'MM', theta0 = 1.5, nMC = NULL,     n_t = 15, n_c = 15,     bar_y_t = 3.2, s_t = 2.0,     bar_y_c = 1.1, s_c = 1.8,     m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,     kappa0_t = NULL, kappa0_c = NULL, nu0_t = NULL, nu0_c = NULL,     mu0_t = NULL, mu0_c = NULL, sigma0_t = NULL, sigma0_c = NULL,     r = NULL,     ne_t = NULL, ne_c = 20L, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = a,     bar_ye_t = NULL, se_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = 0.9, se_c = 1.8,     lower.tail = FALSE   ) }) data.frame(alpha_ec = alpha_seq, P_gt_TV = round(p_alpha, 4)) #>    alpha_ec P_gt_TV #> 1      0.01  0.7994 #> 2      0.10  0.8133 #> 3      0.20  0.8259 #> 4      0.30  0.8361 #> 5      0.40  0.8446 #> 6      0.50  0.8517 #> 7      0.60  0.8577 #> 8      0.70  0.8629 #> 9      0.80  0.8674 #> 10     0.90  0.8713 #> 11     1.00  0.8748

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
text: given true parameters (μt,μc,σt,σc)(\mu_t, \mu_c, \sigma_t, \sigma_c), operating characteristics probabilities decision outcome Go/NoGo/Gray/Miss rule thresholds (γgo,γnogo)(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}, \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}). estimated Monte Carlo simulation nsimn_{\mathrm{sim}} replicates: Pr̂(Go)=1nsim∑=1nsim𝟏[gGo()≥γgo gNoGo()<γnogo],\widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{Go}) =   \frac{1}{n_{\mathrm{sim}}} \sum_{=1}^{n_{\mathrm{sim}}}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[g_{\mathrm{Go}}^{()} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{go}}     \;\text{ }\; g_{\mathrm{NoGo}}^{()} < \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\right], Pr̂(NoGo)=1nsim∑=1nsim𝟏[gGo()<γgo gNoGo()≥γnogo],\widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{NoGo}) =   \frac{1}{n_{\mathrm{sim}}} \sum_{=1}^{n_{\mathrm{sim}}}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[g_{\mathrm{Go}}^{()} < \gamma_{\mathrm{go}}     \;\text{ }\; g_{\mathrm{NoGo}}^{()} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\right], Pr̂(Gray)=1−Pr̂(Go)−Pr̂(NoGo)−Pr̂(Miss),\widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{Gray}) = 1 -   \widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{Go}) - \widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{NoGo}) -   \widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{Miss}), gGo()=P(θ>θTV∣𝐲()),gNoGo()=P(θ≤θMAV∣𝐲())g_{\mathrm{Go}}^{()} = P(\theta > \theta_{\mathrm{TV}} \mid \mathbf{y}^{()}), \qquad g_{\mathrm{NoGo}}^{()} = P(\theta \le \theta_{\mathrm{MAV}} \mid \mathbf{y}^{()}) evaluated ii-th simulated dataset 𝐲()\mathbf{y}^{()}. Miss (gGo≥γgog_{\mathrm{Go}} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{go}} gNoGo≥γnogog_{\mathrm{NoGo}} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}) indicates inconsistent threshold configuration triggers error default (error_if_Miss = TRUE).

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
text: function supports design = 'uncontrolled' (arguments mu0_c r), design = 'external' (power prior arguments ne_c, alpha0e_c, bar_ye_c, se_c), prob = 'predictive' (future sample size arguments m_t, m_c theta_NULL). function signature output format identical across combinations.
code: oc_ctrl <- pbayesdecisionprob1cont(   nsim       = 500L,   prob       = 'posterior',   design     = 'controlled',   prior      = 'vague',   CalcMethod = 'MM',   theta_TV   = 1.5,  theta_MAV = 0.5,  theta_NULL = NULL,   nMC        = NULL,   gamma_go   = 0.80, gamma_nogo = 0.20,   n_t = 15,   n_c = 15,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   kappa0_t = NULL, kappa0_c = NULL, nu0_t = NULL, nu0_c = NULL,   mu0_t = NULL, mu0_c = NULL, sigma0_t = NULL, sigma0_c = NULL,   mu_t    = seq(1.0, 4.0, by = 0.5),   mu_c    = 1.0,   sigma_t = 2.0,   sigma_c = 2.0,   r = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL, se_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = NULL, se_c = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE,   seed = 42L ) print(oc_ctrl) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Continuous Endpoint)  #> ----------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : posterior  #>   Design           : controlled  #>   Prior            : vague  #>   Calc method      : MM  #>   Simulations      : nsim = 500  #>   Threshold(s)     : TV = 1.5, MAV = 0.5  #>   Go  threshold    : gamma_go = 0.8  #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma_nogo = 0.2  #>   Sample size      : n_t = 15, n_c = 15  #>   True SD          : sigma_t = 2, sigma_c = 2  #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE  #>   Seed             : 42  #> ----------------------------------------------------------------  #>  mu_t mu_c    Go  Gray  NoGo #>   1.0    1 0.002 0.064 0.934 #>   1.5    1 0.014 0.168 0.818 #>   2.0    1 0.066 0.352 0.582 #>   2.5    1 0.182 0.502 0.316 #>   3.0    1 0.418 0.460 0.122 #>   3.5    1 0.684 0.288 0.028 #>   4.0    1 0.878 0.118 0.004 #> ---------------------------------------------------------------- plot(oc_ctrl, base_size = 20)

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
text: getgamma1cont() function finds optimal pair (γgo*,γnogo*)(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}^*, \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}^*) grid search Γ={γ(1),…,γ(K)}⊂(0,1)\Gamma = \{\gamma^{(1)}, \ldots, \gamma^{(K)}\} \subset (0, 1). two thresholds calibrated independently separate scenarios: γgo*\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}^*: selected Pr(Go∣γgo*)\Pr(\mathrm{Go} \mid \gamma_{\mathrm{go}}^*) satisfies criterion target value (e.g., Pr(Go)<0.05\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) < 0.05) Go-calibration scenario (mu_t_go, mu_c_go, sigma_t_go, sigma_c_go; typically null scenario μt=μc\mu_t = \mu_c). γnogo*\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}^*: selected Pr(NoGo∣γnogo*)\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo} \mid \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}^*) satisfies criterion (e.g., Pr(NoGo)<0.20\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}) < 0.20) NoGo-calibration scenario (mu_t_nogo, mu_c_nogo, sigma_t_nogo, sigma_c_nogo; typically alternative scenario). search uses two-stage strategy: Stage 1 (Simulate ): nsimn_{\mathrm{sim}} datasets generated true parameter distribution probabilities gGo()g_{\mathrm{Go}}^{()} gNoGo()g_{\mathrm{NoGo}}^{()} computed replicate — independently γ\gamma. Stage 2 (Sweep Γ\Gamma): candidate γ∈Γ\gamma \\Gamma, Pr(Go∣γ)\Pr(\mathrm{Go} \mid \gamma) Pr(NoGo∣γ)\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo} \mid \gamma) computed empirical proportions precomputed indicator values without probability evaluations.

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
text: function supports design = 'uncontrolled' (mu_t_go, mu_t_nogo, mu0_c, r; set mu_c_go mu_c_nogo NULL), design = 'external' (power prior arguments), prob = 'predictive' (theta_NULL, m_t, m_c). calibration plot returned gamma_go/gamma_nogo values available combinations.

title: Single Continuous Endpoint
text: vignette covered single continuous endpoint analysis BayesianQDM: Bayesian model: N-Inv-χ2\chi^2 conjugate posterior (vague informative priors) explicit update formulae κnj\kappa_{nj}, νnj\nu_{nj}, μnj\mu_{nj}, σnj2\sigma_{nj}^2. Posterior predictive distributions: marginal tt-distributions μj\mu_j ỹ‾j\bar{\tilde{y}}_j, explicit scale expressions. Three computation methods: NI (exact), MC (simulation), MM (moment-matching approximation; Yamaguchi et al., 2025, Theorem 1); MM requires νj>4\nu_j > 4 recommended large-scale simulation. Three study designs: controlled (groups observed), uncontrolled (μ0c\mu_{0c} rr fixed prior knowledge), external design (power prior borrowing weight α0e,j\alpha_{0e,j}; vague prior follows Theorem 4 Huang et al. (2025), N-Inv-χ2\chi^2 prior follows Theorems 1–2 Huang et al. (2025)). Operating characteristics: Monte Carlo estimation Pr(Go)\Pr(\mathrm{Go}), Pr(NoGo)\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}), Pr(Gray)\Pr(\mathrm{Gray}) across true parameter scenarios. Optimal threshold search: two-stage precompute--sweep strategy getgamma1cont() user-specified criteria selection rules. See vignette("single-binary") analogous binary endpoint analysis.

title: Two Binary Endpoints
text: extend single-endpoint RA trial bivariate binary design two co-primary binary endpoints: Endpoint 1: ACR20 response (1 = responder, 0 = non-responder) Endpoint 2: DAS28 remission (1 = achieved, 0 = achieved) trial enrolls nt=nc=7n_t = n_c = 7 patients per group 1:1 randomised controlled design. Clinically meaningful thresholds (posterior probability): Null hypothesis thresholds (predictive probability): θNULL1=0.15\theta_{\mathrm{NULL}1} = 0.15, θNULL2=0.15\theta_{\mathrm{NULL}2} = 0.15. Decision thresholds: γgo=0.80\gamma_{\mathrm{go}} = 0.80 (Go), γnogo=0.80\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}} = 0.80 (NoGo). Observed data (used Sections 2–4): treatment group counts four response patterns (Yi,1,Yi,2)∈{(0,0),(0,1),(1,0),(1,1)}(Y_{,1}, Y_{,2}) \\{(0,0),(0,1),(1,0),(1,1)\}: Marginal responder counts: treatment yt,1=5y_{t,1} = 5, yt,2=4y_{t,2} = 4; control yc,1=4y_{c,1} = 4, yc,2=3y_{c,2} = 3.

title: Two Binary Endpoints
text: two endpoints within patient ii (=1,…,nji = 1, \ldots, n_j) correlated, bivariate binary outcome (Yi,1,Yi,2)(Y_{,1}, Y_{,2}) modelled jointly four-cell probability vector 𝐩j=(pj,00,pj,01,pj,10,pj,11)⊤,𝐩j∈Δ3,\mathbf{p}_j = (p_{j,00},\, p_{j,01},\, p_{j,10},\, p_{j,11})^\top, \qquad \mathbf{p}_j \\Delta^3, j∈{t,c}j \\{t, c\} denotes treatment control group, Δ3\Delta^3 denotes 3-simplex (entries non-negative summing 1), subscripts refer values (Yi,1,Yi,2)(Y_{,1}, Y_{,2}). observed count vector 𝐱j=(xj,00,xj,01,xj,10,xj,11)⊤\mathbf{x}_j = (x_{j,00}, x_{j,01}, x_{j,10}, x_{j,11})^\top ∑lmxj,lm=nj\sum_{lm} x_{j,lm} = n_j follows 𝐱j∣𝐩j∼Multinomial(nj,𝐩j).\mathbf{x}_j \mid \mathbf{p}_j \;\sim\;   \mathrm{Multinomial}(n_j,\, \mathbf{p}_j). marginal response rates treatment effects obtained summing: πj1=pj,10+pj,11,πj2=pj,01+pj,11,\pi_{j1} = p_{j,10} + p_{j,11}, \qquad   \pi_{j2} = p_{j,01} + p_{j,11}, θ1=πt1−πc1,θ2=πt2−πc2.\theta_1 = \pi_{t1} - \pi_{c1}, \qquad   \theta_2 = \pi_{t2} - \pi_{c2}.

title: Two Binary Endpoints
text: conjugate prior 𝐩j\mathbf{p}_j Dirichlet distribution: 𝐩j∼Dir(αj,00,αj,01,αj,10,αj,11),\mathbf{p}_j \;\sim\; \mathrm{Dir}(\alpha_{j,00},\, \alpha_{j,01},\,   \alpha_{j,10},\, \alpha_{j,11}), hyperparameters αj,lm>0\alpha_{j,lm} > 0 (corresponding a_t_00, a_t_01, a_t_10, a_t_11 j=tj = t a_c_00, a_c_01, a_c_10, a_c_11 j=cj = c). symmetric choice αj,lm=0.25\alpha_{j,lm} = 0.25 lmlm natural extension Jeffreys prior four-cell multinomial used throughout vignette.

title: Two Binary Endpoints
text: conjugacy Dirichlet-Multinomial model, posterior : 𝐩j∣𝐱j∼Dir(αj,00*,αj,01*,αj,10*,αj,11*),\mathbf{p}_j \mid \mathbf{x}_j \;\sim\;   \mathrm{Dir}(\alpha_{j,00}^*,\, \alpha_{j,01}^*,\,   \alpha_{j,10}^*,\, \alpha_{j,11}^*), posterior parameters αj,lm*=αj,lm+xj,lm,lm∈{00,01,10,11}.\alpha_{j,lm}^* = \alpha_{j,lm} + x_{j,lm}, \qquad   lm \\{00, 01, 10, 11\}. posterior mean cell E[pj,lm∣𝐱j]=αj,lm*/Aj*E[p_{j,lm} \mid \mathbf{x}_j] = \alpha_{j,lm}^* / A_j^*, Aj*=∑lmαj,lm*A_j^* = \sum_{lm} \alpha_{j,lm}^*.

title: Two Binary Endpoints
text: within-group Pearson correlation Endpoint 1 Endpoint 2 ρj=pj,11−πj1πj2πj1(1−πj1)πj2(1−πj2).\rho_j = \frac{p_{j,11} - \pi_{j1}\,\pi_{j2}}                {\sqrt{\pi_{j1}(1-\pi_{j1})\,\pi_{j2}(1-\pi_{j2})}}. specifying operating characteristic scenarios, true parameter vector 𝐩j\mathbf{p}_j specified via reparameterisation (πj1,πj2,ρj)(\pi_{j1}, \pi_{j2}, \rho_j), feasibility constraint: ρmin=max(0,πj1+πj2−1)−πj1πj2πj1(1−πj1)πj2(1−πj2)≤ρj≤min(πj1,πj2)−πj1πj2πj1(1−πj1)πj2(1−πj2)=ρmax.\rho_{\min} = \frac{\max(0,\pi_{j1}+\pi_{j2}-1) - \pi_{j1}\pi_{j2}}                      {\sqrt{\pi_{j1}(1-\pi_{j1})\pi_{j2}(1-\pi_{j2})}} \;\le\; \rho_j \;\le\; \frac{\min(\pi_{j1},\pi_{j2}) - \pi_{j1}\pi_{j2}}      {\sqrt{\pi_{j1}(1-\pi_{j1})\pi_{j2}(1-\pi_{j2})}} = \rho_{\max}. helper function getjointbin() converts (πj1,πj2,ρj)(\pi_{j1}, \pi_{j2}, \rho_j) (pj,00,pj,01,pj,10,pj,11)(p_{j,00}, p_{j,01}, p_{j,10}, p_{j,11}):
code: # Convert marginal rates + correlation to cell probabilities getjointbin(pi1 = 0.30, pi2 = 0.35, rho = 0.20) #>      p00      p01      p10      p11  #> 0.498715 0.201285 0.151285 0.148715 getjointbin(pi1 = 0.20, pi2 = 0.20, rho = 0.00)   # independence #>  p00  p01  p10  p11  #> 0.64 0.16 0.16 0.04

title: Two Binary Endpoints
text: Dirichlet-Multinomial yield tractable closed form joint probability (θ̃1,θ̃2)(\tilde\theta_1, \tilde\theta_2) falls given region, region probabilities estimated Monte Carlo: P̂(Rr)=1nMC∑s=1nMC𝟏[(πt1(s)−πc1(s),πt2(s)−πc2(s))∈Rr],\widehat{P}(R_r) = \frac{1}{n_\mathrm{MC}} \sum_{s=1}^{n_\mathrm{MC}}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[(\pi_{t1}^{(s)} - \pi_{c1}^{(s)},\;   \pi_{t2}^{(s)} - \pi_{c2}^{(s)}) \R_r\right], 𝐩j(s)∼Dir(𝛂j*)\mathbf{p}_j^{(s)} \sim \mathrm{Dir}(\boldsymbol{\alpha}_j^*) draws Dirichlet posterior πj1(s)=pj,10(s)+pj,11(s)\pi_{j1}^{(s)} = p_{j,10}^{(s)} + p_{j,11}^{(s)}, πj2(s)=pj,01(s)+pj,11(s)\pi_{j2}^{(s)} = p_{j,01}^{(s)} + p_{j,11}^{(s)}. predictive probability, future proportion differences θ̃k(s)=π̃t,k(s)−π̃c,k(s)\tilde\theta_k^{(s)} = \tilde{\pi}_{t,k}^{(s)} - \tilde{\pi}_{c,k}^{(s)} computed corresponding Multinomial draws conditioned Dirichlet samples.

title: Two Binary Endpoints
text: concurrent control group enrolled, control distribution specified via hypothetical count vector 𝐳=(z00,z01,z10,z11)\mathbf{z} = (z_{00}, z_{01}, z_{10}, z_{11}) combined Dirichlet prior: 𝐩c∼Dir(αc,00+z00,αc,01+z01,αc,10+z10,αc,11+z11).\mathbf{p}_c \;\sim\;   \mathrm{Dir}(\alpha_{c,00} + z_{00},\; \alpha_{c,01} + z_{01},\;   \alpha_{c,10} + z_{10},\; \alpha_{c,11} + z_{11}). specification encodes prior belief bivariate control response rates — including assumed within-group correlation — without requiring observed control data. hypothetical counts supplied z00, z01, z10, z11.
code: set.seed(1) p_unctrl <- pbayespostpred2bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'uncontrolled',   theta_TV1 = 0.20, theta_MAV1 = 0.10,   theta_TV2 = 0.20, theta_MAV2 = 0.10,   theta_NULL1 = NULL, theta_NULL2 = NULL,   x_t_00 = 1L, x_t_01 = 1L, x_t_10 = 2L, x_t_11 = 3L,   x_c_00 = NULL, x_c_01 = NULL, x_c_10 = NULL, x_c_11 = NULL,   a_t_00 = 0.25, a_t_01 = 0.25, a_t_10 = 0.25, a_t_11 = 0.25,   a_c_00 = 0.25, a_c_01 = 0.25, a_c_10 = 0.25, a_c_11 = 0.25,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   z00 = 2L, z01 = 1L, z10 = 2L, z11 = 1L,   xe_t_00 = NULL, xe_t_01 = NULL, xe_t_10 = NULL, xe_t_11 = NULL,   xe_c_00 = NULL, xe_c_01 = NULL, xe_c_10 = NULL, xe_c_11 = NULL,   alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   nMC = 1000L ) print(round(p_unctrl, 4)) #>    R1    R2    R3    R4    R5    R6    R7    R8    R9  #> 0.262 0.075 0.142 0.083 0.026 0.058 0.193 0.056 0.105

title: Two Binary Endpoints
text: External data group jj (count vector 𝐱ej\mathbf{x}_{ej}, supplied xe_t_00, …, xe_t_11 j=tj = t xe_c_00, …, xe_c_11 j=cj = c) incorporated via Dirichlet power prior weight α0e,j∈(0,1]\alpha_{0e,j} \(0, 1] (alpha0e_t, alpha0e_c). augmented prior parameters : αj,lm*=αj,lm+α0e,j⋅xej,lm.\alpha_{j,lm}^{*} = \alpha_{j,lm} + \alpha_{0e,j} \cdot x_{ej,lm}. Setting α0e,j=1\alpha_{0e,j} = 1 treats external data equivalent current trial data; α0e,j→0\alpha_{0e,j} \0 recovers original prior. current PoC data update augmented parameters: αj,lm**=αj,lm*+xj,lm=αj,lm+α0e,jxej,lm+xj,lm.\alpha_{j,lm}^{**} = \alpha_{j,lm}^{*} + x_{j,lm}   = \alpha_{j,lm} + \alpha_{0e,j}\, x_{ej,lm} + x_{j,lm}. control group uses external data (external control design), xe_t_00 xe_t_11 alpha0e_t set NULL. Sensitivity borrowing weight α0e,c\alpha_{0e,c}:
code: set.seed(2) p_ext <- pbayespostpred2bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'external',   theta_TV1 = 0.20, theta_MAV1 = 0.10,   theta_TV2 = 0.20, theta_MAV2 = 0.10,   theta_NULL1 = NULL, theta_NULL2 = NULL,   x_t_00 = 1L, x_t_01 = 1L, x_t_10 = 2L, x_t_11 = 3L,   x_c_00 = 2L, x_c_01 = 1L, x_c_10 = 2L, x_c_11 = 2L,   a_t_00 = 0.25, a_t_01 = 0.25, a_t_10 = 0.25, a_t_11 = 0.25,   a_c_00 = 0.25, a_c_01 = 0.25, a_c_10 = 0.25, a_c_11 = 0.25,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   z00 = NULL, z01 = NULL, z10 = NULL, z11 = NULL,   xe_t_00 = NULL, xe_t_01 = NULL, xe_t_10 = NULL, xe_t_11 = NULL,   xe_c_00 = 3L,   xe_c_01 = 1L,   xe_c_10 = 2L,   xe_c_11 = 1L,   alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = 0.5,   nMC = 1000L ) print(round(p_ext, 4)) #>    R1    R2    R3    R4    R5    R6    R7    R8    R9  #> 0.215 0.072 0.151 0.087 0.036 0.078 0.151 0.075 0.135 ae_seq <- c(0.01, seq(0.1, 1.0, by = 0.1)) p_ae <- sapply(ae_seq, function(ae) {   set.seed(99)   res <- pbayespostpred2bin(     prob = 'posterior', design = 'external',     theta_TV1 = 0.20, theta_MAV1 = 0.10,     theta_TV2 = 0.20, theta_MAV2 = 0.10,     theta_NULL1 = NULL, theta_NULL2 = NULL,     x_t_00 = 1L, x_t_01 = 1L, x_t_10 = 2L, x_t_11 = 3L,     x_c_00 = 2L, x_c_01 = 1L, x_c_10 = 2L, x_c_11 = 2L,     a_t_00 = 0.25, a_t_01 = 0.25, a_t_10 = 0.25, a_t_11 = 0.25,     a_c_00 = 0.25, a_c_01 = 0.25, a_c_10 = 0.25, a_c_11 = 0.25,     m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,     z00 = NULL, z01 = NULL, z10 = NULL, z11 = NULL,     xe_t_00 = NULL, xe_t_01 = NULL, xe_t_10 = NULL, xe_t_11 = NULL,     xe_c_00 = 3L,   xe_c_01 = 1L,   xe_c_10 = 2L,   xe_c_11 = 1L,     alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = ae, nMC = 500L   )   res["R1"] }) data.frame(alpha0e_c = ae_seq, P_R1 = round(p_ae, 4)) #>    alpha0e_c  P_R1 #> 1       0.01 0.142 #> 2       0.10 0.164 #> 3       0.20 0.184 #> 4       0.30 0.180 #> 5       0.40 0.196 #> 6       0.50 0.208 #> 7       0.60 0.220 #> 8       0.70 0.246 #> 9       0.80 0.230 #> 10      0.90 0.228 #> 11      1.00 0.238

title: Two Binary Endpoints
text: given true parameters (𝐩t,𝐩c)(\mathbf{p}_t, \mathbf{p}_c), operating characteristics computed exact enumeration possible multinomial count combinations via two-stage strategy. Stage 1 (precomputation): count combinations 𝐱t,\mathbf{x}_{t,} (KtK_t combinations) 𝐱c,j\mathbf{x}_{c,j} (KcK_c combinations) enumerated allmultinom(). pair (,j)(, j), pbayespostpred2bin() computes region probability vector , yielding ĝgo,ij\hat{g}_{\mathrm{go},ij} ĝnogo,ij\hat{g}_{\mathrm{nogo},ij} independent decision threshold. Stage 2 (weighting): given thresholds (γgo,γnogo)(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}, \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}): Pr(Go)=∑=1Kt∑j=1Kcwij𝟏[ĝgo,ij≥γgo ĝnogo,ij<γnogo],\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) = \sum_{=1}^{K_t} \sum_{j=1}^{K_c}   w_{ij}\, \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{\mathrm{go},ij} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{go}}   \text{ } \hat{g}_{\mathrm{nogo},ij} < \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\right], Pr(NoGo)=∑=1Kt∑j=1Kcwij𝟏[ĝnogo,ij≥γnogo ĝgo,ij<γgo],\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}) = \sum_{=1}^{K_t} \sum_{j=1}^{K_c}   w_{ij}\, \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{\mathrm{nogo},ij} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}   \text{ } \hat{g}_{\mathrm{go},ij} < \gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\right], wij=PMult(𝐱t,;nt,𝐩t)×PMult(𝐱c,j;nc,𝐩c)w_{ij} = P_\mathrm{Mult}(\mathbf{x}_{t,};\, n_t, \mathbf{p}_t) \times P_\mathrm{Mult}(\mathbf{x}_{c,j};\, n_c, \mathbf{p}_c), ĝgo,ij=∑r∈GoRegionsP̂(Rr∣𝐱t,,𝐱c,j),\hat{g}_{\mathrm{go},ij}   = \sum_{r \\text{GoRegions}}   \hat{P}(R_r \mid \mathbf{x}_{t,}, \mathbf{x}_{c,j}),ĝnogo,ij=∑r∈NoGoRegionsP̂(Rr∣𝐱t,,𝐱c,j).\hat{g}_{\mathrm{nogo},ij} = \sum_{r \\text{NoGoRegions}} \hat{P}(R_r \mid \mathbf{x}_{t,}, \mathbf{x}_{c,j}). Miss (ĝgo,ij≥γgo\hat{g}_{\mathrm{go},ij} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{go}} ĝnogo,ij≥γnogo\hat{g}_{\mathrm{nogo},ij} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}) indicates inconsistent threshold configuration triggers error default (error_if_Miss = TRUE). Gray comprises remaining outcomes. large ntn_t ncn_c, CalcMethod = 'MC' option replaces full enumeration nsimn_\mathrm{sim} multinomial draws, deduplicates K≪nsimK \ll n_\mathrm{sim} unique pairs, evaluates pbayespostpred2bin() unique pairs. Note computation time. number multinomial count combinations grows rapidly nn: njn_j patients 4 response categories number combinations (nj+33)\binom{n_j + 3}{3}, equals 286 nj=10n_j = 10 1771 nj=20n_j = 20. Exact method two groups means 2862≈82,000286^2 \approx 82{,}000 unique pairs requiring nMC Dirichlet draws. predictive probability used (mj>0m_j > 0), additional layer Multinomial sampling added inside Dirichlet draw, multiplying computation time. Use small nMC (100–500) n_t = n_c = 10 demonstration; switch CalcMethod = 'MC' moderate nsim larger sample sizes.

title: Two Binary Endpoints
text: function supports design = 'uncontrolled' (hypothetical count vector arguments z00, z01, z10, z11), design = 'external' (power prior arguments xe_c_00, …, xe_c_11 alpha0e_c), prob = 'predictive' (future sample size arguments m_t, m_c theta_NULL1, theta_NULL2). within-group correlation rho_t can also varied study effect operating characteristics. function signature output format identical across combinations.

title: Two Binary Endpoints
text: getgamma2bin() finds optimal pair (γgo*,γnogo*)(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}^*, \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}^*) two-stage precompute--sweep strategy pbayesdecisionprob2bin(), sweeps two-dimensional grid Γgo×Γnogo\Gamma_{\mathrm{go}} \times \Gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}} (gamma_go_grid ×\timesgamma_nogo_grid). two thresholds calibrated independently separate scenarios: γgo*\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}^*: calibrated Go-calibration scenario (pi_t1_go, pi_t2_go, rho_t_go, pi_c1_go, pi_c2_go, rho_c_go; typically null scenario πt=πc\pi_t = \pi_c), Pr(Go)<𝚝𝚊𝚛𝚐𝚎𝚝_𝚐𝚘\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) < \texttt{target_go}. γnogo*\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}^*: calibrated NoGo-calibration scenario (pi_t1_nogo, pi_t2_nogo, rho_t_nogo, pi_c1_nogo, pi_c2_nogo, rho_c_nogo; typically alternative scenario), Pr(NoGo)<𝚝𝚊𝚛𝚐𝚎𝚝_𝚗𝚘𝚐𝚘\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}) < \texttt{target_nogo}. Stage 1 (precomputation): pbayespostpred2bin() called every unique multinomial outcome combination (𝐱t,,𝐱c,j)(\mathbf{x}_{t,}, \mathbf{x}_{c,j}) enumerated allmultinom(). region probability vector summed GoRegions NoGoRegions obtain ĝgo,ij\hat{g}_{\mathrm{go},ij} ĝnogo,ij\hat{g}_{\mathrm{nogo},ij}, independent (γgo,γnogo)(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}, \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}). Stage 2 (gamma sweep): every pair (γgo,γnogo)∈Γgo×Γnogo(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}, \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}) \\Gamma_{\mathrm{go}} \times \Gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}: Pr(Go∣γgo,γnogo)=∑,jwij𝟏[ĝgo,ij≥γgo ĝnogo,ij<γnogo],\Pr(\mathrm{Go} \mid \gamma_{\mathrm{go}}, \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}) =   \sum_{,j} w_{ij}\,   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{\mathrm{go},ij} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{go}} \text{ }   \hat{g}_{\mathrm{nogo},ij} < \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\right], Pr(NoGo∣γgo,γnogo)=∑,jwij𝟏[ĝnogo,ij≥γnogo ĝgo,ij<γgo].\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo} \mid \gamma_{\mathrm{go}}, \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}) =   \sum_{,j} w_{ij}\,   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{\mathrm{nogo},ij} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}} \text{ }   \hat{g}_{\mathrm{go},ij} < \gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\right]. Results stored |Γgo|×|Γnogo||\Gamma_{\mathrm{go}}| \times |\Gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}| matrices PrGo_grid PrNoGo_grid. Stage 3 (optimal selection): candidate γgo\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}, worst-case Pr(Go)\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) γnogo\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}} compared target_go; analogously γnogo\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}.

title: Two Binary Endpoints
text: Null scenario: πt1=πc1=0.20\pi_{t1} = \pi_{c1} = 0.20, πt2=πc2=0.20\pi_{t2} = \pi_{c2} = 0.20, ρt=ρc=0\rho_t = \rho_c = 0 (treatment effect, independence).  function supports design = 'uncontrolled' (pi_t1_go, pi_t2_go, rho_t_go, pi_t1_nogo, pi_t2_nogo, rho_t_nogo; set pi_c*_go pi_c*_nogo NULL), design = 'external' (power prior arguments), prob = 'predictive' (theta_NULL1, theta_NULL2, m_t, m_c). calibration plot returned gamma_go/gamma_nogo values available combinations.
code: res_gamma <- getgamma2bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled',   GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,   pi_t1_go = 0.20, pi_t2_go = 0.20, rho_t_go = 0.0,   pi_c1_go = 0.20, pi_c2_go = 0.20, rho_c_go = 0.0,   pi_t1_nogo = 0.40, pi_t2_nogo = 0.40, rho_t_nogo = 0.0,   pi_c1_nogo = 0.20, pi_c2_nogo = 0.20, rho_c_nogo = 0.0,   target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,   n_t = 7L, n_c = 7L,   a_t_00 = 0.25, a_t_01 = 0.25, a_t_10 = 0.25, a_t_11 = 0.25,   a_c_00 = 0.25, a_c_01 = 0.25, a_c_10 = 0.25, a_c_11 = 0.25,   theta_TV1  = 0.20, theta_MAV1 = 0.10,   theta_TV2  = 0.20, theta_MAV2 = 0.10,   theta_NULL1 = NULL, theta_NULL2 = NULL,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   z00 = NULL, z01 = NULL, z10 = NULL, z11 = NULL,   xe_t_00 = NULL, xe_t_01 = NULL, xe_t_10 = NULL, xe_t_11 = NULL,   xe_c_00 = NULL, xe_c_01 = NULL, xe_c_10 = NULL, xe_c_11 = NULL,   alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   nMC = 200L,   gamma_go_grid   = seq(0.05, 0.95, by = 0.05),   gamma_nogo_grid = seq(0.05, 0.95, by = 0.05) ) plot(res_gamma, base_size = 20)

title: Two Binary Endpoints
text: vignette covered two binary endpoint analysis BayesianQDM: Bayesian model: Dirichlet-Multinomial conjugate, modelling bivariate binary outcome four-cell probability vector 𝐩j\mathbf{p}_j (j∈{t,c}j \\{t, c\}). Posterior update: αj,lm*=αj,lm+xj,lm\alpha_{j,lm}^* = \alpha_{j,lm} + x_{j,lm}. symmetric Jeffreys-type prior αj,lm=0.25\alpha_{j,lm} = 0.25 recommended. Within-group correlation: parameterised via ρj\rho_j using moment-matching identity; feasible range enforced getjointbin(). Nine-region grid posterior probability four-region grid predictive probability, identical structure two continuous endpoint case. Monte Carlo evaluation region probabilities via Dirichlet draws; closed-form expression joint probability exists bivariate binary case. Three study designs: controlled design (standard posterior update), uncontrolled design (hypothetical count vector 𝐳\mathbf{z} fixes control Dirichlet distribution), external control design (Dirichlet power prior αj,lm*=αj,lm+α0e,jxej,lm\alpha_{j,lm}^* = \alpha_{j,lm} + \alpha_{0e,j}\, x_{ej,lm}). Exact operating characteristics: two-stage precomputation multinomial count combinations via allmultinom(), multinomial probability weighting (outer Monte Carlo needed small nn). Optimal threshold search: three-stage precompute--sweep getgamma2bin(), producing |Γgo|×|Γnogo||\Gamma_{\mathrm{go}}| \times |\Gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}| matrices PrGo_grid PrNoGo_grid, visualised contour plots. See vignette("overview") comparison across four endpoint types.

",   p_pred["R1"], ifelse(p_pred["R1"] >= 0.80, "YES", "NO") )) #>  #> Go  region (R1): P = 1.0000  >= gamma1 (0.80)? YES cat(sprintf(   "NoGo region (R4): P = %.4f  >= gamma2 (0.20)? %s

code: Se2_ext <- matrix(c(15.0, 2.5, 2.5, 7.5), 2, 2)  set.seed(2) p_ext <- pbayespostpred2cont(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'external', prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart',   theta_TV1  = 1.5, theta_MAV1 = 0.5,   theta_TV2  = 1.0, theta_MAV2 = 0.3,   theta_NULL1 = NULL, theta_NULL2 = NULL,   n_t = 20L, n_c = 20L,   ybar_t = c(3.5, 2.1), S_t = S_t,   ybar_c = c(1.8, 1.0), S_c = S_c,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   kappa0_t = 2.0, nu0_t = 5.0, mu0_t = c(2.0, 1.0), Lambda0_t = L0,   kappa0_c = 2.0, nu0_c = 5.0, mu0_c = c(0.0, 0.0), Lambda0_c = L0,   r = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = 10L, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = 0.5,   bar_ye_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = c(1.5, 0.8), se_t = NULL, se_c = Se2_ext,   nMC = 5000L ) print(round(p_ext, 4)) #>     R1     R2     R3     R4     R5     R6     R7     R8     R9  #> 0.5712 0.2078 0.0000 0.1166 0.1040 0.0004 0.0000 0.0000 0.0000

title: Two Continuous Endpoints
text: given true parameters (𝛍t,Σt,𝛍c,Σc)(\boldsymbol{\mu}_t, \Sigma_t, \boldsymbol{\mu}_c, \Sigma_c) (mu_t, Sigma_t, mu_c, Sigma_c), operating characteristics estimated Monte Carlo nsimn_\mathrm{sim} replicates (nsim). Let ĝGo,=P(GoRegions∣𝐘())\hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go},} = P(\text{GoRegions} \mid \mathbf{Y}^{()}) ĝNoGo,=P(NoGoRegions∣𝐘())\hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo},} = P(\text{NoGoRegions} \mid \mathbf{Y}^{()}) ii-th simulated dataset. : Pr̂(Go)=1nsim∑=1nsim𝟏[ĝGo,≥γ1andĝNoGo,<γ2],\widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{Go}) =   \frac{1}{n_\mathrm{sim}} \sum_{=1}^{n_\mathrm{sim}}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go},} \ge \gamma_1 \;\text{}\;   \hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo},} < \gamma_2\right], Pr̂(NoGo)=1nsim∑=1nsim𝟏[ĝNoGo,≥γ2andĝGo,<γ1],\widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{NoGo}) =   \frac{1}{n_\mathrm{sim}} \sum_{=1}^{n_\mathrm{sim}}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo},} \ge \gamma_2 \;\text{}\;   \hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go},} < \gamma_1\right], Pr̂(Miss)=1nsim∑=1nsim𝟏[ĝGo,≥γ1andĝNoGo,≥γ2],\widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{Miss}) =   \frac{1}{n_\mathrm{sim}} \sum_{=1}^{n_\mathrm{sim}}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go},} \ge \gamma_1 \;\text{}\;   \hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo},} \ge \gamma_2\right], Pr̂(Gray)=1−Pr̂(Go)−Pr̂(NoGo)−Pr̂(Miss).\widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{Gray}) = 1 -   \widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{Go}) - \widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{NoGo}) -   \widehat{\Pr}(\mathrm{Miss}). γ1\gamma_1 γ2\gamma_2 correspond arguments gamma_go gamma_nogo, respectively. Miss (ĝGo≥γ1\hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go}} \ge \gamma_1 ĝNoGo≥γ2\hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo}} \ge \gamma_2 simultaneously) indicates inconsistent threshold configuration triggers error default (error_if_Miss = TRUE).

title: Two Continuous Endpoints
text: Treatment effect scenarios defined two-dimensional grid (μt1,μt2)(\mu_{t1}, \mu_{t2}) fixing 𝛍c=(0,0)⊤\boldsymbol{\mu}_c = (0, 0)^\top. full factorial combination unique μt1\mu_{t1} μt2\mu_{t2} values triggers tile plot plot(), colour intensity encoding Pr(Go)\Pr(\mathrm{Go}):  function supports design = 'uncontrolled' (hypothetical control mean mu0_c scale factor r) design = 'external' (power prior arguments ne_c, bar_ye_c, se_c, alpha0e_c). argument prob = 'predictive' switches predictive probability (future sample sizes m_t, m_c null thresholds theta_NULL1, theta_NULL2). function signature output format identical across combinations.

title: Two Continuous Endpoints
text: two decision thresholds (γ1,γ2)(\gamma_1, \gamma_2), optimal search performed two-dimensional grid Γ1×Γ2\Gamma_1 \times \Gamma_2 (arguments gamma_go_grid gamma_nogo_grid). two thresholds calibrated independently separate scenarios: γ1*\gamma_1^*: calibrated Go-calibration scenario (mu_t_go, Sigma_t_go, mu_c_go, Sigma_c_go; typically null scenario 𝛍t=𝛍c\boldsymbol{\mu}_t = \boldsymbol{\mu}_c), Pr(Go)<𝚝𝚊𝚛𝚐𝚎𝚝_𝚐𝚘\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) < \texttt{target\_go}. γ2*\gamma_2^*: calibrated NoGo-calibration scenario (mu_t_nogo, Sigma_t_nogo, mu_c_nogo, Sigma_c_nogo; typically alternative scenario), Pr(NoGo)<𝚝𝚊𝚛𝚐𝚎𝚝_𝚗𝚘𝚐𝚘\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}) < \texttt{target\_nogo}. getgamma2cont() uses three-stage strategy: Stage 1 (Simulate precompute): nsimn_\mathrm{sim} bivariate datasets generated separately calibration scenario. replicate ii, pbayespostpred2cont() called vectorised mode return full region probability vector. marginal Go NoGo probabilities per replicate : ĝGo,=∑ℓ∈GoRegionsp̂iℓ,ĝNoGo,=∑ℓ∈NoGoRegionsp̂iℓ.\hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go},} = \sum_{\ell \\text{GoRegions}} \hat{p}_{\ell}, \qquad   \hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo},} = \sum_{\ell \\text{NoGoRegions}} \hat{p}_{\ell}. Stage 2 (Gamma sweep): candidate γ1∈Γ1\gamma_1 \\Gamma_1 (Go-calibration scenario) γ2∈Γ2\gamma_2 \\Gamma_2 (NoGo-calibration scenario): Pr(Go∣γ1)=1nsim∑=1nsim𝟏[ĝGo,≥γ1],\Pr(\mathrm{Go} \mid \gamma_1) =   \frac{1}{n_\mathrm{sim}} \sum_{=1}^{n_\mathrm{sim}}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{\mathrm{Go},} \ge \gamma_1\right], Pr(NoGo∣γ2)=1nsim∑=1nsim𝟏[ĝNoGo,≥γ2].\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo} \mid \gamma_2) =   \frac{1}{n_\mathrm{sim}} \sum_{=1}^{n_\mathrm{sim}}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{\mathrm{NoGo},} \ge \gamma_2\right]. results stored vectors PrGo_grid PrNoGo_grid indexed gamma_go_grid gamma_nogo_grid, respectively. Stage 3 (Optimal selection): γ1*\gamma_1^* smallest grid value satisfying Pr(Go∣γ1)<𝚝𝚊𝚛𝚐𝚎𝚝_𝚐𝚘\Pr(\mathrm{Go} \mid \gamma_1) < \texttt{target\_go}; γ2*\gamma_2^* smallest grid value satisfying Pr(NoGo∣γ2)<𝚝𝚊𝚛𝚐𝚎𝚝_𝚗𝚘𝚐𝚘\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo} \mid \gamma_2) < \texttt{target\_nogo}.

title: Two Continuous Endpoints
text: Go-calibration scenario: 𝛍t=(0.5,0.3)⊤\boldsymbol{\mu}_t = (0.5, 0.3)^\top, 𝛍c=(0,0)⊤\boldsymbol{\mu}_c = (0, 0)^\top (effect MAV boundary – Go yet warranted, Pr(Go)<𝚝𝚊𝚛𝚐𝚎𝚝_𝚐𝚘\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) < \texttt{target\_go} required). NoGo-calibration scenario: 𝛍t=(1.0,0.6)⊤\boldsymbol{\mu}_t = (1.0, 0.6)^\top, 𝛍c=(0,0)⊤\boldsymbol{\mu}_c = (0, 0)^\top (effect MAV TV – NoGo unlikely).  function supports design = 'uncontrolled' (mu0_c r) design = 'external' (power prior arguments ne_c, bar_ye_c, se_c, alpha0e_c). Setting prob = 'predictive' switches predictive probability calibration (theta_NULL1, theta_NULL2, m_t, m_c). function signature identical across combinations.
code: res_gamma <- getgamma2cont(   nsim = 500L, prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled',   prior = 'vague',   GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,   mu_t_go    = c(0.5, 0.3), Sigma_t_go    = Sigma,   mu_c_go    = c(0.0, 0.0), Sigma_c_go    = Sigma,   mu_t_nogo  = c(1.0, 0.6), Sigma_t_nogo  = Sigma,   mu_c_nogo  = c(0.0, 0.0), Sigma_c_nogo  = Sigma,   target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,   n_t = 20L, n_c = 20L,   theta_TV1  = 1.5, theta_MAV1 = 0.5,   theta_TV2  = 1.0, theta_MAV2 = 0.3,   theta_NULL1 = NULL, theta_NULL2 = NULL,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   kappa0_t = NULL, nu0_t = NULL, mu0_t = NULL, Lambda0_t = NULL,   kappa0_c = NULL, nu0_c = NULL, mu0_c = NULL, Lambda0_c = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = NULL, se_t = NULL, se_c = NULL,   nMC = 500L, CalcMethod = 'MC',   gamma_go_grid   = seq(0.05, 0.95, by = 0.05),   gamma_nogo_grid = seq(0.05, 0.95, by = 0.05),   seed = 42L ) plot(res_gamma, base_size = 20)

title: Two Continuous Endpoints
text: vignette covered two continuous endpoint analysis BayesianQDM: Bayesian model: NIW conjugate posterior explicit update formulae κnj\kappa_{nj}, νnj\nu_{nj}, 𝛍nj\boldsymbol{\mu}_{nj}, Λnj\Lambda_{nj}; bivariate non-standardised tt marginal posterior. Nine-region grid posterior probability four-region grid predictive probability; typical choice Go = R1, NoGo = R9 (posterior) R4 (predictive). Two computation methods: MC (vectorised pre-generated draws Cholesky reuse) MM (fourth-moment matching per component + mvtnorm::pmvt joint probability). Three study designs: controlled, uncontrolled (𝛍0c\boldsymbol{\mu}_{0c} rr fixed), external control (NIW power prior explicit augmentation formulae κ0caug\kappa_{0c}^{\mathrm{aug}}, ν0caug\nu_{0c}^{\mathrm{aug}}, 𝛍0caug\boldsymbol{\mu}_{0c}^{\mathrm{aug}}, Λ0caug\Lambda_{0c}^{\mathrm{aug}}). Operating characteristics: Monte Carlo estimation vectorised region probability computation. Optimal threshold search: three-stage strategy getgamma2cont() calibrating γ1*\gamma_1^* γ2*\gamma_2^* independently separate Go- NoGo-calibration scenarios, calibration curves visualised via plot() optimal thresholds marked target probability levels (Pr(Go)<0.05\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) < 0.05 null, Pr(NoGo)<0.20\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}) < 0.20 alternative). See vignette("two-binary") analogous two binary endpoint analysis.

title: BayesianQDM
text: BayesianQDM provides comprehensive framework Bayesian Quantitative Decision-Making clinical trials. package enables researchers compute posterior probabilities, posterior predictive probabilities, Go/NoGo/Gray decision probabilities single two-endpoint analyses binary continuous outcomes. package also provides functions find optimal Go/NoGo thresholds satisfy user-specified operating characteristic criteria.

title: BayesianQDM
text: Single Two Endpoints: Supports single binary, single continuous, two binary, two continuous endpoint analyses Multiple Study Designs: Controlled, uncontrolled (hypothetical control), external designs Flexible Prior Specification: Vague (Jeffreys) priors conjugate priors (Beta, Normal-Inverse-Chi-squared, Normal-Inverse-Wishart, Dirichlet) Power Priors: Exact conjugate representation incorporating historical external data without MCMC Multiple Calculation Methods: Numerical integration (NI), Monte Carlo simulation (MC), Moment-Matching approximation (MM) continuous endpoints Three-Zone Decision Framework: Go/NoGo/Gray probability calculation user-defined thresholds (TV MAV) Optimal Threshold Search: Grid-search functions find Go/NoGo thresholds satisfying target operating characteristics

title: BayesianQDM

title: BayesianQDM
text: single continuous endpoints, three methods available computing CDF difference two independent t-distributions: NI (Numerical Integration): Exact computation via adaptive quadrature (stats::integrate) MC (Monte Carlo): Simulation-based estimation; supports vectorised batch processing MM (Moment-Matching): Closed-form approximation matching first two even moments difference distribution single t-distribution; fully vectorised recommended large-scale simulation studies two continuous endpoints, MC MM methods available computing joint rectangular region probabilities bivariate t-distribution approximation.

title: BayesianQDM
text: getgamma family functions finds optimal Go threshold γgo\gamma_go NoGo threshold γnogo\gamma_nogo grid search, given user-specified target operating characteristics (e.g., Pr(Go) Pr(NoGo) specified true parameter scenarios). search follows two-stage precompute--sweep strategy computational efficiency.
