what: Enumerate All Multinomial Count Vectors for a Bivariate Binary Outcome — allmultinom
title: Enumerate All Multinomial Count Vectors for a Bivariate Binary Outcome — allmultinom
text: Generates complete set non-negative integer vectors \((x_{00}, x_{01}, x_{10}, x_{11})\) satisfying \(x_{00} + x_{01} + x_{10} + x_{11} = n_j\), \(n_j\) sample size group \(j \\{t, c\}\). row returned matrix corresponds one possible realisation aggregated bivariate binary counts. enumeration prerequisite exact operating characteristic assessment pbayesdecisionprob2bin.

title: Enumerate All Multinomial Count Vectors for a Bivariate Binary Outcome — allmultinom
code: allmultinom(n)

title: Enumerate All Multinomial Count Vectors for a Bivariate Binary Outcome — allmultinom
text: n single non-negative integer giving sample size group (\(n_j\)).

title: Enumerate All Multinomial Count Vectors for a Bivariate Binary Outcome — allmultinom
text: integer matrix \(\binom{n_j + 3}{3}\) rows 4 columns named x00, x01, x10, x11. row distinct non-negative integer solution \(x_{00} + x_{01} + x_{10} + x_{11} = n_j\). Rows ordered x00 (ascending), x10 (ascending), x01 (ascending).

title: Enumerate All Multinomial Count Vectors for a Bivariate Binary Outcome — allmultinom
text: number non-negative integer solutions \(x_{00} + x_{01} + x_{10} + x_{11} = n_j\) stars--bars count $$\binom{n_j + 3}{3} = \frac{(n_j + 1)(n_j + 2)(n_j + 3)}{6}.$$ example, \(n_j = 10\) yields 286 rows \(n_j = 20\) yields 1771 rows. matrix pre-allocated avoid repeated memory reallocation, making function efficient sample sizes typical rare-disease proof--concept studies. function internal computational building block used pbayesdecisionprob2bin enumerate sample space multinomial probabilities decision indicators summed computing exact operating characteristics.

title: Enumerate All Multinomial Count Vectors for a Bivariate Binary Outcome — allmultinom
code: # Example 1: n = 2 (smallest non-trivial case) allmultinom(2) #>       x00 x01 x10 x11 #>  [1,]   0   0   0   2 #>  [2,]   0   1   0   1 #>  [3,]   0   2   0   0 #>  [4,]   0   0   1   1 #>  [5,]   0   1   1   0 #>  [6,]   0   0   2   0 #>  [7,]   1   0   0   1 #>  [8,]   1   1   0   0 #>  [9,]   1   0   1   0 #> [10,]   2   0   0   0  # Example 2: n = 10 (typical rare-disease PoC group size) mat <- allmultinom(10) nrow(mat)              # Should be choose(13, 3) = 286 #> [1] 286 all(rowSums(mat) == 10)  # Every row must sum to n #> [1] TRUE  # Example 3: n = 0 (edge case - only the all-zero row) allmultinom(0) #>      x00 x01 x10 x11 #> [1,]   0   0   0   0  # Example 4: Verify column names colnames(allmultinom(5)) #> [1] "x00" "x01" "x10" "x11"  # Example 5: Row counts match the stars-and-bars formula n <- 15L mat <- allmultinom(n) nrow(mat) == choose(n + 3L, 3L)  # Should be TRUE #> [1] TRUE

what: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for a Single Binary Endpoint — getgamma1bin
title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for a Single Binary Endpoint — getgamma1bin
text: Computes optimal Go threshold \(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\) NoGo threshold \(\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\) single binary endpoint searching grid candidate values.  two thresholds calibrated independently separate scenarios: \(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\) smallest value gamma_grid marginal Go probability \(\Pr(g_{\mathrm{Go}} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{go}})\) strictly less target_go Go-calibration scenario (pi_t_go, pi_c_go); typically Null scenario. \(\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\) smallest value gamma_grid marginal NoGo probability \(\Pr(g_{\mathrm{NoGo}} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}})\) strictly less target_nogo NoGo-calibration scenario (pi_t_nogo, pi_c_nogo); typically Alternative scenario. \(g_{\mathrm{Go}} = P(\theta > \theta_{\mathrm{TV}} \mid y_t, y_c)\) \(g_{\mathrm{NoGo}} = P(\theta \le \theta_{\mathrm{MAV}} \mid y_t, y_c)\) prob = 'posterior', consistent decision rule pbayesdecisionprob1bin.

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for a Single Binary Endpoint — getgamma1bin
code: getgamma1bin(   prob = "posterior",   design = "controlled",   theta_TV = NULL,   theta_MAV = NULL,   theta_NULL = NULL,   pi_t_go,   pi_c_go = NULL,   pi_t_nogo,   pi_c_nogo = NULL,   target_go,   target_nogo,   n_t,   n_c,   a_t,   a_c,   b_t,   b_c,   z = NULL,   m_t = NULL,   m_c = NULL,   ne_t = NULL,   ne_c = NULL,   ye_t = NULL,   ye_c = NULL,   alpha0e_t = NULL,   alpha0e_c = NULL,   gamma_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01) )

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for a Single Binary Endpoint — getgamma1bin
text: prob character string specifying probability type. Must 'posterior' 'predictive'. design character string specifying trial design. Must 'controlled', 'uncontrolled', 'external'. theta_TV numeric scalar (-1, 1) giving Target Value (TV) threshold treatment effect.  Required prob = 'posterior'; set NULL otherwise. theta_MAV numeric scalar (-1, 1) giving Minimum Acceptable Value (MAV) threshold.  Must satisfy theta_TV > theta_MAV.  Required prob = 'posterior'; set NULL otherwise. theta_NULL numeric scalar (-1, 1) giving null hypothesis threshold used predictive probability.  Required prob = 'predictive'; set NULL otherwise. pi_t_go numeric scalar (0, 1) giving true treatment response rate Go-calibration scenario (typically Null). pi_c_go numeric scalar (0, 1) giving true control response rate Go-calibration scenario.  Set NULL design = 'uncontrolled'. pi_t_nogo numeric scalar (0, 1) giving true treatment response rate NoGo-calibration scenario (typically Alternative). pi_c_nogo numeric scalar (0, 1) giving true control response rate NoGo-calibration scenario.  Set NULL design = 'uncontrolled'. target_go numeric scalar (0, 1) giving upper bound marginal Go probability Go-calibration scenario. optimal \(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\) smallest grid value satisfying \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) < \code{target\_go}\). target_nogo numeric scalar (0, 1) giving upper bound marginal NoGo probability NoGo-calibration scenario. optimal \(\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\) largest grid value satisfying \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}) < \code{target\_nogo}\). n_t positive integer giving number patients treatment group PoC trial. n_c positive integer giving number patients control group PoC trial. a_t positive numeric scalar giving first shape parameter Beta prior treatment group. a_c positive numeric scalar giving first shape parameter Beta prior control group. b_t positive numeric scalar giving second shape parameter Beta prior treatment group. b_c positive numeric scalar giving second shape parameter Beta prior control group. z non-negative integer giving hypothetical number responders control group.  Required design = 'uncontrolled'; set NULL otherwise. m_t positive integer giving future sample size treatment group.  Required prob = 'predictive'; set NULL otherwise. m_c positive integer giving future sample size control group.  Required prob = 'predictive'; set NULL otherwise. ne_t positive integer giving number patients treatment group external data set.  Required design = 'external'; set NULL otherwise. ne_c positive integer giving number patients control group external data set.  Required design = 'external'; set NULL otherwise. ye_t non-negative integer giving number responders treatment group external data set.  Required design = 'external'; set NULL otherwise. ye_c non-negative integer giving number responders control group external data set.  Required design = 'external'; set NULL otherwise. alpha0e_t numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight treatment group.  Required design = 'external'; set NULL otherwise. alpha0e_c numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight control group.  Required design = 'external'; set NULL otherwise. gamma_grid numeric vector candidate threshold values (0, 1) search .  Defaults seq(0.01, 0.99, = 0.01).

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for a Single Binary Endpoint — getgamma1bin
text: list class getgamma1bin following elements: gamma_go Optimal Go threshold: smallest value gamma_grid \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) < \code{target\_go}\) Go-calibration scenario. NA value exists. gamma_nogo Optimal NoGo threshold: smallest value gamma_grid \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}) < \code{target\_nogo}\) NoGo-calibration scenario. NA value exists. PrGo_opt Marginal \(\Pr(g_{\mathrm{Go}} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{go}})\) optimal \(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\) Go-calibration scenario.  NA gamma_go NA. PrNoGo_opt Marginal \(\Pr(g_{\mathrm{NoGo}} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}})\) optimal \(\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\) NoGo-calibration scenario.  NA gamma_nogo NA. target_go value target_go supplied user. target_nogo value target_nogo supplied user. grid_results data frame columns gamma_grid, PrGo_grid (marginal Go probability Go-calibration scenario), PrNoGo_grid (marginal NoGo probability NoGo-calibration scenario).

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for a Single Binary Endpoint — getgamma1bin
text: function uses two-stage precompute--sweep strategy: Precomputation: possible outcome pairs \((y_t, y_c)\) enumerated.  pair, pbayespostpred1bin computes \(g_{\mathrm{Go}}\) (lower.tail = FALSE theta_TV) \(g_{\mathrm{NoGo}}\) (lower.tail         = TRUE theta_MAV).  step independent \(\gamma\). Gamma sweep: Marginal probabilities computed weighted sums binary indicators grid: \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) uses w_go (weights pi_t_go, pi_c_go) indicator \(g_{\mathrm{Go}} \ge \gamma\); \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo})\) uses w_nogo (weights pi_t_nogo, pi_c_nogo) indicator \(g_{\mathrm{NoGo}} \ge \gamma\). \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo})\) monotone non-increasing functions \(\gamma\).  optimal \(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\) smallest grid value crossing target_go.  optimal \(\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\) also smallest grid value crossing target_nogo: smaller \(\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\) makes NoGo harder trigger (permissive), least restrictive threshold still controls false NoGo rate.

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for a Single Binary Endpoint — getgamma1bin

what: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for a Single Continuous Endpoint — getgamma1cont
title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for a Single Continuous Endpoint — getgamma1cont
text: Computes optimal Go threshold \(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\) NoGo threshold \(\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\) single continuous endpoint searching grid candidate values.  two thresholds calibrated independently separate scenarios: \(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\) smallest value gamma_grid marginal Go probability \(\Pr(g_{\mathrm{Go}} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{go}})\) strictly less target_go Go-calibration scenario (mu_t_go, mu_c_go, sigma_t_go, sigma_c_go); typically Null scenario. \(\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\) smallest value gamma_grid marginal NoGo probability \(\Pr(g_{\mathrm{NoGo}} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}})\) strictly less target_nogo NoGo-calibration scenario (mu_t_nogo, mu_c_nogo, sigma_t_nogo, sigma_c_nogo); typically Alternative scenario. \(g_{\mathrm{Go}} = P(\theta > \theta_{\mathrm{TV}} \mid \bar{y}_t, s_t, \bar{y}_c, s_c)\) \(g_{\mathrm{NoGo}} = P(\theta \le \theta_{\mathrm{MAV}} \mid \bar{y}_t, s_t, \bar{y}_c, s_c)\) prob = 'posterior', consistent decision rule pbayesdecisionprob1cont.

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for a Single Continuous Endpoint — getgamma1cont
code: getgamma1cont(   nsim,   prob = "posterior",   design = "controlled",   prior = "vague",   CalcMethod = "NI",   theta_TV = NULL,   theta_MAV = NULL,   theta_NULL = NULL,   nMC = NULL,   mu_t_go,   mu_c_go = NULL,   sigma_t_go,   sigma_c_go = NULL,   mu_t_nogo,   mu_c_nogo = NULL,   sigma_t_nogo,   sigma_c_nogo = NULL,   target_go,   target_nogo,   n_t,   n_c = NULL,   m_t = NULL,   m_c = NULL,   kappa0_t = NULL,   kappa0_c = NULL,   nu0_t = NULL,   nu0_c = NULL,   mu0_t = NULL,   mu0_c = NULL,   sigma0_t = NULL,   sigma0_c = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne_t = NULL,   ne_c = NULL,   alpha0e_t = NULL,   alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL,   bar_ye_c = NULL,   se_t = NULL,   se_c = NULL,   gamma_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),   seed )

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for a Single Continuous Endpoint — getgamma1cont
text: nsim positive integer giving number Monte Carlo simulation replicates used approximate operating characteristics. prob character string specifying probability type. Must 'posterior' 'predictive'. design character string specifying trial design. Must 'controlled', 'uncontrolled', 'external'. prior character string specifying prior distribution. Must 'vague' 'N-Inv-Chisq'. CalcMethod character string specifying computation method pbayespostpred1cont. Must 'NI', 'MC', 'MM'. theta_TV numeric scalar giving Target Value (TV) threshold treatment effect.  Required prob = 'posterior'; set NULL otherwise. theta_MAV numeric scalar giving Minimum Acceptable Value (MAV) threshold.  Must satisfy theta_TV > theta_MAV.  Required prob = 'posterior'; set NULL otherwise. theta_NULL numeric scalar giving null hypothesis threshold predictive probability.  Required prob = 'predictive'; set NULL otherwise. nMC positive integer giving number inner Monte Carlo draws passed pbayespostpred1cont.  Required CalcMethod = 'MC'; set NULL otherwise. mu_t_go numeric scalar giving true mean treatment group Go-calibration scenario (typically Null). mu_c_go numeric scalar giving true mean control group Go-calibration scenario.  Set NULL design = 'uncontrolled'. sigma_t_go positive numeric scalar giving true standard deviation treatment group Go-calibration scenario. sigma_c_go positive numeric scalar giving true standard deviation control group Go-calibration scenario. Set NULL design = 'uncontrolled'. mu_t_nogo numeric scalar giving true mean treatment group NoGo-calibration scenario (typically Alternative). mu_c_nogo numeric scalar giving true mean control group NoGo-calibration scenario.  Set NULL design = 'uncontrolled'. sigma_t_nogo positive numeric scalar giving true standard deviation treatment group NoGo-calibration scenario. sigma_c_nogo positive numeric scalar giving true standard deviation control group NoGo-calibration scenario. Set NULL design = 'uncontrolled'. target_go numeric scalar (0, 1) giving upper bound marginal Go probability Go-calibration scenario. optimal \(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\) smallest grid value satisfying \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) < \code{target\_go}\). target_nogo numeric scalar (0, 1) giving upper bound marginal NoGo probability NoGo-calibration scenario. optimal \(\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\) smallest grid value satisfying \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}) < \code{target\_nogo}\). n_t positive integer giving number patients treatment group PoC trial. n_c positive integer giving number patients control group PoC trial.  Required design = 'controlled' 'external'; set NULL design = 'uncontrolled'. m_t positive integer giving future sample size treatment group. Required prob = 'predictive'; set NULL otherwise. m_c positive integer giving future sample size control group. Required prob = 'predictive'; set NULL otherwise. kappa0_t positive numeric scalar giving prior precision parameter treatment group N-Inv-Chi-squared prior. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq'; set NULL otherwise. kappa0_c positive numeric scalar giving prior precision parameter control group.  Required prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq' design != 'uncontrolled'; set NULL otherwise. nu0_t positive numeric scalar giving prior degrees freedom treatment group N-Inv-Chi-squared prior.  Required prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq'; set NULL otherwise. nu0_c positive numeric scalar giving prior degrees freedom control group.  Required prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq' design != 'uncontrolled'; set NULL otherwise. mu0_t numeric scalar giving prior mean treatment group N-Inv-Chi-squared prior.  Required prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq'; set NULL otherwise. mu0_c numeric scalar giving prior mean control group (fixed hypothetical control mean uncontrolled design). Required prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq'; set NULL otherwise. sigma0_t positive numeric scalar giving prior scale parameter treatment group N-Inv-Chi-squared prior.  Required prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq'; set NULL otherwise. sigma0_c positive numeric scalar giving prior scale parameter control group.  Required prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq' design != 'uncontrolled'; set NULL otherwise. r positive numeric scalar giving variance ratio \(\sigma_c^2 / \sigma_t^2\) used uncontrolled design. Required design = 'uncontrolled'; set NULL otherwise. ne_t positive integer giving number patients treatment group external data set.  Required design = 'external' external treatment data available; otherwise set NULL. ne_c positive integer giving number patients control group external data set.  Required design = 'external' external control data available; otherwise set NULL. alpha0e_t numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight external treatment group data.  Required design = 'external' ne_t non-NULL; set NULL otherwise. alpha0e_c numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight external control group data.  Required design = 'external' ne_c non-NULL; set NULL otherwise. bar_ye_t numeric scalar giving sample mean external treatment group data.  Required ne_t non-NULL; set NULL otherwise. bar_ye_c numeric scalar giving sample mean external control group data.  Required ne_c non-NULL; set NULL otherwise. se_t positive numeric scalar giving sample standard deviation external treatment group data.  Required ne_t non-NULL; set NULL otherwise. se_c positive numeric scalar giving sample standard deviation external control group data.  Required ne_c non-NULL; set NULL otherwise. gamma_grid numeric vector candidate threshold values (0, 1) search .  Defaults seq(0.01, 0.99, = 0.01). seed numeric scalar reproducible random number generation. Go-calibration simulation uses seed NoGo-calibration simulation uses seed + 1 ensure independence two scenarios.

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for a Single Continuous Endpoint — getgamma1cont
text: list class getgamma1cont following elements: gamma_go Optimal Go threshold: smallest value gamma_grid \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) < \code{target\_go}\) Go-calibration scenario. NA value exists. gamma_nogo Optimal NoGo threshold: smallest value gamma_grid \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}) < \code{target\_nogo}\) NoGo-calibration scenario. NA value exists. PrGo_opt Marginal \(\Pr(g_{\mathrm{Go}} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{go}})\) optimal \(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\) Go-calibration scenario.  NA gamma_go NA. PrNoGo_opt Marginal \(\Pr(g_{\mathrm{NoGo}} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}})\) optimal \(\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\) NoGo-calibration scenario.  NA gamma_nogo NA. target_go value target_go supplied user. target_nogo value target_nogo supplied user. grid_results data frame columns gamma_grid, PrGo_grid (marginal Go probability Go-calibration scenario), PrNoGo_grid (marginal NoGo probability NoGo-calibration scenario).

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for a Single Continuous Endpoint — getgamma1cont
text: function uses two-stage simulate--sweep strategy: Simulation: nsim datasets generated independently calibration scenario.  Go-calibration scenario, standardised residuals shifted mu_t_go mu_c_go; NoGo-calibration scenario, mu_t_nogo mu_c_nogo. pbayespostpred1cont called per scenario obtain \(g_{\mathrm{Go}}\) (lower.tail = FALSE theta_TV) \(g_{\mathrm{NoGo}}\) (lower.tail         = TRUE theta_MAV). Gamma sweep: Marginal probabilities estimated proportion simulated datasets satisfying respective indicator: \(g_{\mathrm{Go}} \ge \gamma\) PrGo_grid, \(g_{\mathrm{NoGo}} \ge \gamma\) PrNoGo_grid.  monotone non-increasing \(\gamma\). optimal \(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\) \(\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\) smallest grid value crossing respective target probability.

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for a Single Continuous Endpoint — getgamma1cont

what: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Binary Endpoints — getgamma2bin
title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Binary Endpoints — getgamma2bin
text: Computes optimal Go threshold \(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\) NoGo threshold \(\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\) two binary endpoints searching two-dimensional grid candidate value pairs.  two thresholds calibrated independently separate scenarios: \(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\) smallest value gamma_go_grid worst-case marginal Go probability \(\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\) gamma_nogo_grid strictly less target_go Go-calibration scenario (pi_t1_go, pi_t2_go, rho_t_go, pi_c1_go, pi_c2_go, rho_c_go); typically Null scenario. \(\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\) smallest value gamma_nogo_grid worst-case marginal NoGo probability \(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\) gamma_go_grid strictly less target_nogo NoGo-calibration scenario (pi_t1_nogo, pi_t2_nogo, rho_t_nogo, pi_c1_nogo, pi_c2_nogo, rho_c_nogo); typically Alternative scenario.

Required prob = 'posterior'; otherwise set NULL. theta_MAV1 numeric scalar giving MAV threshold Endpoint 1.  Required prob = 'posterior'; otherwise set NULL. theta_TV2 numeric scalar giving TV threshold Endpoint 2. Required design = 'uncontrolled'; otherwise set NULL. z01 non-negative integer; see z00. z10 non-negative integer; see z00. z11 non-negative integer; see z00. xe_t_00 non-negative integer giving external treatment group count pattern (0,0).  Required design = 'external' external treatment data used; otherwise NULL. xe_t_01 non-negative integer; see xe_t_00. xe_t_10 non-negative integer; see xe_t_00. xe_t_11 non-negative integer; see xe_t_00. xe_c_00 non-negative integer giving external control group count pattern (0,0).  Required design = 'external' external control data used; otherwise NULL. xe_c_01 non-negative integer; see xe_c_00. xe_c_10 non-negative integer; see xe_c_00. xe_c_11 non-negative integer; see xe_c_00. alpha0e_t numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight external treatment group data.  Required external treatment data used; otherwise NULL. alpha0e_c numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight external control group data. title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Binary Endpoints — getgamma2bin
text: list class getgamma2bin following elements: gamma_go Optimal Go threshold: smallest value gamma_go_grid marginal \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) < \code{target\_go}\) Go-calibration scenario.  NA value exists. gamma_nogo Optimal NoGo threshold: smallest value gamma_nogo_grid marginal \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}) < \code{target\_nogo}\) NoGo-calibration scenario.  NA value exists. PrGo_opt Marginal \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) gamma_go Go-calibration scenario. NA gamma_go NA. PrNoGo_opt Marginal \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo})\) gamma_nogo NoGo-calibration scenario. NA gamma_nogo NA. target_go value target_go supplied user. target_nogo value target_nogo supplied user. grid_results data frame columns gamma_grid, PrGo_grid (marginal Go probability Go-calibration scenario), PrNoGo_grid (marginal NoGo probability NoGo-calibration scenario).

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Binary Endpoints — getgamma2bin
text: function uses two-stage precompute--sweep strategy pbayesdecisionprob2bin. Stage 1 (precomputation): pbayespostpred2bin called every possible multinomial outcome combination \((x_t, x_c)\) enumerated allmultinom.  resulting region probability vector summed GoRegions NoGoRegions obtain \(\hat{g}_{Go,ij}\) \(\hat{g}_{NoGo,ij}\).  independent calibration scenario; multinomial weights differ. Stage 2 (gamma sweep): pair \((\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}, \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}})\) two-dimensional grid, operating characteristics computed separately calibration scenario using respective multinomial weights: $$\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) = \sum_{,j} w_{ij}^{(\mathrm{go})}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{Go,ij} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{go}},\;   \hat{g}_{NoGo,ij} < \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\right]$$ $$\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}) = \sum_{,j} w_{ij}^{(\mathrm{nogo})}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{NoGo,ij} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}},\;   \hat{g}_{Go,ij} < \gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\right]$$ Stage 3 (optimal threshold selection): candidate \(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\), worst-case \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) \(\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\) gamma_nogo_grid computed; optimal \(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\) smallest grid value worst-case probability less target_go. Analogously, optimal \(\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\) smallest grid value worst-case \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo})\) less target_nogo.

what: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Continuous Endpoints — getgamma2cont
title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Continuous Endpoints — getgamma2cont
text: Computes optimal Go threshold \(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\) NoGo threshold \(\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\) two continuous endpoints searching independently candidate threshold grids.  two thresholds calibrated marginally separate scenarios: \(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\) smallest value gamma_go_grid marginal Go probability strictly less target_go Go-calibration scenario (mu_t_go, Sigma_t_go, mu_c_go, Sigma_c_go); typically Null scenario. \(\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\) smallest value gamma_nogo_grid marginal NoGo probability strictly less target_nogo NoGo-calibration scenario (mu_t_nogo, Sigma_t_nogo, mu_c_nogo, Sigma_c_nogo); typically Alternative scenario.

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Continuous Endpoints — getgamma2cont
code: getgamma2cont(   nsim = 10000L,   prob = "posterior",   design = "controlled",   prior = "vague",   GoRegions,   NoGoRegions,   mu_t_go,   Sigma_t_go,   mu_c_go = NULL,   Sigma_c_go = NULL,   mu_t_nogo,   Sigma_t_nogo,   mu_c_nogo = NULL,   Sigma_c_nogo = NULL,   target_go,   target_nogo,   n_t,   n_c = NULL,   theta_TV1 = NULL,   theta_MAV1 = NULL,   theta_TV2 = NULL,   theta_MAV2 = NULL,   theta_NULL1 = NULL,   theta_NULL2 = NULL,   m_t = NULL,   m_c = NULL,   kappa0_t = NULL,   nu0_t = NULL,   mu0_t = NULL,   Lambda0_t = NULL,   kappa0_c = NULL,   nu0_c = NULL,   mu0_c = NULL,   Lambda0_c = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne_t = NULL,   ne_c = NULL,   alpha0e_t = NULL,   alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL,   bar_ye_c = NULL,   se_t = NULL,   se_c = NULL,   nMC = NULL,   CalcMethod = "MC",   gamma_go_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),   gamma_nogo_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),   seed )

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Continuous Endpoints — getgamma2cont
text:
nsim positive integer giving number Monte Carlo datasets simulate per calibration scenario.  Default 10000L. prob character string specifying probability type. Must 'posterior' 'predictive'. design character string specifying trial design. Must 'controlled', 'uncontrolled', 'external'. prior character string specifying prior distribution. Must 'vague' 'N-Inv-Wishart'. GoRegions integer vector region indices (subset 1:9) constitute Go region.  9 regions defined cross-classification two treatment effects \((	heta_1, 	heta_2)\) relative (TV1, MAV1) (TV2, MAV2): region \(k = (z_1 - 1) 	imes 3 + z_2\) \(z_i = 1\) \(	heta_i > TV_i\), \(z_i = 2\) \(MAV_i < 	heta_i \le TV_i\), \(z_i = 3\) \(	heta_i \le MAV_i\). NoGoRegions integer vector region indices (subset 1:9) constitute NoGo region. Must disjoint GoRegions. mu_t_go length-2 numeric vector giving true bivariate mean treatment group Go-calibration scenario (typically Null). Sigma_t_go 2x2 positive-definite numeric matrix giving true within-group covariance treatment group Go-calibration scenario. mu_c_go length-2 numeric vector giving true bivariate mean control group Go-calibration scenario.  Required design = 'controlled' 'external'; set NULL design = 'uncontrolled'. Sigma_c_go 2x2 positive-definite numeric matrix giving true within-group covariance control group Go-calibration scenario.  Required design = 'controlled' 'external'; set NULL design = 'uncontrolled'. mu_t_nogo length-2 numeric vector giving true bivariate mean treatment group NoGo-calibration scenario (typically Alternative). Sigma_t_nogo 2x2 positive-definite numeric matrix giving true within-group covariance treatment group NoGo-calibration scenario. mu_c_nogo length-2 numeric vector giving true bivariate mean control group NoGo-calibration scenario.  Required design = 'controlled' 'external'; set NULL design = 'uncontrolled'. Sigma_c_nogo 2x2 positive-definite numeric matrix giving true within-group covariance control group NoGo-calibration scenario.  Required design = 'controlled' 'external'; set NULL design = 'uncontrolled'. target_go numeric scalar (0, 1) giving upper bound marginal Go probability Go-calibration scenario. optimal \(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\) smallest grid value satisfying constraint. target_nogo numeric scalar (0, 1) giving upper bound marginal NoGo probability NoGo-calibration scenario.  optimal \(\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\) smallest grid value satisfying constraint. n_t positive integer giving number patients treatment group PoC trial. n_c positive integer giving number patients control group PoC trial.  Set NULL design = 'uncontrolled'. theta_TV1 numeric scalar giving TV threshold Endpoint 1.  Required prob = 'posterior'; otherwise set NULL. theta_MAV1 numeric scalar giving MAV threshold Endpoint 1.  Required prob = 'posterior'; otherwise set NULL. theta_TV2 numeric scalar giving TV threshold Endpoint 2.  Required prob = 'posterior'; otherwise set NULL. theta_MAV2 numeric scalar giving MAV threshold Endpoint 2.  Required prob = 'posterior'; otherwise set NULL. theta_NULL1 numeric scalar giving null hypothesis threshold Endpoint 1.  Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set NULL. theta_NULL2 numeric scalar giving null hypothesis threshold Endpoint 2.  Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set NULL. m_t positive integer giving future sample size treatment group.  Required prob = 'predictive'; set NULL otherwise. m_c positive integer giving future sample size control group.  Required prob = 'predictive'; set NULL otherwise. kappa0_t Positive numeric scalar.  NIW prior hyperparameter \(\kappa_{01}\) treatment group.  Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart'; otherwise NULL. nu0_t Positive numeric scalar.  NIW prior degrees freedom \(
u_{01}\) treatment group.  Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart'; otherwise NULL. mu0_t Length-2 numeric vector.  NIW prior mean \(\mu_{01}\) treatment group.  Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart'; otherwise NULL. Lambda0_t 2x2 positive-definite numeric matrix.  NIW prior scale matrix \(\Lambda_{01}\) treatment group.  Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart'; otherwise NULL. kappa0_c Positive numeric scalar; see kappa0_t. control group. nu0_c Positive numeric scalar; see nu0_t. control group. mu0_c Length-2 numeric vector; see mu0_t.  control group.  May required vague prior uncontrolled design; see pbayesdecisionprob2cont. Lambda0_c 2x2 matrix; see Lambda0_t. control group. r positive numeric scalar giving power prior weight control group design = 'uncontrolled' prior = 'vague'.  Otherwise NULL. ne_t positive integer giving external treatment sample size. Required design = 'external' external treatment data used; otherwise NULL. ne_c positive integer giving external control sample size. Required design = 'external' external control data used; otherwise NULL. alpha0e_t numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight external treatment data.  Required external treatment data used; otherwise NULL. alpha0e_c numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight external control data.  Required external control data used; otherwise NULL. bar_ye_t length-2 numeric vector.  External treatment sample mean.  Required external treatment data used; otherwise NULL. bar_ye_c length-2 numeric vector.  External control sample mean. Required external control data used; otherwise NULL. se_t 2x2 numeric matrix.  External treatment sum--squares matrix.  Required external treatment data used; otherwise NULL. se_c 2x2 numeric matrix.  External control sum--squares matrix.  Required external control data used; otherwise NULL. nMC positive integer giving number Monte Carlo draws passed pbayespostpred2cont.  Required CalcMethod = 'MC'.  May set NULL CalcMethod = 'MM' \(
u_k > 4\); CalcMethod = 'MM' \(
u_k \le 4\) causes fallback MC, nMC must positive integer. Default NULL. CalcMethod character string specifying computation method passed pbayespostpred2cont.  Must 'MC' (default) 'MM'. gamma_go_grid numeric vector candidate Go threshold values (0, 1) search .  Defaults seq(0.01, 0.99, = 0.01). gamma_nogo_grid numeric vector candidate NoGo threshold values (0, 1) search .  Defaults seq(0.01, 0.99, = 0.01). seed numeric scalar reproducible random number generation. Go-calibration simulation uses seed NoGo-calibration simulation uses seed + 1 ensure independence two scenarios.

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Continuous Endpoints — getgamma2cont
text: list class getgamma2cont following elements: gamma_go Optimal Go threshold: smallest value gamma_go_grid marginal \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) < \code{target\_go}\) Go-calibration scenario.  NA value exists. gamma_nogo Optimal NoGo threshold: smallest value gamma_nogo_grid marginal \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}) < \code{target\_nogo}\) NoGo-calibration scenario.  NA value exists. PrGo_opt Marginal \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) gamma_go Go-calibration scenario. NA gamma_go NA. PrNoGo_opt Marginal \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo})\) gamma_nogo NoGo-calibration scenario. NA gamma_nogo NA. target_go value target_go supplied user. target_nogo value target_nogo supplied user. grid_results data frame columns gamma_grid, PrGo_grid (marginal Go probability Go-calibration scenario), PrNoGo_grid (marginal NoGo probability NoGo-calibration scenario).

title: Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Continuous Endpoints — getgamma2cont
text: function uses two-stage simulate--sweep strategy: Stage 1 (simulation precomputation): nsim bivariate datasets generated independently calibration scenario. Go-calibration scenario, datasets drawn \(N_2(\mu_{t,\mathrm{go}}, \Sigma_{t,\mathrm{go}})\) (\(N_2(\mu_{c,\mathrm{go}}, \Sigma_{c,\mathrm{go}})\) controlled/external designs); NoGo-calibration scenario, corresponding _nogo parameters used. pbayespostpred2cont called per scenario vectorised mode return \(nsim \times 9\) matrix region probabilities.  probabilities summed GoRegions (Go scenario) NoGoRegions (NoGo scenario) obtain \(\hat{g}_{Go,}\) \(\hat{g}_{NoGo,}\), independent decision thresholds. Stage 2 (gamma sweep): pair \((\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}, \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}})\) two-dimensional grid, operating characteristics computed separately calibration scenario: $$\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) = \frac{1}{n_{\mathrm{sim}}} \sum_{=1}^{n_{\mathrm{sim}}}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{Go,} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{go}},\;   \hat{g}_{NoGo,} < \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\right]$$ $$\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}) = \frac{1}{n_{\mathrm{sim}}} \sum_{=1}^{n_{\mathrm{sim}}}   \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{NoGo,} \ge \gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}},\;   \hat{g}_{Go,} < \gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\right]$$ Stage 3 (optimal threshold selection): candidate \(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\), worst-case \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) \(\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\) gamma_nogo_grid computed; optimal \(\gamma_{\mathrm{go}}\) smallest grid value worst-case probability less target_go. Analogously, optimal \(\gamma_{\mathrm{nogo}}\) smallest grid value worst-case \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo})\) less target_nogo.

}  # Example 4: Controlled design, predictive probability, vague prior if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ Sigma_null <- matrix(c(6400.0, 15.0, 15.0, 36.0), 2, 2) Sigma_alt  <- matrix(c(6400.0, 15.0, 15.0, 36.0), 2, 2) getgamma2cont(   nsim = 1000L, prob = 'predictive', design = 'controlled',   prior = 'vague',   GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 4L,   mu_t_go = c(-5.0, 0.0), Sigma_t_go = Sigma_null,   mu_c_go = c(-10.0, -1.0), Sigma_c_go = Sigma_null,   mu_t_nogo = c(5.0, 1.0), Sigma_t_nogo = Sigma_alt,   mu_c_nogo = c(-10.0, -1.0), Sigma_c_nogo = Sigma_alt,   target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,   n_t = 30L, n_c = 30L,   theta_TV1 = NULL, theta_MAV1 = NULL,   theta_TV2 = NULL, theta_MAV2 = NULL,   theta_NULL1 = 5.0, theta_NULL2 = 1.0,   m_t = 100L, m_c = 100L,   kappa0_t = NULL, nu0_t = NULL, mu0_t = NULL, Lambda0_t = NULL,   kappa0_c = NULL, nu0_c = NULL, mu0_c = NULL, Lambda0_c = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = NULL, se_t = NULL, se_c = NULL,   nMC = 500L, CalcMethod = 'MC',   gamma_go_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),   gamma_nogo_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),   seed = 1L ) } # }  # Example 5: Uncontrolled design, predictive probability, vague prior if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ Sigma_null <- matrix(c(6400.0, 15.0, 15.0, 36.0), 2, 2) Sigma_alt  <- matrix(c(6400.0, 15.0, 15.0, 36.0), 2, 2) getgamma2cont(   nsim = 1000L, prob = 'predictive', design = 'uncontrolled',   prior = 'vague',   GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 4L,   mu_t_go = c(-5.0, 0.0), Sigma_t_go = Sigma_null,   mu_c_go = NULL, Sigma_c_go = NULL,   mu_t_nogo = c(5.0, 1.0), Sigma_t_nogo = Sigma_alt,   mu_c_nogo = NULL, Sigma_c_nogo = NULL,   target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,   n_t = 30L, n_c = NULL,   theta_TV1 = NULL, theta_MAV1 = NULL,   theta_TV2 = NULL, theta_MAV2 = NULL,   theta_NULL1 = 5.0, theta_NULL2 = 1.0,   m_t = 100L, m_c = 100L,   kappa0_t = NULL, nu0_t = NULL, mu0_t = NULL, Lambda0_t = NULL,   kappa0_c = NULL, nu0_c = NULL, mu0_c = c(-10.0, -1.0), Lambda0_c = NULL,   r = 1.0,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = NULL, se_t = NULL, se_c = NULL,   nMC = 500L, CalcMethod = 'MC',   gamma_go_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),   gamma_nogo_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),   seed = 1L ) } # }  # Example 6: External design (control only), predictive probability, NIW prior if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ Sigma  <- matrix(c(6400.0, 15.0, 15.0, 36.0), 2, 2) Lambda <- matrix(c(6400.0, 15.0, 15.0, 36.0), 2, 2) se_c   <- matrix(c(6400.0, 15.0, 15.0, 36.0), 2, 2) getgamma2cont(   nsim = 1000L, prob = 'predictive', design = 'external',   prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart',   GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 4L,   mu_t_go = c(-5.0, 0.0), Sigma_t_go = Sigma,   mu_c_go = c(-10.0, -1.0), Sigma_c_go = Sigma,   mu_t_nogo = c(5.0, 1.0), Sigma_t_nogo = Sigma,   mu_c_nogo = c(-10.0, -1.0), Sigma_c_nogo = Sigma,   target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,   n_t = 30L, n_c = 30L,   theta_TV1 = NULL, theta_MAV1 = NULL,   theta_TV2 = NULL, theta_MAV2 = NULL,   theta_NULL1 = 5.0, theta_NULL2 = 1.0,   m_t = 100L, m_c = 100L,   kappa0_t = 0.1, nu0_t = 4.0, mu0_t = c(0.0, 1.0),  Lambda0_t = Lambda,   kappa0_c = 0.1, nu0_c = 4.0, mu0_c = c(-10.0, -1.0), Lambda0_c = Lambda,   r = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = 10L, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = 0.5,   bar_ye_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = c(-10.0, -1.0), se_t = NULL, se_c = se_c,   nMC = 500L, CalcMethod = 'MC',   gamma_go_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),   gamma_nogo_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),   seed = 1L ) } # }

what: Compute Joint Bivariate Binary Probabilities from Marginal Rates and Correlation — getjointbin
title: Compute Joint Bivariate Binary Probabilities from Marginal Rates and Correlation — getjointbin
text: Converts parameterisation \((\pi_{j1}, \pi_{j2}, \rho_j)\) four-cell joint probability vector \((p_{j,00}, p_{j,01}, p_{j,10}, p_{j,11})\) bivariate binary outcome group \(j \\{t, c\}\). conversion uses standard moment-matching identity Pearson correlation two Bernoulli variables, checks requested correlation feasible supplied marginal rates.

title: Compute Joint Bivariate Binary Probabilities from Marginal Rates and Correlation — getjointbin
code: getjointbin(pi1, pi2, rho, tol = 1e-10)

title: Compute Joint Bivariate Binary Probabilities from Marginal Rates and Correlation — getjointbin
text: pi1 single numeric value (0, 1) giving marginal response probability Endpoint 1 (\(\pi_{j1}\)) group \(j\). pi2 single numeric value (0, 1) giving marginal response probability Endpoint 2 (\(\pi_{j2}\)) group \(j\). rho single numeric value giving Pearson correlation (\(\rho_j\)) Endpoint 1 Endpoint 2 group \(j\). Must lie within feasible range \([\rho_{\min}, \rho_{\max}]\) determined pi1 pi2 (see Details). Use rho = 0 independence. tol single positive numeric value specifying tolerance used checking whether rho lies within feasible range whether resulting probabilities non-negative. Default 1e-10.

title: Compute Joint Bivariate Binary Probabilities from Marginal Rates and Correlation — getjointbin
text: named numeric vector length 4 elements p00, p01, p10, p11, p_lm = Pr(Endpoint 1 = l, Endpoint 2 = m) \(l, m \\{0, 1\}\). elements non-negative sum 1.

title: Compute Joint Bivariate Binary Probabilities from Marginal Rates and Correlation — getjointbin
text: bivariate binary outcome \((Y_{i1}, Y_{i2})\) patient \(\) (\(= 1, \ldots, n_j\)) group \(j\) marginal success probabilities \(\pi_{j1} = \Pr(Y_{i1} = 1)\) \(\pi_{j2} = \Pr(Y_{i2} = 1)\), Pearson correlation $$   \rho_j = \frac{p_{j,11} - \pi_{j1} \pi_{j2}}                 {\sqrt{\pi_{j1}(1 - \pi_{j1})\, \pi_{j2}(1 - \pi_{j2})}}. $$ Solving \(p_{j,11}\) gives $$p_{j,11} = \rho_j \sqrt{\pi_{j1}(1-\pi_{j1})\,\pi_{j2}(1-\pi_{j2})}   + \pi_{j1} \pi_{j2},$$ remaining probabilities follow: $$p_{j,10} = \pi_{j1} - p_{j,11}, \quad       p_{j,01} = \pi_{j2} - p_{j,11}, \quad       p_{j,00} = 1 - p_{j,10} - p_{j,01} - p_{j,11}.$$ four cell probabilities lie [0, 1], correlation must satisfy $$   \rho_{\min} =     \frac{\max(0,\; \pi_{j1} + \pi_{j2} - 1) - \pi_{j1}\pi_{j2}}          {\sqrt{\pi_{j1}(1-\pi_{j1})\,\pi_{j2}(1-\pi_{j2})}}   \;\le\; \rho_j \;\le\;   \frac{\min(\pi_{j1}, \pi_{j2}) - \pi_{j1}\pi_{j2}}        {\sqrt{\pi_{j1}(1-\pi_{j1})\,\pi_{j2}(1-\pi_{j2})}}   = \rho_{\max}. $$ function raises error rho falls outside range (subject tol).

title: Compute Joint Bivariate Binary Probabilities from Marginal Rates and Correlation — getjointbin
code: # Example 1: Independent endpoints (rho = 0) getjointbin(pi1 = 0.3, pi2 = 0.4, rho = 0.0) #>  p00  p01  p10  p11  #> 0.42 0.28 0.18 0.12   # Example 2: Positive correlation getjointbin(pi1 = 0.3, pi2 = 0.4, rho = 0.3) #>       p00       p01       p10       p11  #> 0.4873498 0.2126502 0.1126502 0.1873498   # Example 3: Negative correlation getjointbin(pi1 = 0.3, pi2 = 0.4, rho = -0.2) #>        p00        p01        p10        p11  #> 0.37510011 0.32489989 0.22489989 0.07510011   # Example 4: Verify cell probabilities sum to 1 p <- getjointbin(pi1 = 0.25, pi2 = 0.35, rho = 0.1) sum(p)  # Should be 1 #> [1] 1  # Example 5: Verify marginal recovery p <- getjointbin(pi1 = 0.25, pi2 = 0.35, rho = 0.1) p["p10"] + p["p11"]  # Should equal pi1 = 0.25 #>  p10  #> 0.25  p["p01"] + p["p11"]  # Should equal pi2 = 0.35 #>  p01  #> 0.35

what: Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with a Single Binary Endpoint — pbayesdecisionprob1bin
title: Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with a Single Binary Endpoint — pbayesdecisionprob1bin
text: Evaluates operating characteristics (Go, NoGo, Gray probabilities) binary-outcome clinical trials Bayesian framework enumerating possible trial outcomes. function supports controlled, uncontrolled, external designs.

title: Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with a Single Binary Endpoint — pbayesdecisionprob1bin
code: pbayesdecisionprob1bin(   prob = "posterior",   design = "controlled",   theta_TV = NULL,   theta_MAV = NULL,   theta_NULL = NULL,   gamma_go,   gamma_nogo,   pi_t,   pi_c = NULL,   n_t,   n_c,   a_t,   a_c,   b_t,   b_c,   z = NULL,   m_t = NULL,   m_c = NULL,   ne_t = NULL,   ne_c = NULL,   ye_t = NULL,   ye_c = NULL,   alpha0e_t = NULL,   alpha0e_c = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE,   Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE )

title: Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with a Single Binary Endpoint — pbayesdecisionprob1bin
text: prob character string specifying probability type. Must 'posterior' 'predictive'. design character string specifying trial design. Must 'controlled', 'uncontrolled', 'external'. theta_TV numeric scalar giving target value (TV) threshold used Go decision prob = 'posterior'. Set NULL prob = 'predictive'. theta_MAV numeric scalar giving minimum acceptable value (MAV) threshold used NoGo decision prob = 'posterior'. Must satisfy theta_TV > theta_MAV. Set NULL prob = 'predictive'. theta_NULL numeric scalar giving null hypothesis threshold used Go NoGo decisions prob = 'predictive'. Set NULL prob = 'posterior'. gamma_go numeric scalar (0, 1) giving minimum posterior predictive probability required Go decision. gamma_nogo numeric scalar (0, 1) giving minimum posterior predictive probability required NoGo decision. ordering constraint gamma_go gamma_nogo imposed, though combination determines frequency Miss outcomes. pi_t numeric value vector giving true response probability(s) treatment group used evaluate operating characteristics. element must (0, 1). pi_c numeric value vector giving true response probability(s) control group. design = 'uncontrolled', parameter used calculations must supplied; excluded output. supplied vector, must length pi_t. n_t positive integer giving number patients treatment group proof--concept (PoC) trial. n_c positive integer giving number patients control group PoC trial. design = 'uncontrolled', hypothetical control sample size (required consistency designs). a_t positive numeric scalar giving first shape parameter (alpha) prior Beta distribution treatment group. a_c positive numeric scalar giving first shape parameter (alpha) prior Beta distribution control group. b_t positive numeric scalar giving second shape parameter (beta) prior Beta distribution treatment group. b_c positive numeric scalar giving second shape parameter (beta) prior Beta distribution control group. z non-negative integer giving hypothetical number responders control group. Required design = 'uncontrolled'; otherwise set NULL. used, y_c NULL. m_t positive integer giving number patients treatment group future trial. Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set NULL. m_c positive integer giving number patients control group future trial. Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set NULL. ne_t positive integer giving number patients treatment group external data set. Required design = 'external' external treatment data available; otherwise set NULL. ne_c positive integer giving number patients control group external data set. Required design = 'external' external control data available; otherwise set NULL. ye_t non-negative integer giving number responders treatment group external data set. Required design = 'external'; otherwise set NULL. ye_c non-negative integer giving number responders control group external data set. Required design = 'external'; otherwise set NULL. alpha0e_t numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight treatment group. Required design = 'external'; otherwise NULL. alpha0e_c numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight control group. Required design = 'external'; otherwise NULL. error_if_Miss logical scalar; TRUE (default), function stops error Miss probability > 0, prompting reconsideration thresholds. Gray_inc_Miss logical scalar; TRUE, Miss probability added Gray probability. FALSE (default), Miss reported separately. Active error_if_Miss = FALSE.

title: Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with a Single Binary Endpoint — pbayesdecisionprob1bin
text: data frame one row per pi_t scenario columns: pi_t True treatment response probability. pi_c True control response probability (omitted uncontrolled design). Go Probability making Go decision. Gray Probability making Gray (inconclusive) decision. NoGo Probability making NoGo decision. Miss (Optional) Probability Go NoGo criteria simultaneously met. Included error_if_Miss = FALSE Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE. returned object S3 class pbayesdecisionprob1bin associated print method.

title: Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with a Single Binary Endpoint — pbayesdecisionprob1bin
text: Operating characteristics computed exact enumeration: possible outcome pairs \((y_t, y_c)\) \(y_t \\{0,\ldots,n_t\}\) \(y_c \\{0,\ldots,n_c\}\) (fixed \(z\) uncontrolled) evaluated. pair, pbayespostpred1bin computes posterior predictive probability thresholds (TV/MAV NULL). Outcomes classified Go, NoGo, Miss, Gray: Go: \(P(\mathrm{Go}) \ge \gamma_1\) \(P(\mathrm{NoGo}) < \gamma_2\) NoGo: \(P(\mathrm{Go}) < \gamma_1\) \(P(\mathrm{NoGo}) \ge \gamma_2\) Miss: Go NoGo criteria met simultaneously Gray: neither Go NoGo criteria met outcome weighted binomial probability true rates.

title: Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with a Single Binary Endpoint — pbayesdecisionprob1bin
code: # Example 1: Controlled design with posterior probability pbayesdecisionprob1bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled',   theta_TV = 0.4, theta_MAV = 0.2, theta_NULL = NULL,   gamma_go = 0.8, gamma_nogo = 0.2,   pi_t = c(0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8), pi_c = rep(0.2, 4),   n_t = 12, n_c = 12,   a_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, b_c = 0.5,   z = NULL, m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, ye_t = NULL, ye_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE ) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Binary Endpoint)  #> ----------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : posterior  #>   Design           : controlled  #>   Threshold(s)     : TV = 0.4, MAV = 0.2  #>   Go  threshold    : gamma_go = 0.8  #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma_nogo = 0.2  #>   Sample size      : n_t = 12, n_c = 12  #>   Prior (Beta)     : a_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, b_c = 0.5  #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE  #> ----------------------------------------------------------------  #>  pi_t pi_c     Go   Gray   NoGo #>   0.2  0.2 0.0004 0.0098 0.9898 #>   0.4  0.2 0.0277 0.1405 0.8318 #>   0.6  0.2 0.2224 0.3427 0.4349 #>   0.8  0.2 0.6559 0.2549 0.0892 #> ----------------------------------------------------------------   # Example 2: Uncontrolled design with hypothetical control pbayesdecisionprob1bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'uncontrolled',   theta_TV = 0.30, theta_MAV = 0.15, theta_NULL = NULL,   gamma_go = 0.75, gamma_nogo = 0.25,   pi_t = c(0.3, 0.5, 0.7), pi_c = NULL,   n_t = 15, n_c = 15,   a_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, b_c = 0.5,   z = 5, m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, ye_t = NULL, ye_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE ) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Binary Endpoint)  #> ----------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : posterior  #>   Design           : uncontrolled  #>   Threshold(s)     : TV = 0.3, MAV = 0.15  #>   Go  threshold    : gamma_go = 0.75  #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma_nogo = 0.25  #>   Sample size      : n_t = 15, n_c = 15  #>   Prior (Beta)     : a_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, b_c = 0.5  #>   Uncontrolled     : z = 5  #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE  #> ----------------------------------------------------------------  #>  pi_t     Go   Gray   NoGo #>   0.3 0.0000 0.0036 0.9963 #>   0.5 0.0176 0.1333 0.8491 #>   0.7 0.2969 0.4248 0.2784 #> ----------------------------------------------------------------   # Example 3: External design with 50 percent power prior borrowing pbayesdecisionprob1bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'external',   theta_TV = 0.4, theta_MAV = 0.2, theta_NULL = NULL,   gamma_go = 0.8, gamma_nogo = 0.2,   pi_t = c(0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8), pi_c = rep(0.2, 4),   n_t = 12, n_c = 12,   a_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, b_c = 0.5,   z = NULL, m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   ne_t = 15, ne_c = 15, ye_t = 6, ye_c = 4, alpha0e_t = 0.5, alpha0e_c = 0.5,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE ) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Binary Endpoint)  #> ----------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : posterior  #>   Design           : external  #>   Threshold(s)     : TV = 0.4, MAV = 0.2  #>   Go  threshold    : gamma_go = 0.8  #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma_nogo = 0.2  #>   Sample size      : n_t = 12, n_c = 12  #>   Prior (Beta)     : a_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, b_c = 0.5  #>   External data    : ne_t = 15, ne_c = 15, ye_t = 6, ye_c = 4  #>                      alpha0e_t = 0.5, alpha0e_c = 0.5  #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE  #> ----------------------------------------------------------------  #>  pi_t pi_c     Go   Gray   NoGo #>   0.2  0.2 0.0000 0.0023 0.9977 #>   0.4  0.2 0.0003 0.0756 0.9241 #>   0.6  0.2 0.0104 0.3742 0.6154 #>   0.8  0.2 0.1145 0.6966 0.1889 #> ----------------------------------------------------------------   # Example 4: Posterior predictive probability for controlled design pbayesdecisionprob1bin(   prob = 'predictive', design = 'controlled',   theta_TV = NULL, theta_MAV = NULL, theta_NULL = 0,   gamma_go = 0.9, gamma_nogo = 0.3,   pi_t = c(0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8), pi_c = rep(0.2, 4),   n_t = 12, n_c = 12,   a_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, b_c = 0.5,   z = NULL, m_t = 30, m_c = 30,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, ye_t = NULL, ye_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE ) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Binary Endpoint)  #> ----------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : predictive  #>   Design           : controlled  #>   Threshold(s)     : NULL = 0  #>   Go  threshold    : gamma_go = 0.9  #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma_nogo = 0.3  #>   Sample size      : n_t = 12, n_c = 12  #>   Prior (Beta-bin) : a_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, b_c = 0.5  #>   Future trial     : m_t = 30, m_c = 30  #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE  #> ----------------------------------------------------------------  #>  pi_t pi_c     Go   Gray   NoGo #>   0.2  0.2 0.0518 0.1672 0.7810 #>   0.4  0.2 0.3196 0.3429 0.3375 #>   0.6  0.2 0.7302 0.2007 0.0691 #>   0.8  0.2 0.9638 0.0324 0.0038 #> ----------------------------------------------------------------   # Example 5: Uncontrolled design with posterior predictive probability pbayesdecisionprob1bin(   prob = 'predictive', design = 'uncontrolled',   theta_TV = NULL, theta_MAV = NULL, theta_NULL = 0,   gamma_go = 0.75, gamma_nogo = 0.25,   pi_t = c(0.3, 0.5, 0.7), pi_c = NULL,   n_t = 15, n_c = 15,   a_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, b_c = 0.5,   z = 5, m_t = 30, m_c = 30,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, ye_t = NULL, ye_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE ) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Binary Endpoint)  #> ----------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : predictive  #>   Design           : uncontrolled  #>   Threshold(s)     : NULL = 0  #>   Go  threshold    : gamma_go = 0.75  #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma_nogo = 0.25  #>   Sample size      : n_t = 15, n_c = 15  #>   Prior (Beta-bin) : a_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, b_c = 0.5  #>   Uncontrolled     : z = 5  #>   Future trial     : m_t = 30, m_c = 30  #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE  #> ----------------------------------------------------------------  #>  pi_t     Go   Gray   NoGo #>   0.3 0.0500 0.0000 0.9500 #>   0.5 0.5000 0.0000 0.5000 #>   0.7 0.9500 0.0000 0.0500 #> ----------------------------------------------------------------   # Example 6: External design with posterior predictive probability pbayesdecisionprob1bin(   prob = 'predictive', design = 'external',   theta_TV = NULL, theta_MAV = NULL, theta_NULL = 0,   gamma_go = 0.9, gamma_nogo = 0.3,   pi_t = c(0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8), pi_c = rep(0.2, 4),   n_t = 12, n_c = 12,   a_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, b_c = 0.5,   z = NULL, m_t = 30, m_c = 30,   ne_t = 15, ne_c = 15, ye_t = 6, ye_c = 4, alpha0e_t = 0.5, alpha0e_c = 0.5,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE ) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Binary Endpoint)  #> ----------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : predictive  #>   Design           : external  #>   Threshold(s)     : NULL = 0  #>   Go  threshold    : gamma_go = 0.9  #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma_nogo = 0.3  #>   Sample size      : n_t = 12, n_c = 12  #>   Prior (Beta-bin) : a_t = 0.5, a_c = 0.5, b_t = 0.5, b_c = 0.5  #>   Future trial     : m_t = 30, m_c = 30  #>   External data    : ne_t = 15, ne_c = 15, ye_t = 6, ye_c = 4  #>                      alpha0e_t = 0.5, alpha0e_c = 0.5  #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE  #> ----------------------------------------------------------------  #>  pi_t pi_c     Go   Gray   NoGo #>   0.2  0.2 0.0303 0.2029 0.7668 #>   0.4  0.2 0.2296 0.4340 0.3363 #>   0.6  0.2 0.5888 0.3421 0.0691 #>   0.8  0.2 0.9115 0.0847 0.0038 #> ----------------------------------------------------------------

what: Bayesian Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for Single Continuous Endpoint — pbayesdecisionprob1cont
title: Bayesian Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for Single Continuous Endpoint — pbayesdecisionprob1cont
text: Evaluates Go/NoGo/Gray decision probabilities single continuous endpoint via Monte Carlo simulation. Supports controlled (parallel control), uncontrolled (single-arm informative priors), external (power prior borrowing) designs posterior predictive probability approaches.

title: Bayesian Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for Single Continuous Endpoint — pbayesdecisionprob1cont
code: pbayesdecisionprob1cont(   nsim,   prob,   design,   prior,   CalcMethod,   theta_TV = NULL,   theta_MAV = NULL,   theta_NULL = NULL,   nMC = NULL,   gamma_go,   gamma_nogo,   n_t,   n_c = NULL,   m_t = NULL,   m_c = NULL,   kappa0_t = NULL,   kappa0_c = NULL,   nu0_t = NULL,   nu0_c = NULL,   mu0_t = NULL,   mu0_c = NULL,   sigma0_t = NULL,   sigma0_c = NULL,   mu_t,   mu_c = NULL,   sigma_t,   sigma_c = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne_t = NULL,   ne_c = NULL,   alpha0e_t = NULL,   alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL,   bar_ye_c = NULL,   se_t = NULL,   se_c = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE,   Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE,   seed )

title: Bayesian Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for Single Continuous Endpoint — pbayesdecisionprob1cont
text: nsim positive integer specifying number Monte Carlo simulation replicates. prob character string specifying probability type: 'posterior' 'predictive'. design character string specifying trial design: 'controlled', 'uncontrolled', 'external'. prior character string specifying prior distribution: 'vague' 'N-Inv-Chisq'. CalcMethod character string specifying computation method: 'NI' (Numerical Integration), 'MC' (Monte Carlo), 'MM' (Moment Matching). theta_TV numeric value representing target value (TV) threshold Go decision. Required prob = 'posterior'; otherwise NULL. Default NULL. theta_MAV numeric value representing minimum acceptable value (MAV) threshold NoGo decision. Required prob = 'posterior'; otherwise NULL. Default NULL. theta_NULL numeric value representing null hypothesis threshold. Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise NULL. Default NULL. nMC positive integer specifying number Monte Carlo draws computing posterior probabilities. Required CalcMethod = 'MC'; otherwise NULL. gamma_go numeric scalar (0, 1) giving minimum posterior predictive probability required Go decision. gamma_nogo numeric scalar (0, 1) giving minimum posterior predictive probability required NoGo decision. ordering constraint gamma_go gamma_nogo imposed, though combination determines frequency Miss outcomes. n_t positive integer giving number patients treatment group proof--concept (PoC) trial. n_c positive integer giving number patients control group proof--concept (PoC) trial. Required design = 'controlled' 'external'; set NULL design = 'uncontrolled'. m_t positive integer representing future sample size treatment group. Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise NULL. Default NULL. m_c positive integer representing future sample size control group. Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise NULL. Default NULL. mu_t numeric value representing true mean treatment group simulation. mu_c numeric value representing true mean control group simulation. uncontrolled design, represents historical control mean. Set NULL design = 'uncontrolled'. sigma_t positive numeric value representing true standard deviation treatment group simulation. sigma_c positive numeric value representing true standard deviation control group simulation. uncontrolled design, represents historical control standard deviation. Set NULL design = 'uncontrolled'. r positive numeric value representing variance scaling factor allows scale hypothetical control different treatment. Specifically, sd.control = sqrt(r) * sd.treatment. Required design = 'uncontrolled'. r = 1, control treatment variance scale. ne_t positive integer representing number patients treatment group external data. Required design = 'external' external treatment data available. ne_c positive integer representing number patients control group external data. Required design = 'external' external control data available. alpha0e_t numeric value (0, 1] representing power prior scale parameter treatment group. title: Bayesian Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for Single Continuous Endpoint — pbayesdecisionprob1cont
text: function performs Monte Carlo simulation evaluate operating characteristics : Generating random trial data based specified true parameters Computing posterior predictive probabilities simulated trial Classifying trial Go, NoGo, Gray based decision thresholds Posterior parameter calculations (mu.t1, mu.t2, sd.t1, sd.t2, nu.t1, nu.t2) fully vectorized nsim simulated datasets. pbayespostpred1cont called twice (per threshold), call receiving vectors length nsim returning vector nsim probabilities - inner loop simulation replicates required. external designs, power priors incorporated using exact conjugate representation: Power priors normal data mathematically equivalent Normal-Inverse-Chi-squared distributions enables closed-form computation without MCMC sampling Alpha parameters control degree borrowing (0 = borrowing, 1 = full borrowing) Decision rules: Go: gGo >= gamma_go gNoGo < gamma_nogo NoGo: gGo < gamma_go gNoGo >= gamma_nogo Gray: neither Go NoGo criterion met Miss: Go NoGo criteria met simultaneously Handling Miss probability: error_if_Miss = TRUE (default): Function stops error Miss probability > 0, prompting reconsideration thresholds error_if_Miss = FALSE Gray_inc_Miss = TRUE: Miss probability added Gray probability error_if_Miss = FALSE Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE: Miss probability reported separate category function can used : Controlled design: Two-arm randomized trial Uncontrolled design: Single-arm trial informative priors (historical control) External design: Incorporating historical data power priors

code: # Example 1: Controlled design with vague prior and NI method # (default: error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE) pbayesdecisionprob1cont(   nsim = 100, prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled', prior = 'vague', CalcMethod = 'NI',   theta_TV = 1.5, theta_MAV = -0.5, theta_NULL = NULL,   nMC = NULL, gamma_go = 0.7, gamma_nogo = 0.2,   n_t = 15, n_c = 15, m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   kappa0_t = NULL, kappa0_c = NULL, nu0_t = NULL, nu0_c = NULL,   mu0_t = NULL, mu0_c = NULL, sigma0_t = NULL, sigma0_c = NULL,   mu_t = 3, mu_c = 1, sigma_t = 1.2, sigma_c = 1.1,   r = NULL, ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = NULL, se_t = NULL, se_c = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE, seed = 2 ) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Continuous Endpoint)  #> ----------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : posterior  #>   Design           : controlled  #>   Prior            : vague  #>   Calc method      : NI  #>   Simulations      : nsim = 100  #>   Threshold(s)     : TV = 1.5, MAV = -0.5  #>   Go  threshold    : gamma_go = 0.7  #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma_nogo = 0.2  #>   Sample size      : n_t = 15, n_c = 15  #>   True SD          : sigma_t = 1.2, sigma_c = 1.1  #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE  #>   Seed             : 2  #> ----------------------------------------------------------------  #>  mu_t mu_c   Go Gray NoGo #>     3    1 0.73 0.27    0 #> ----------------------------------------------------------------   # Example 2: Uncontrolled design with informative prior pbayesdecisionprob1cont(   nsim = 100, prob = 'posterior', design = 'uncontrolled', prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq', CalcMethod = 'NI',   theta_TV = 1.0, theta_MAV = 0.0, theta_NULL = NULL,   nMC = NULL, gamma_go = 0.8, gamma_nogo = 0.2,   n_t = 20, n_c = NULL, m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   kappa0_t = 2, kappa0_c = NULL, nu0_t = 5, nu0_c = NULL,   mu0_t = 3.0, mu0_c = 1.5, sigma0_t = 1.5, sigma0_c = NULL,   mu_t = 3.5, mu_c = NULL, sigma_t = 1.3, sigma_c = NULL,   r = 1, ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = NULL, se_t = NULL, se_c = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE, seed = 3 ) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Continuous Endpoint)  #> --------------------------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : posterior  #>   Design           : uncontrolled  #>   Prior            : N-Inv-Chisq  #>   Calc method      : NI  #>   Simulations      : nsim = 100  #>   Threshold(s)     : TV = 1, MAV = 0  #>   Go  threshold    : gamma_go = 0.8  #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma_nogo = 0.2  #>   Sample size      : n_t = 20, n_c = NULL  #>   True SD          : sigma_t = 1.3, sigma_c = NULL  #>   Variance ratio   : r = 1  #>   Prior (treatment): kappa0_t = 2, nu0_t = 5, mu0_t = 3, sigma0_t = 1.5  #>   Prior (control)  : kappa0_c = NULL, nu0_c = NULL, mu0_c = 1.5, sigma0_c = NULL  #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE  #>   Seed             : 3  #> --------------------------------------------------------------------------------  #>  mu_t Go Gray NoGo #>   3.5  1    0    0 #> --------------------------------------------------------------------------------   # Example 3: External design with control data using MM approximation pbayesdecisionprob1cont(   nsim = 100, prob = 'posterior', design = 'external', prior = 'vague', CalcMethod = 'MM',   theta_TV = 1.0, theta_MAV = 0.0, theta_NULL = NULL,   nMC = NULL, gamma_go = 0.8, gamma_nogo = 0.2,   n_t = 12, n_c = 12, m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   kappa0_t = NULL, kappa0_c = NULL, nu0_t = NULL, nu0_c = NULL,   mu0_t = NULL, mu0_c = NULL, sigma0_t = NULL, sigma0_c = NULL,   mu_t = 2, mu_c = 0, sigma_t = 1, sigma_c = 1,   r = NULL, ne_t = NULL, ne_c = 20, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = 0.5,   bar_ye_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = 0, se_t = NULL, se_c = 1,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE, seed = 4 ) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Continuous Endpoint)  #> --------------------------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : posterior  #>   Design           : external  #>   Prior            : vague  #>   Calc method      : MM  #>   Simulations      : nsim = 100  #>   Threshold(s)     : TV = 1, MAV = 0  #>   Go  threshold    : gamma_go = 0.8  #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma_nogo = 0.2  #>   Sample size      : n_t = 12, n_c = 12  #>   True SD          : sigma_t = 1, sigma_c = 1  #>   External (treat.): ne_t = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, bar_ye_t = NULL, se_t = NULL  #>   External (cont.) : ne_c = 20, alpha0e_c = 0.5, bar_ye_c = 0, se_c = 1  #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE  #>   Seed             : 4  #> --------------------------------------------------------------------------------  #>  mu_t mu_c   Go Gray NoGo #>     2    0 0.97 0.03    0 #> --------------------------------------------------------------------------------   # Example 4: Controlled design with predictive probability pbayesdecisionprob1cont(   nsim = 100, prob = 'predictive', design = 'controlled', prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq', CalcMethod = 'NI',   theta_TV = NULL, theta_MAV = NULL, theta_NULL = 2.0,   nMC = NULL, gamma_go = 0.75, gamma_nogo = 0.35,   n_t = 15, n_c = 15, m_t = 50, m_c = 50,   kappa0_t = 3, kappa0_c = 3, nu0_t = 4, nu0_c = 4,   mu0_t = 3.5, mu0_c = 1.5, sigma0_t = 1.5, sigma0_c = 1.5,   mu_t = 3.2, mu_c = 1.3, sigma_t = 1.4, sigma_c = 1.2,   r = NULL, ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = NULL, se_t = NULL, se_c = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE, seed = 5 ) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Continuous Endpoint)  #> -------------------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : predictive  #>   Design           : controlled  #>   Prior            : N-Inv-Chisq  #>   Calc method      : NI  #>   Simulations      : nsim = 100  #>   Threshold(s)     : NULL = 2  #>   Go  threshold    : gamma_go = 0.75  #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma_nogo = 0.35  #>   Sample size      : n_t = 15, n_c = 15  #>   True SD          : sigma_t = 1.4, sigma_c = 1.2  #>   Future size      : m_t = 50, m_c = 50  #>   Prior (treatment): kappa0_t = 3, nu0_t = 4, mu0_t = 3.5, sigma0_t = 1.5  #>   Prior (control)  : kappa0_c = 3, nu0_c = 4, mu0_c = 1.5, sigma0_c = 1.5  #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE  #>   Seed             : 5  #> -------------------------------------------------------------------------  #>  mu_t mu_c   Go Gray NoGo #>   3.2  1.3 0.24 0.06  0.7 #> -------------------------------------------------------------------------   # Example 5: Uncontrolled design with predictive probability pbayesdecisionprob1cont(   nsim = 100, prob = 'predictive', design = 'uncontrolled', prior = 'vague', CalcMethod = 'NI',   theta_TV = NULL, theta_MAV = NULL, theta_NULL = 1.0,   nMC = NULL, gamma_go = 0.75, gamma_nogo = 0.35,   n_t = 20, n_c = NULL, m_t = 40, m_c = 40,   kappa0_t = NULL, kappa0_c = NULL, nu0_t = NULL, nu0_c = NULL,   mu0_t = NULL, mu0_c = 1.5, sigma0_t = NULL, sigma0_c = NULL,   mu_t = 3, mu_c = NULL, sigma_t = 1.3, sigma_c = NULL,   r = 1, ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = NULL, se_t = NULL, se_c = NULL,   error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE, seed = 9 ) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Continuous Endpoint)  #> ----------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : predictive  #>   Design           : uncontrolled  #>   Prior            : vague  #>   Calc method      : NI  #>   Simulations      : nsim = 100  #>   Threshold(s)     : NULL = 1  #>   Go  threshold    : gamma_go = 0.75  #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma_nogo = 0.35  #>   Sample size      : n_t = 20, n_c = NULL  #>   True SD          : sigma_t = 1.3, sigma_c = NULL  #>   Variance ratio   : r = 1  #>   Future size      : m_t = 40, m_c = 40  #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = TRUE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE  #>   Seed             : 9  #> ----------------------------------------------------------------  #>  mu_t   Go Gray NoGo #>     3 0.84 0.05 0.11 #> ----------------------------------------------------------------   # Example 6: External design with predictive probability using MC method pbayesdecisionprob1cont(   nsim = 100, prob = 'predictive', design = 'external', prior = 'vague', CalcMethod = 'MC',   theta_TV = NULL, theta_MAV = NULL, theta_NULL = 1.5,   nMC = 5000, gamma_go = 0.7, gamma_nogo = 0.4,   n_t = 12, n_c = 12, m_t = 30, m_c = 30,   kappa0_t = NULL, kappa0_c = NULL, nu0_t = NULL, nu0_c = NULL,   mu0_t = NULL, mu0_c = NULL, sigma0_t = NULL, sigma0_c = NULL,   mu_t = 2.5, mu_c = 1.0, sigma_t = 1.3, sigma_c = 1.1,   r = NULL, ne_t = 15, ne_c = 18, alpha0e_t = 0.6, alpha0e_c = 0.7,   bar_ye_t = 2.3, bar_ye_c = 0.9, se_t = 1.2, se_c = 1.0,   error_if_Miss = FALSE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE, seed = 6 ) #> Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities (Single Continuous Endpoint)  #> ---------------------------------------------------------------------------  #>   Probability type : predictive  #>   Design           : external  #>   Prior            : vague  #>   Calc method      : MC  #>   Simulations      : nsim = 100  #>   MC draws         : nMC = 5000  #>   Threshold(s)     : NULL = 1.5  #>   Go  threshold    : gamma_go = 0.7  #>   NoGo threshold   : gamma_nogo = 0.4  #>   Sample size      : n_t = 12, n_c = 12  #>   True SD          : sigma_t = 1.3, sigma_c = 1.1  #>   Future size      : m_t = 30, m_c = 30  #>   External (treat.): ne_t = 15, alpha0e_t = 0.6, bar_ye_t = 2.3, se_t = 1.2  #>   External (cont.) : ne_c = 18, alpha0e_c = 0.7, bar_ye_c = 0.9, se_c = 1  #>   Miss handling    : error_if_Miss = FALSE, Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE  #>   Seed             : 6  #> ---------------------------------------------------------------------------  #>  mu_t mu_c   Go Gray NoGo Miss #>   2.5    1 0.21 0.12 0.67    0 #> ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

title: Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for a Clinical Trial with Two Binary Endpoints — pbayesdecisionprob2bin
text: Computation proceeds two stages efficiency.  Stage 1, posterior predictive probabilities precomputed every possible multinomial count combination \((x_t, x_c)\).  Stage 2, operating characteristics scenario obtained weighting Stage 1 decisions multinomial probabilities true parameters, avoiding repeated Monte Carlo sampling per scenario. Two-stage computation. Stage 1 (precomputation): possible multinomial count combinations \(x_t\) enumerated using allmultinom. design = 'controlled' 'external', combinations \(x_c\) also enumerated Go/NoGo probability matrix PrGo[, j] dimensions \(n_t \times n_c\). design = 'uncontrolled', control distribution fixed \(\mathrm{Dir}(\alpha_{2,**} + z_{**})\) depend observed control counts; probability matrix therefore dimensions \(n_t \times 1\), eliminating \(\binom{n_2+3}{3}\)-row control enumeration associated Gamma sampling. stage independent true scenario parameters. Stage 2 (scenario evaluation): scenario \((\pi_{t1}, \pi_{t2}, \rho_t, \pi_{c1}, \pi_{c2}, \rho_c)\), getjointbin converts marginal rates correlation four-cell probability vector \(p_k\).  multinomial probability mass function weights Stage 1 decisions: $$\Pr(\mathrm{Go} \mid \Theta) = \sum_{,j}       \mathbf{1}(\mathrm{Go}_{ij}) \cdot       P(x_{t,} \mid n_1, p_t) \cdot P(x_{c,j} \mid n_2, p_c),$$ \(\mathbf{1}(\mathrm{Go}_{ij})\) Go indicator Stage 1. Stage 2 requires additional Monte Carlo fast even large scenario grids. Decision categories. Go: sum Go region probabilities \(\ge \gamma_1\) sum NoGo region probabilities \(< \gamma_2\). NoGo: sum Go region probabilities \(< \gamma_1\) sum NoGo region probabilities \(\ge \gamma_2\). Miss: Go NoGo criteria satisfied simultaneously. Gray: neither Go NoGo criterion satisfied.
