what: Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for Two Continuous Endpoints — pbayesdecisionprob2cont
title: Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for Two Continuous Endpoints — pbayesdecisionprob2cont
text: Computes operating characteristics (Go, NoGo, Gray, optionally Miss probabilities) two-continuous-endpoint Bayesian Go/NoGo decision framework Monte Carlo simulation treatment scenarios.

title: Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for Two Continuous Endpoints — pbayesdecisionprob2cont
code: pbayesdecisionprob2cont(   nsim,   prob,   design,   prior,   GoRegions,   NoGoRegions,   gamma_go,   gamma_nogo,   theta_TV1 = NULL,   theta_MAV1 = NULL,   theta_TV2 = NULL,   theta_MAV2 = NULL,   theta_NULL1 = NULL,   theta_NULL2 = NULL,   n_t,   n_c = NULL,   m_t = NULL,   m_c = NULL,   mu_t,   Sigma_t,   mu_c = NULL,   Sigma_c = NULL,   kappa0_t = NULL,   nu0_t = NULL,   mu0_t = NULL,   Lambda0_t = NULL,   kappa0_c = NULL,   nu0_c = NULL,   mu0_c = NULL,   Lambda0_c = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne_t = NULL,   ne_c = NULL,   alpha0e_t = NULL,   alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL,   bar_ye_c = NULL,   se_t = NULL,   se_c = NULL,   nMC = NULL,   CalcMethod = "MC",   error_if_Miss = TRUE,   Gray_inc_Miss = FALSE,   seed )

title: Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for Two Continuous Endpoints — pbayesdecisionprob2cont
text:
nsim positive integer. Number simulated datasets per scenario. prob character string specifying probability type. Must 'posterior' 'predictive'. design character string specifying trial design. Must 'controlled', 'uncontrolled', 'external'. prior character string specifying prior distribution. Must 'vague' 'N-Inv-Wishart'. GoRegions integer vector specifying nine posterior regions (R1–R9) four predictive regions (R1–R4) constitute Go decision.  prob = 'posterior', valid values integers 1–9; prob = 'predictive', 1–4. common choice GoRegions = 1 (endpoints exceed TV NULL posterior/predictive, respectively). NoGoRegions integer vector specifying regions constitute NoGo decision.  common choice NoGoRegions = 9 (endpoints MAV) posterior, NoGoRegions = 4 predictive.  Must disjoint GoRegions. gamma_go numeric scalar (0, 1). Go threshold: Go decision made \(P(\mathrm{GoRegions}) \ge \gamma_1\). gamma_nogo numeric scalar (0, 1). NoGo threshold: NoGo decision made \(P(\mathrm{NoGoRegions}) \ge \gamma_2\). ordering constraint gamma_go gamma_nogo imposed; combination determines frequency Miss outcomes. theta_TV1 numeric scalar giving target value (TV) threshold Endpoint 1. Required prob = 'posterior'; must satisfy theta_TV1 > theta_MAV1. Set NULL prob = 'predictive'. theta_MAV1 numeric scalar giving minimum acceptable value (MAV) threshold Endpoint 1. Required prob = 'posterior'; must satisfy theta_TV1 > theta_MAV1. Set NULL prob = 'predictive'. theta_TV2 numeric scalar giving target value (TV) threshold Endpoint 2. Required prob = 'posterior'; must satisfy theta_TV2 > theta_MAV2. Set NULL prob = 'predictive'. theta_MAV2 numeric scalar giving minimum acceptable value (MAV) threshold Endpoint 2. Required prob = 'posterior'; must satisfy theta_TV2 > theta_MAV2. Set NULL prob = 'predictive'. theta_NULL1 numeric scalar giving null hypothesis threshold Endpoint 1. Required prob = 'predictive'; set NULL prob = 'posterior'. theta_NULL2 numeric scalar giving null hypothesis threshold Endpoint 2. Required prob = 'predictive'; set NULL prob = 'posterior'. n_t positive integer giving number patients treatment group proof--concept (PoC) trial. n_c positive integer giving number patients control group PoC trial. design = 'uncontrolled', hypothetical control sample size (required consistency designs). m_t positive integer giving number patients treatment group future trial. Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set NULL. m_c positive integer giving number patients control group future trial. Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set NULL. mu_t Numeric matrix 2 columns. row gives true treatment mean vector one scenario.  length-2 vector coerced 1-row matrix. Sigma_t 2x2 positive-definite matrix. True treatment covariance. mu_c Numeric matrix 2 columns length-2 vector. True control (hypothetical control) mean vector(s). Sigma_c 2x2 positive-definite matrix. True control covariance. kappa0_t positive numeric scalar giving NIW prior concentration parameter treatment group. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart'; otherwise set NULL. nu0_t numeric scalar giving NIW prior degrees freedom treatment group. Must greater 3. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart'; otherwise set NULL. mu0_t numeric vector length 2 giving NIW prior mean treatment group. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart'; otherwise set NULL. Lambda0_t 2x2 positive-definite numeric matrix giving NIW prior scale matrix treatment group. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart'; otherwise set NULL. kappa0_c positive numeric scalar giving NIW prior concentration parameter control group. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart' design != 'uncontrolled'; otherwise set NULL. nu0_c numeric scalar giving NIW prior degrees freedom control group. Must greater 3. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart' design != 'uncontrolled'; otherwise set NULL. mu0_c numeric vector length 2 giving NIW prior mean control group, hypothetical control location design = 'uncontrolled'. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart'; otherwise set NULL. Lambda0_c 2x2 positive-definite numeric matrix giving NIW prior scale matrix control group. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart' design != 'uncontrolled'; otherwise set NULL. r positive numeric scalar giving variance scaling factor hypothetical control distribution. Required design = 'uncontrolled'; otherwise set NULL. ne_t positive integer giving external treatment group sample size. Required design = 'external' external treatment data used; otherwise set NULL. ne_c positive integer giving external control group sample size. Required design = 'external' external control data used; otherwise set NULL. alpha0e_t numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight external treatment data. Required external treatment data used; otherwise set NULL. alpha0e_c numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight external control data. Required external control data used; otherwise set NULL. bar_ye_t numeric vector length 2 giving external treatment group sample mean. Required external treatment data used; otherwise set NULL. bar_ye_c numeric vector length 2 giving external control group sample mean. Required external control data used; otherwise set NULL. se_t 2x2 numeric matrix giving external treatment group sum--squares matrix. Required external treatment data used; otherwise set NULL. se_c 2x2 numeric matrix giving external control group sum--squares matrix. Required external control data used; otherwise set NULL. nMC positive integer giving number Monte Carlo draws used estimate region probabilities. Default 10000. Required CalcMethod = 'MC'. May set NULL CalcMethod = 'MM' \(
u_k > 4\) (MM method uses mvtnorm::pmvt analytically); CalcMethod = 'MM' \(
u_k \le 4\) causes fallback MC, nMC must positive integer. CalcMethod character string specifying computation method. Must 'MC' (Monte Carlo, default) 'MM' (Moment-Matching via mvtnorm::pmvt). CalcMethod = 'MM' \(
u_k \le 4\), warning issued function falls back CalcMethod = 'MC'. error_if_Miss Logical. TRUE (default), function stops error positive Miss probability obtained. FALSE, Miss probability handled according Gray_inc_Miss. Gray_inc_Miss Logical. TRUE, Miss probability included Gray probability. FALSE (default), Miss probability reported separate column. Active error_if_Miss = FALSE. seed single numeric value. Seed reproducible random number generation.

title: Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for Two Continuous Endpoints — pbayesdecisionprob2cont
text: data frame class c("pbayesdecisionprob2cont", "data.frame") columns scenario parameters Go, NoGo, Gray (optionally Miss) probabilities.  input parameters attached attributes.

title: Go/NoGo/Gray Decision Probabilities for Two Continuous Endpoints — pbayesdecisionprob2cont
text: function follows structure pbayesdecisionprob1cont: scenario \(s\), nsim datasets simulated generating treatment (control) observations \(N_2(\mu_k^{(s)}, \Sigma_k)\).  minimise overhead, raw standardised residuals generated (scenario- invariant) shifted scenario mean. nsim simulated sufficient statistics \((\bar{y}_{1,}, S_{1,})\) (\((\bar{y}_{2,}, S_{2,})\) controlled/external designs) passed pbayespostpred2cont single vectorised call, returning \(nsim \times n_{\rm regions}\) matrix region probabilities. Go/NoGo/Miss probabilities obtained column means indicator matrices derived region probability matrix.

what: Bayesian Posterior or Posterior Predictive Probability for a Single Binary Endpoint — pbayespostpred1bin
title: Bayesian Posterior or Posterior Predictive Probability for a Single Binary Endpoint — pbayespostpred1bin
text: Computes Bayesian posterior probability posterior predictive probability binary-outcome clinical trials beta-binomial conjugate model. function supports controlled, uncontrolled, external designs, optional incorporation external data power priors. Vector inputs y_t y_c supported efficient batch processing (e.g., across possible trial outcomes pbayesdecisionprob1bin).

title: Bayesian Posterior or Posterior Predictive Probability for a Single Binary Endpoint — pbayespostpred1bin
code: pbayespostpred1bin(   prob = "posterior",   design = "controlled",   theta0,   n_t,   n_c,   y_t,   y_c = NULL,   a_t,   a_c,   b_t,   b_c,   m_t = NULL,   m_c = NULL,   z = NULL,   ne_t = NULL,   ne_c = NULL,   ye_t = NULL,   ye_c = NULL,   alpha0e_t = NULL,   alpha0e_c = NULL,   lower.tail = TRUE )

title: Bayesian Posterior or Posterior Predictive Probability for a Single Binary Endpoint — pbayespostpred1bin
text: prob character string specifying probability type. Must 'posterior' 'predictive'. design character string specifying trial design. Must 'controlled', 'uncontrolled', 'external'. theta0 numeric scalar (-1, 1) giving threshold treatment effect (difference response rates). n_t positive integer giving number patients treatment group proof--concept (PoC) trial. n_c positive integer giving number patients control group PoC trial. design = 'uncontrolled', hypothetical control sample size (required consistency designs). y_t non-negative integer integer vector giving observed number responders treatment group (must satisfy 0 <= y_t <= n_t). y_c non-negative integer integer vector giving number responders control group (must satisfy 0 <= y_c <= n_c). Set NULL design = 'uncontrolled' use z instead. provided vector, must length y_t. a_t positive numeric scalar giving first shape parameter (alpha) prior Beta distribution treatment group. a_c positive numeric scalar giving first shape parameter (alpha) prior Beta distribution control group. b_t positive numeric scalar giving second shape parameter (beta) prior Beta distribution treatment group. b_c positive numeric scalar giving second shape parameter (beta) prior Beta distribution control group. m_t positive integer giving number patients treatment group future trial. Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set NULL. m_c positive integer giving number patients control group future trial. Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set NULL. z non-negative integer giving hypothetical number responders control group. Required design = 'uncontrolled'; otherwise set NULL. used, y_c NULL. ne_t positive integer giving number patients treatment group external data set. Required design = 'external' external treatment data available; otherwise set NULL. ne_c positive integer giving number patients control group external data set. Required design = 'external' external control data available; otherwise set NULL. ye_t non-negative integer giving number responders treatment group external data set. Required design = 'external'; otherwise set NULL. ye_c non-negative integer giving number responders control group external data set. Required design = 'external'; otherwise set NULL. alpha0e_t numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight external treatment data. Required external treatment data used; otherwise set NULL. alpha0e_c numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight external control data. Required external control data used; otherwise set NULL. lower.tail logical scalar; TRUE (default), function returns \(P(\mathrm{effect} \le \theta_0)\), otherwise \(P(\mathrm{effect} > \theta_0)\).

title: Bayesian Posterior or Posterior Predictive Probability for a Single Binary Endpoint — pbayespostpred1bin
text: numeric scalar vector [0, 1]. y_t y_c vectors length \(n\), vector length \(n\) returned.

title: Bayesian Posterior or Posterior Predictive Probability for a Single Binary Endpoint — pbayespostpred1bin
text: Posterior shape parameters computed beta-binomial conjugate model: Prior: \(\pi_j \sim \mathrm{Beta}(a_j, b_j)\). Posterior: \(\pi_j \mid y_j \sim \mathrm{Beta}(a_j + y_j, b_j + n_j - y_j)\). design = 'external', external data incorporated power prior, inflates prior weighted external sufficient statistics: $$(\alpha_j^*, \beta_j^*) = (a_j + ae_j \cdot ye_j, b_j + ae_j \cdot       (ne_j - ye_j)).$$ design = 'uncontrolled', hypothetical control value z used place y_c, n_c acts hypothetical control sample size. final probability obtained calling pbetadiff prob = 'posterior' pbetabinomdiff prob = 'predictive', applied element-wise via mapply.

title: Bayesian Posterior or Posterior Predictive Probability for a Single Binary Endpoint — pbayespostpred1bin

what: Bayesian Posterior or Posterior Predictive Probability for a Single Continuous Endpoint — pbayespostpred1cont
title: Bayesian Posterior or Posterior Predictive Probability for a Single Continuous Endpoint — pbayespostpred1cont
text: Computes Bayesian posterior probability posterior predictive probability continuous-outcome clinical trials Normal-Inverse-Chi-squared (vague Jeffreys) conjugate model. function supports controlled, uncontrolled, external designs, optional incorporation external data power priors. Vector inputs bar_y_t, s_t, bar_y_c, s_c supported efficient batch processing (e.g., across simulation replicates pbayesdecisionprob1cont).

title: Bayesian Posterior or Posterior Predictive Probability for a Single Continuous Endpoint — pbayespostpred1cont

title: Bayesian Posterior or Posterior Predictive Probability for a Single Continuous Endpoint — pbayespostpred1cont
text: prob character string specifying probability type. Must 'posterior' 'predictive'. design character string specifying trial design. Must 'controlled', 'uncontrolled', 'external'. prior character string specifying prior type. Must 'vague' (Jeffreys) 'N-Inv-Chisq' (Normal-Inverse-Chi-squared conjugate). CalcMethod character string specifying calculation method. Must 'NI' (numerical integration), 'MC' (Monte Carlo), 'MM' (Moment-Matching approximation). large nsim multi-scenario use, 'MM' strongly recommended; see Details. theta0 numeric scalar giving threshold treatment effect (difference means). nMC positive integer giving number Monte Carlo draws. Required CalcMethod = 'MC'; otherwise NULL. n_t positive integer giving number patients treatment group proof--concept (PoC) trial. n_c positive integer giving number patients control group PoC trial. Required design = 'controlled' 'external'; set NULL design = 'uncontrolled'. m_t positive integer giving number patients treatment group future trial. Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set NULL. m_c positive integer giving number patients control group future trial. Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set NULL. kappa0_t positive numeric scalar giving prior precision parameter treatment group. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq'; otherwise NULL. kappa0_c positive numeric scalar giving prior precision parameter control group. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq' design = 'controlled'; otherwise NULL. nu0_t positive numeric scalar giving prior degrees freedom treatment group. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq'; otherwise NULL. nu0_c positive numeric scalar giving prior degrees freedom control group. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq' design = 'controlled'; otherwise NULL. mu0_t numeric scalar giving prior mean treatment group. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq'; otherwise NULL. mu0_c numeric scalar giving prior mean control group (design = 'controlled' prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq') hypothetical control mean (design = 'uncontrolled'). Required uncontrolled design; otherwise NULL. sigma0_t positive numeric scalar giving prior scale treatment group. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq'; otherwise NULL. sigma0_c positive numeric scalar giving prior scale control group. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq' design = 'controlled'; otherwise NULL. bar_y_t numeric scalar vector giving sample mean treatment group. vector length nsim supplied, posterior parameters computed simultaneously replicates. bar_y_c numeric scalar vector giving sample mean control group. Required design = 'controlled' 'external'; set NULL uncontrolled design. s_t positive numeric scalar vector giving sample standard deviation treatment group. s_c positive numeric scalar vector giving sample standard deviation control group. Required design = 'controlled' 'external'; otherwise NULL. r positive numeric scalar giving variance scaling factor hypothetical control. Required design = 'uncontrolled'; otherwise NULL. hypothetical control scale \(\mathrm{sd.control} = \sqrt{r} \cdot \mathrm{sd.treatment}\). ne_t positive integer giving number patients treatment group external data set. Required design = 'external' external treatment data available; otherwise set NULL. ne_c positive integer giving number patients control group external data set. Required design = 'external' external control data available; otherwise set NULL. alpha0e_t numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight external treatment data. Required external treatment data used; otherwise set NULL. alpha0e_c numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight external control data. Required external control data used; otherwise set NULL. bar_ye_t numeric scalar giving external treatment group sample mean. Required ne_t provided; otherwise NULL. bar_ye_c numeric scalar giving external control group sample mean. Required ne_c provided; otherwise NULL. se_t positive numeric scalar giving external treatment group sample SD. Required ne_t provided; otherwise NULL. se_c positive numeric scalar giving external control group sample SD. Required ne_c provided; otherwise NULL. lower.tail logical scalar; TRUE (default), function returns \(P(\mathrm{effect} \le \theta_0)\), otherwise \(P(\mathrm{effect} > \theta_0)\).

title: Bayesian Posterior or Posterior Predictive Probability for a Single Continuous Endpoint — pbayespostpred1cont
text: numeric scalar vector [0, 1]. input data parameters (bar_y_t, etc.) vectors length \(n\), vector length \(n\) returned.

title: Bayesian Posterior or Posterior Predictive Probability for a Single Continuous Endpoint — pbayespostpred1cont
text: Normal-Inverse-Chi-squared (N-Inv-ChiSq) conjugate model, marginal posterior/predictive distribution group mean follows non-standardised t-distribution, parameterised location \(\mu_j\), scale \(\sigma_j\), degrees freedom \(\nu_j\) depend design probability type. final probability computed one three methods: NI: exact numerical integration via ptdiff_NI. MC: Monte Carlo simulation via ptdiff_MC. MM: Moment-Matching approximation via ptdiff_MM. Performance note: CalcMethod = 'NI' calls integrate() per element input vector, prohibitively slow large vectors (e.g., nsim x n_scenarios). CalcMethod = 'MC' creates nMC x n matrix internally, consuming \(O(nMC \cdot n)\) memory. large-scale simulation use CalcMethod = 'MM', fully vectorised exact normal limit.

title: Bayesian Posterior or Posterior Predictive Probability for a Single Continuous Endpoint — pbayespostpred1cont
code: # Example 1: Controlled design - posterior probability with N-Inv-Chisq pbayespostpred1cont(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled', prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq',   CalcMethod = 'NI', theta0 = 2, n_t = 12, n_c = 12,   kappa0_t = 5, kappa0_c = 5, nu0_t = 5, nu0_c = 5,   mu0_t = 5, mu0_c = 5, sigma0_t = sqrt(5), sigma0_c = sqrt(5),   bar_y_t = 2, bar_y_c = 0, s_t = 1, s_c = 1,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL, r = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = NULL, se_t = NULL, se_c = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE ) #> [1] 0.2438672  # Example 2: Uncontrolled design - posterior probability with vague prior pbayespostpred1cont(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'uncontrolled', prior = 'vague',   CalcMethod = 'MM', theta0 = 1.5, n_t = 15, n_c = NULL,   bar_y_t = 3.5, bar_y_c = NULL, s_t = 1.2, s_c = NULL,   mu0_c = 1.5, r = 1.2,   kappa0_t = NULL, kappa0_c = NULL, nu0_t = NULL, nu0_c = NULL,   mu0_t = NULL, sigma0_t = NULL, sigma0_c = NULL,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = NULL, se_t = NULL, se_c = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE ) #> [1] 0.8484152  # Example 3: External design - posterior probability pbayespostpred1cont(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'external', prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq',   CalcMethod = 'MM', theta0 = 2, n_t = 12, n_c = 12,   kappa0_t = 5, kappa0_c = 5, nu0_t = 5, nu0_c = 5,   mu0_t = 5, mu0_c = 5, sigma0_t = sqrt(5), sigma0_c = sqrt(5),   bar_y_t = 2.5, bar_y_c = 1, s_t = 1.1, s_c = 0.9,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL, r = NULL,   ne_t = 10, ne_c = 10, alpha0e_t = 0.5, alpha0e_c = 0.5,   bar_ye_t = 2, bar_ye_c = 0.5, se_t = 1, se_c = 0.8,   lower.tail = FALSE ) #> [1] 0.08934116  # Example 4: Controlled design - posterior predictive probability pbayespostpred1cont(   prob = 'predictive', design = 'controlled', prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq',   CalcMethod = 'MM', theta0 = 1, n_t = 12, n_c = 12,   kappa0_t = 5, kappa0_c = 5, nu0_t = 5, nu0_c = 5,   mu0_t = 5, mu0_c = 5, sigma0_t = sqrt(5), sigma0_c = sqrt(5),   bar_y_t = 2, bar_y_c = 0, s_t = 1, s_c = 1,   m_t = 20, m_c = 20, r = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = NULL, se_t = NULL, se_c = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE ) #> [1] 0.6961224  # Example 5: Uncontrolled design - posterior predictive probability pbayespostpred1cont(   prob = 'predictive', design = 'uncontrolled', prior = 'vague',   CalcMethod = 'MM', theta0 = 1.5, n_t = 15, n_c = NULL,   bar_y_t = 3.5, bar_y_c = NULL, s_t = 1.2, s_c = NULL,   mu0_c = 1.5, r = 1.2,   kappa0_t = NULL, kappa0_c = NULL, nu0_t = NULL, nu0_c = NULL,   mu0_t = NULL, sigma0_t = NULL, sigma0_c = NULL,   m_t = 20, m_c = 20,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = NULL, se_t = NULL, se_c = NULL,   lower.tail = FALSE ) #> [1] 0.8745171  # Example 6: External design - posterior predictive probability pbayespostpred1cont(   prob = 'predictive', design = 'external', prior = 'N-Inv-Chisq',   CalcMethod = 'MM', theta0 = 1, n_t = 12, n_c = 12,   kappa0_t = 5, kappa0_c = 5, nu0_t = 5, nu0_c = 5,   mu0_t = 5, mu0_c = 5, sigma0_t = sqrt(5), sigma0_c = sqrt(5),   bar_y_t = 2.5, bar_y_c = 1, s_t = 1.1, s_c = 0.9,   m_t = 20, m_c = 20, r = NULL,   ne_t = 10, ne_c = 10, alpha0e_t = 0.5, alpha0e_c = 0.5,   bar_ye_t = 2, bar_ye_c = 0.5, se_t = 1, se_c = 0.8,   lower.tail = FALSE ) #> [1] 0.594831

what: Bayesian Posterior or Posterior Predictive Probability for Two Binary Endpoints — pbayespostpred2bin
title: Bayesian Posterior or Posterior Predictive Probability for Two Binary Endpoints — pbayespostpred2bin
text: Computes Bayesian posterior probability posterior predictive probability clinical trials two binary endpoints Dirichlet-multinomial conjugate model. function returns probabilities nine decision regions (posterior) four decision regions (predictive) defined target values (TV) minimum acceptable values (MAV) endpoints. Three study designs supported: controlled, uncontrolled (hypothetical control), external (power prior).

title: Bayesian Posterior or Posterior Predictive Probability for Two Binary Endpoints — pbayespostpred2bin
code: pbayespostpred2bin(   prob = "posterior",   design = "controlled",   theta_TV1 = NULL,   theta_MAV1 = NULL,   theta_TV2 = NULL,   theta_MAV2 = NULL,   theta_NULL1 = NULL,   theta_NULL2 = NULL,   x_t_00,   x_t_01,   x_t_10,   x_t_11,   x_c_00 = NULL,   x_c_01 = NULL,   x_c_10 = NULL,   x_c_11 = NULL,   a_t_00,   a_t_01,   a_t_10,   a_t_11,   a_c_00,   a_c_01,   a_c_10,   a_c_11,   m_t = NULL,   m_c = NULL,   z00 = NULL,   z01 = NULL,   z10 = NULL,   z11 = NULL,   xe_t_00 = NULL,   xe_t_01 = NULL,   xe_t_10 = NULL,   xe_t_11 = NULL,   xe_c_00 = NULL,   xe_c_01 = NULL,   xe_c_10 = NULL,   xe_c_11 = NULL,   alpha0e_t = NULL,   alpha0e_c = NULL,   nMC = 10000L )

title: Bayesian Posterior or Posterior Predictive Probability for Two Binary Endpoints — pbayespostpred2bin
text: prob character string specifying probability type. Must 'posterior' 'predictive'. design character string specifying trial design. Must 'controlled', 'uncontrolled', 'external'. theta_TV1 numeric scalar giving target value (TV) threshold Endpoint 1. Required prob = 'posterior'; must satisfy theta_TV1 > theta_MAV1. Set NULL prob = 'predictive'. theta_MAV1 numeric scalar giving minimum acceptable value (MAV) threshold Endpoint 1. Required prob = 'posterior'; must satisfy theta_TV1 > theta_MAV1. Set NULL prob = 'predictive'. theta_TV2 numeric scalar giving target value (TV) threshold Endpoint 2. Required prob = 'posterior'; must satisfy theta_TV2 > theta_MAV2. Set NULL prob = 'predictive'. theta_MAV2 numeric scalar giving minimum acceptable value (MAV) threshold Endpoint 2. Required prob = 'posterior'; must satisfy theta_TV2 > theta_MAV2. Set NULL prob = 'predictive'. theta_NULL1 numeric scalar giving null hypothesis threshold Endpoint 1. Required prob = 'predictive'; set NULL prob = 'posterior'. theta_NULL2 numeric scalar giving null hypothesis threshold Endpoint 2. Required prob = 'predictive'; set NULL prob = 'posterior'. x_t_00 non-negative integer giving count (0, 0) responses treatment group (Endpoint 1 = 0, Endpoint 2 = 0). x_t_01 non-negative integer giving count (0, 1) responses treatment group. x_t_10 non-negative integer giving count (1, 0) responses treatment group. x_t_11 non-negative integer giving count (1, 1) responses treatment group. x_c_00 non-negative integer giving count (0, 0) responses control group. used design = 'uncontrolled'; set NULL case. x_c_01 non-negative integer giving count (0, 1) responses control group. used design = 'uncontrolled'; set NULL case. x_c_10 non-negative integer giving count (1, 0) responses control group. used design = 'uncontrolled'; set NULL case. x_c_11 non-negative integer giving count (1, 1) responses control group. used design = 'uncontrolled'; set NULL case. a_t_00 positive numeric scalar giving Dirichlet prior parameter (0, 0) response pattern treatment group. a_t_01 positive numeric scalar giving Dirichlet prior parameter (0, 1) response pattern treatment group. a_t_10 positive numeric scalar giving Dirichlet prior parameter (1, 0) response pattern treatment group. a_t_11 positive numeric scalar giving Dirichlet prior parameter (1, 1) response pattern treatment group. a_c_00 positive numeric scalar giving Dirichlet prior parameter (0, 0) response pattern control group. design = 'uncontrolled', serves hyperparameter hypothetical control distribution. a_c_01 positive numeric scalar giving Dirichlet prior parameter (0, 1) response pattern control group. design = 'uncontrolled', serves hyperparameter hypothetical control distribution. a_c_10 positive numeric scalar giving Dirichlet prior parameter (1, 0) response pattern control group. design = 'uncontrolled', serves hyperparameter hypothetical control distribution. a_c_11 positive numeric scalar giving Dirichlet prior parameter (1, 1) response pattern control group. design = 'uncontrolled', serves hyperparameter hypothetical control distribution. m_t positive integer giving number patients treatment group future trial. Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set NULL. m_c positive integer giving number patients control group future trial. Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set NULL. z00 non-negative integer giving hypothetical control count pattern (0, 0). Required design = 'uncontrolled'; otherwise set NULL. z01 non-negative integer giving hypothetical control count pattern (0, 1). Required design = 'uncontrolled'; otherwise set NULL. z10 non-negative integer giving hypothetical control count pattern (1, 0). Required design = 'uncontrolled'; otherwise set NULL. z11 non-negative integer giving hypothetical control count pattern (1, 1). Required design = 'uncontrolled'; otherwise set NULL. xe_t_00 non-negative integer giving external treatment group count pattern (0, 0). Required design = 'external' external treatment data used; otherwise NULL. xe_t_01 non-negative integer giving external treatment group count pattern (0, 1). Required external treatment data; otherwise NULL. xe_t_10 non-negative integer giving external treatment group count pattern (1, 0). Required external treatment data; otherwise NULL. xe_t_11 non-negative integer giving external treatment group count pattern (1, 1). Required external treatment data; otherwise NULL. xe_c_00 non-negative integer giving external control group count pattern (0, 0). Required external control data; otherwise NULL. xe_c_10 non-negative integer giving external control group count pattern (1, 0). Required external control data; otherwise NULL. xe_c_11 non-negative integer giving external control group count pattern (1, 1). Required external control data; otherwise NULL. alpha0e_t numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight external treatment data. Required external treatment data used; otherwise set NULL. alpha0e_c numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight external control data. Required external control data used; otherwise set NULL. nMC positive integer giving number Monte Carlo draws used estimate region probabilities. Default 10000. title: Bayesian Posterior or Posterior Predictive Probability for Two Binary Endpoints — pbayespostpred2bin
text: named numeric vector region probabilities. prob = 'posterior': length 9, named R1, ..., R9, corresponding regions defined TV MAV thresholds endpoints (row-major order: Endpoint 1 varies slowest). prob = 'predictive': length 4, named R1, ..., R4. elements non-negative sum 1.

title: Bayesian Posterior or Posterior Predictive Probability for Two Binary Endpoints — pbayespostpred2bin
text: Model. four response categories ordered (0, 0), (0, 1), (1, 0), (1, 1). group \(j\), observed count vector follows multinomial distribution, conjugate Dirichlet prior placed cell probability vector \(p_j\): $$p_j \sim \mathrm{Dir}(\alpha_{j,00}, \alpha_{j,01}, \alpha_{j,10},       \alpha_{j,11}).$$ posterior $$p_j \mid x_j \sim \mathrm{Dir}(\alpha_{j,00} + x_{j,00},       \alpha_{j,01} + x_{j,01}, \alpha_{j,10} + x_{j,10},       \alpha_{j,11} + x_{j,11}).$$ Marginal response rates \(\pi_{j1} = p_{j,10} + p_{j,11}\) (Endpoint 1) \(\pi_{j2} = p_{j,01} + p_{j,11}\) (Endpoint 2). Treatment effects \(\theta_1 = \pi_{t1} - \pi_{c1}\) \(\theta_2 = \pi_{t2} - \pi_{c2}\). Uncontrolled design. design = 'uncontrolled', hypothetical control distribution specified Dirichlet distribution directly via prior hyperparameters hypothetical control counts z00, z01, z10, z11. External design. design = 'external', power prior augments Dirichlet prior parameters.

title: Bayesian Posterior or Posterior Predictive Probability for Two Binary Endpoints — pbayespostpred2bin
code: # Example 1: Controlled design - posterior probability pbayespostpred2bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled',   theta_TV1 = 0.15, theta_MAV1 = 0.05, theta_TV2 = 0.10, theta_MAV2 = 0.0,   theta_NULL1 = NULL, theta_NULL2 = NULL,   x_t_00 = 2, x_t_01 = 3, x_t_10 = 5, x_t_11 = 2,   x_c_00 = 3, x_c_01 = 2, x_c_10 = 4, x_c_11 = 1,   a_t_00 = 1, a_t_01 = 1, a_t_10 = 1, a_t_11 = 1,   a_c_00 = 1, a_c_01 = 1, a_c_10 = 1, a_c_11 = 1,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   z00 = NULL, z01 = NULL, z10 = NULL, z11 = NULL,   xe_t_00 = NULL, xe_t_01 = NULL, xe_t_10 = NULL, xe_t_11 = NULL,   xe_c_00 = NULL, xe_c_01 = NULL, xe_c_10 = NULL, xe_c_11 = NULL,   alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL ) #>     R1     R2     R3     R4     R5     R6     R7     R8     R9  #> 0.1191 0.0696 0.1193 0.0961 0.0464 0.0742 0.2475 0.1058 0.1220   # Example 2: Uncontrolled design - posterior probability pbayespostpred2bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'uncontrolled',   theta_TV1 = 0.15, theta_MAV1 = 0.05, theta_TV2 = 0.10, theta_MAV2 = 0.0,   theta_NULL1 = NULL, theta_NULL2 = NULL,   x_t_00 = 2, x_t_01 = 3, x_t_10 = 5, x_t_11 = 2,   x_c_00 = NULL, x_c_01 = NULL, x_c_10 = NULL, x_c_11 = NULL,   a_t_00 = 1, a_t_01 = 1, a_t_10 = 1, a_t_11 = 1,   a_c_00 = 1, a_c_01 = 1, a_c_10 = 1, a_c_11 = 1,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   z00 = 1, z01 = 2, z10 = 2, z11 = 1,   xe_t_00 = NULL, xe_t_01 = NULL, xe_t_10 = NULL, xe_t_11 = NULL,   xe_c_00 = NULL, xe_c_01 = NULL, xe_c_10 = NULL, xe_c_11 = NULL,   alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL ) #>     R1     R2     R3     R4     R5     R6     R7     R8     R9  #> 0.0480 0.0438 0.2316 0.0360 0.0367 0.1286 0.1218 0.0875 0.2660   # Example 3: External design - posterior probability pbayespostpred2bin(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'external',   theta_TV1 = 0.15, theta_MAV1 = 0.05, theta_TV2 = 0.10, theta_MAV2 = 0.0,   theta_NULL1 = NULL, theta_NULL2 = NULL,   x_t_00 = 2, x_t_01 = 3, x_t_10 = 5, x_t_11 = 2,   x_c_00 = 3, x_c_01 = 2, x_c_10 = 4, x_c_11 = 1,   a_t_00 = 1, a_t_01 = 1, a_t_10 = 1, a_t_11 = 1,   a_c_00 = 1, a_c_01 = 1, a_c_10 = 1, a_c_11 = 1,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   z00 = NULL, z01 = NULL, z10 = NULL, z11 = NULL,   xe_t_00 = 2, xe_t_01 = 2, xe_t_10 = 3, xe_t_11 = 1,   xe_c_00 = 2, xe_c_01 = 1, xe_c_10 = 2, xe_c_11 = 1,   alpha0e_t = 0.5, alpha0e_c = 0.5 ) #>     R1     R2     R3     R4     R5     R6     R7     R8     R9  #> 0.0891 0.0668 0.1052 0.1026 0.0597 0.0793 0.2449 0.1195 0.1329   # Example 4: Controlled design - title: Region Probabilities for Two Continuous Endpoints — pbayespostpred2cont
text: Computes posterior posterior predictive probability bivariate treatment effect \(\theta = \mu_t - \mu_c\) falls 9 (posterior) 4 (predictive) rectangular regions defined decision thresholds.

title: Region Probabilities for Two Continuous Endpoints — pbayespostpred2cont
code: pbayespostpred2cont(   prob,   design,   prior,   theta_TV1 = NULL,   theta_MAV1 = NULL,   theta_TV2 = NULL,   theta_MAV2 = NULL,   theta_NULL1 = NULL,   theta_NULL2 = NULL,   n_t,   n_c = NULL,   ybar_t,   S_t,   ybar_c = NULL,   S_c = NULL,   m_t = NULL,   m_c = NULL,   kappa0_t = NULL,   nu0_t = NULL,   mu0_t = NULL,   Lambda0_t = NULL,   kappa0_c = NULL,   nu0_c = NULL,   mu0_c = NULL,   Lambda0_c = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne_t = NULL,   ne_c = NULL,   alpha0e_t = NULL,   alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL,   bar_ye_c = NULL,   se_t = NULL,   se_c = NULL,   nMC = 10000L,   CalcMethod = "MC" )

title: Region Probabilities for Two Continuous Endpoints — pbayespostpred2cont
text:
prob character string specifying probability type. Must 'posterior' 'predictive'. design character string specifying trial design. Must 'controlled', 'uncontrolled', 'external'. prior character string specifying prior distribution. Must 'vague' 'N-Inv-Wishart'. theta_TV1 numeric scalar giving target value (TV) threshold Endpoint 1. Required prob = 'posterior'; must satisfy theta_TV1 > theta_MAV1. Set NULL prob = 'predictive'. theta_MAV1 numeric scalar giving minimum acceptable value (MAV) threshold Endpoint 1. Required prob = 'posterior'; must satisfy theta_TV1 > theta_MAV1. Set NULL prob = 'predictive'. theta_TV2 numeric scalar giving target value (TV) threshold Endpoint 2. Required prob = 'posterior'; must satisfy theta_TV2 > theta_MAV2. Set NULL prob = 'predictive'. theta_MAV2 numeric scalar giving minimum acceptable value (MAV) threshold Endpoint 2. Required prob = 'posterior'; must satisfy theta_TV2 > theta_MAV2. Set NULL prob = 'predictive'. theta_NULL1 numeric scalar giving null hypothesis threshold Endpoint 1. Required prob = 'predictive'; set NULL prob = 'posterior'. theta_NULL2 numeric scalar giving null hypothesis threshold Endpoint 2. Required prob = 'predictive'; set NULL prob = 'posterior'. n_t positive integer giving number patients treatment group proof--concept (PoC) trial. n_c positive integer giving number patients control group PoC trial. design = 'uncontrolled', hypothetical control sample size (required consistency designs). ybar_t numeric vector length 2 numeric matrix 2 columns giving sample mean(s) treatment group. matrix \(N\) rows supplied, function computes probabilities \(N\) observations simultaneously returns \(N 	imes n_{m regions}\) matrix. S_t 2x2 numeric matrix giving sum--squares matrix treatment group (single observation), list \(N\) matrices (vectorised call). Must consistent ybar_t: ybar_t matrix, S_t must list length. ybar_c numeric vector length 2, numeric matrix 2 columns, NULL giving sample mean(s) control group. used design = 'uncontrolled'. S_c 2x2 numeric matrix, list 2x2 matrices, NULL giving sum--squares matrix/matrices control group. used design = 'uncontrolled'. m_t positive integer giving number patients treatment group future trial. Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set NULL. m_c positive integer giving number patients control group future trial. Required prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set NULL. kappa0_t positive numeric scalar giving NIW prior concentration parameter treatment group. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart'; otherwise set NULL. nu0_t numeric scalar giving NIW prior degrees freedom treatment group. Must greater 3. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart'; otherwise set NULL. mu0_t numeric vector length 2 giving NIW prior mean treatment group. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart'; otherwise set NULL. Lambda0_t 2x2 positive-definite numeric matrix giving NIW prior scale matrix treatment group. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart'; otherwise set NULL. kappa0_c positive numeric scalar giving NIW prior concentration parameter control group. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart' design != 'uncontrolled'; otherwise set NULL. nu0_c numeric scalar giving NIW prior degrees freedom control group. Must greater 3. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart' design != 'uncontrolled'; otherwise set NULL. mu0_c numeric vector length 2 giving NIW prior mean control group, hypothetical control location design = 'uncontrolled'. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart'; otherwise set NULL. Lambda0_c 2x2 positive-definite numeric matrix giving NIW prior scale matrix control group. Required prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart' design != 'uncontrolled'; otherwise set NULL. r positive numeric scalar giving variance scaling factor hypothetical control distribution. Required design = 'uncontrolled'; otherwise set NULL. ne_t positive integer giving external treatment group sample size. Required design = 'external' external treatment data used; otherwise set NULL. ne_c positive integer giving external control group sample size. Required design = 'external' external control data used; otherwise set NULL. alpha0e_t numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight external treatment data. Required external treatment data used; otherwise set NULL. alpha0e_c numeric scalar (0, 1] giving power prior weight external control data. Required external control data used; otherwise set NULL. bar_ye_t numeric vector length 2 giving external treatment group sample mean. Required external treatment data used; otherwise set NULL. bar_ye_c numeric vector length 2 giving external control group sample mean. Required external control data used; otherwise set NULL. se_t 2x2 numeric matrix giving external treatment group sum--squares matrix. Required external treatment data used; otherwise set NULL. se_c 2x2 numeric matrix giving external control group sum--squares matrix. Required external control data used; otherwise set NULL. nMC positive integer giving number Monte Carlo draws used estimate region probabilities. Default 10000. Required CalcMethod = 'MC'. May set NULL CalcMethod = 'MM' \(
u_k > 4\) (MM method uses mvtnorm::pmvt analytically); CalcMethod = 'MM' \(
u_k \le 4\) causes fallback MC, nMC must positive integer. CalcMethod character string specifying computation method. Must 'MC' (Monte Carlo, default) 'MM' (Moment-Matching via mvtnorm::pmvt). CalcMethod = 'MM' \(
u_k \le 4\), warning issued function falls back CalcMethod = 'MC'.

title: Region Probabilities for Two Continuous Endpoints — pbayespostpred2cont
text: ybar_t length-2 vector (single observation): named numeric vector length 9 (R1–R9) prob = 'posterior' length 4 (R1–R4) prob = 'predictive'. elements non-negative sum 1.
code: When \code{ybar_t} is an \eqn{N \times 2} matrix (vectorised call):     a numeric matrix with \eqn{N} rows and 9 (or 4) columns, with     column names \code{R1}--\code{R9} (or \code{R1}--\code{R4}). Each     row sums to 1.

title: Region Probabilities for Two Continuous Endpoints — pbayespostpred2cont
text:
Model. endpoints follow bivariate Normal distribution \(y_{k,j} \sim N_2(\mu_k, \Sigma_k)\) group \(k \{t, c\}\). treatment effect \(	heta = \mu_t - \mu_c\). Posterior distribution (vague prior). $$\mu_k | Y_k \sim t_{n_k - 2}\!\left( ar{y}_k,\;        rac{S_k}{n_k(n_k - 2)}ight)$$ Posterior distribution (NIW prior). $$\mu_k | Y_k \sim t_{
u_{nk} - 1}\!\left(\mu_{nk},\;        rac{\Lambda_{nk}}{\kappa_{nk}(
u_{nk} - 1)}ight)$$ updated hyperparameters \(\kappa_{nk} = \kappa_{0k} + n_k\), \(
u_{nk} = 
u_{0k} + n_k\), \(\mu_{nk} = (\kappa_{0k}\mu_{0k} + n_k ar{y}_k)/\kappa_{nk}\), \(\Lambda_{nk} = \Lambda_{0k} + S_k + \kappa_{0k}n_k( ar{y}_k - \mu_{0k})( ar{y}_k - \mu_{0k})^T / \kappa_{nk}\). Predictive distribution. scale matrix single future observation inflated \((1 + n_k)/n_k\) (vague) \((1 + \kappa_{nk})/\kappa_{nk}\) (NIW) relative posterior. mean \(m_k\) future observations scale divided \(m_k\). Posterior probability regions (prob = 'posterior'). Row-major 3x3 grid; Endpoint 1 varies slowest: R1: \(	heta_1 > TV_1\) \(	heta_2 > TV_2\) R2: \(	heta_1 > TV_1\) \(TV_2 \ge 	heta_2 > MAV_2\) R3: \(	heta_1 > TV_1\) \(	heta_2 \le MAV_2\) R4: \(TV_1 \ge 	heta_1 > MAV_1\) \(	heta_2 > TV_2\) R5: \(TV_1 \ge 	heta_1 > MAV_1\) \(TV_2 \ge 	heta_2 > MAV_2\) R6: \(TV_1 \ge 	heta_1 > MAV_1\) \(	heta_2 \le MAV_2\) R7: \(	heta_1 \le MAV_1\) \(	heta_2 > TV_2\) R8: \(	heta_1 \le MAV_1\) \(TV_2 \ge 	heta_2 > MAV_2\) R9: \(	heta_1 \le MAV_1\) \(	heta_2 \le MAV_2\) Predictive probability regions (prob = 'predictive'). Row-major 2x2 grid; Endpoint 1 varies slowest: R1: \(	ilde	heta_1 > 	heta_{m NULL1}\) \(	ilde	heta_2 > 	heta_{m NULL2}\) R2: \(	ilde	heta_1 > 	heta_{m NULL1}\) \(	ilde	heta_2 \le 	heta_{m NULL2}\) R3: \(	ilde	heta_1 \le 	heta_{m NULL1}\) \(	ilde	heta_2 > 	heta_{m NULL2}\) R4: \(	ilde	heta_1 \le 	heta_{m NULL1}\) \(	ilde	heta_2 \le 	heta_{m NULL2}\) Uncontrolled design. NIW prior: \(\mu_c \sim t_{
u_{nt} - 1}(\mu_{0c},\; r\Lambda_{nt} / [\kappa_{nt}(
u_{nt} - 1)])\). parameter r allows variance scale hypothetical control differ treatment group. External design. Incorporated via power prior NIW conjugate representation. effective posterior hyperparameters obtained constructing power prior external data weight \(a_0\), updating current PoC data (see Conjugate_power_prior.pdf, Theorem 5). Vectorised usage. ybar_t supplied \(N 	imes 2\) matrix S_t list \(N\) scatter matrices, function computes region probabilities \(N\) observations single call, returning \(N 	imes n_{m regions}\) matrix.  CalcMethod = 'MC', standard normal chi-squared variates pre-generated (size nMC) reused across observations, Cholesky factor replicate-specific scale matrix recomputed per observation.  CalcMethod = 'MM', moment-matching parameters computed per observation (since depend replicate-specific \(V_k\)) mvtnorm::pmvt called per region per observation.

posterior probability, NIW prior S_t  <- matrix(c(18.0, 3.6, 3.6, 9.0), 2, 2) S_c  <- matrix(c(16.0, 2.8, 2.8, 8.5), 2, 2) L0   <- matrix(c(20.0, 0.0, 0.0, 10.0), 2, 2) se_c <- matrix(c(15.0, 2.5, 2.5, 7.5), 2, 2) pbayespostpred2cont(   prob = 'posterior', design = 'external', prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart',   theta_TV1 = 1.5, theta_MAV1 = 0.5,   theta_TV2 = 1.0, theta_MAV2 = 0.3,   theta_NULL1 = NULL, theta_NULL2 = NULL,   n_t = 12L, n_c = 12L,   ybar_t = c(3.5, 2.1), S_t = S_t,   ybar_c = c(1.8, 1.0), S_c = S_c,   m_t = NULL, m_c = NULL,   kappa0_t = 2.0, nu0_t = 5.0, mu0_t = c(2.0, 1.0), Lambda0_t = L0,   kappa0_c = 2.0, nu0_c = 5.0, mu0_c = c(1.0, 0.5), Lambda0_c = L0,   r = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = 10L, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = 0.5,   bar_ye_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = c(1.5, 0.8), se_t = NULL, se_c = se_c,   nMC = 1000L ) #>    R1    R2    R3    R4    R5    R6    R7    R8    R9  #> 0.352 0.231 0.023 0.169 0.189 0.015 0.006 0.012 0.003   # Example 4: Controlled design - posterior predictive probability, vague prior S_t <- matrix(c(18.0, 3.6, 3.6, 9.0), 2, 2) S_c <- matrix(c(16.0, 2.8, 2.8, 8.5), 2, 2) pbayespostpred2cont(   prob = 'predictive', design = 'controlled', prior = 'vague',   theta_TV1 = NULL, theta_MAV1 = NULL,   theta_TV2 = NULL, theta_MAV2 = NULL,   theta_NULL1 = 0.5, theta_NULL2 = 0.3,   n_t = 12L, n_c = 12L,   ybar_t = c(3.5, 2.1), S_t = S_t,   ybar_c = c(1.8, 1.0), S_c = S_c,   m_t = 30L, m_c = 30L,   kappa0_t = NULL, nu0_t = NULL, mu0_t = NULL, Lambda0_t = NULL,   kappa0_c = NULL, nu0_c = NULL, mu0_c = NULL, Lambda0_c = NULL,   r = NULL,   ne_t = NULL, ne_c = NULL, alpha0e_t = NULL, alpha0e_c = NULL,   bar_ye_t = NULL, bar_ye_c = NULL, se_t = NULL, se_c = NULL,   nMC = 1000L ) #>    R1    R2    R3    R4  #> 0.991 0.005 0.003 0.001   # Example 5: Uncontrolled design - what: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference Between Two Independent Beta-Binomial Proportions — pbetabinomdiff
title: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference Between Two Independent Beta-Binomial Proportions — pbetabinomdiff
text: Calculates cumulative distribution function (CDF) difference two independent Beta-Binomial proportions exact enumeration. Specifically, computes \(P((Y_t / m_t) - (Y_c / m_c) \le q)\) \(P((Y_t / m_t) - (Y_c / m_c) > q)\), \(Y_j \sim \mathrm{BetaBinomial}(m_j, \alpha_j, \beta_j)\) \(j \\{t, c\}\).

title: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference Between Two Independent Beta-Binomial Proportions — pbetabinomdiff
code: pbetabinomdiff(   q,   m_t,   m_c,   alpha_t,   alpha_c,   beta_t,   beta_c,   lower.tail = TRUE )

title: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference Between Two Independent Beta-Binomial Proportions — pbetabinomdiff
text: q numeric scalar representing quantile threshold difference proportions. m_t positive integer giving number future patients treatment group. m_c positive integer giving number future patients control group. alpha_t positive numeric scalar giving first shape parameter Beta mixing distribution treatment group. alpha_c positive numeric scalar giving first shape parameter Beta mixing distribution control group. beta_t positive numeric scalar giving second shape parameter Beta mixing distribution treatment group. beta_c positive numeric scalar giving second shape parameter Beta mixing distribution control group. lower.tail logical scalar; TRUE (default), function returns \(P((Y_t / m_t) - (Y_c / m_c) \le q)\), otherwise \(P((Y_t / m_t) - (Y_c / m_c) > q)\).

title: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference Between Two Independent Beta-Binomial Proportions — pbetabinomdiff
text: numeric scalar [0, 1].

title: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference Between Two Independent Beta-Binomial Proportions — pbetabinomdiff
text: probability mass function \(Y_j \sim \mathrm{BetaBinomial}(m_j, \alpha_j, \beta_j)\) : $$P(Y_j = k) = \binom{m_j}{k}   \frac{B(k + \alpha_j,\; m_j - k + \beta_j)}{B(\alpha_j, \beta_j)},   \quad k = 0, \ldots, m_j$$ \(B(\cdot, \cdot)\) Beta function. exact CDF obtained enumerating \((m_t + 1)(m_c + 1)\) outcome combinations summing joint probabilities proportion difference satisfies specified condition. Computation time therefore grows quadratically \(m_t\) \(m_c\); large future sample sizes consider normal approximation. Beta-Binomial distribution arises success probability Binomial model follows Beta prior, making appropriate posterior predictive calculations Bayesian binary-endpoint trials.

title: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference Between Two Independent Beta-Binomial Proportions — pbetabinomdiff
code: # P((Y_t/12) - (Y_c/12) > 0.2) with symmetric Beta(0.5, 0.5) priors pbetabinomdiff(0.2, 12, 12, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, lower.tail = FALSE) #> [1] 0.3372581  # P((Y_t/20) - (Y_c/15) > 0.1) with different future sample sizes pbetabinomdiff(0.1, 20, 15, 1, 1, 1, 1, lower.tail = FALSE) #> [1] 0.4017857  # P((Y_t/10) - (Y_c/10) > 0) with informative priors pbetabinomdiff(0, 10, 10, 2, 3, 3, 2, lower.tail = FALSE) #> [1] 0.2383331  # Lower tail: P((Y_t/15) - (Y_c/15) <= 0.05) with vague priors pbetabinomdiff(0.05, 15, 15, 1, 1, 1, 1, lower.tail = TRUE) #> [1] 0.53125

what: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference Between Two Independent Beta Variables — pbetadiff
title: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference Between Two Independent Beta Variables — pbetadiff
text: Calculates cumulative distribution function (CDF) difference two independent Beta-distributed random variables. Specifically, computes \(P(\pi_t - \pi_c \le q)\) \(P(\pi_t - \pi_c > q)\), \(\pi_j \sim \mathrm{Beta}(\alpha_j, \beta_j)\) \(j \\{t, c\}\).

title: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference Between Two Independent Beta Variables — pbetadiff
code: pbetadiff(q, alpha_t, alpha_c, beta_t, beta_c, lower.tail = TRUE)

title: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference Between Two Independent Beta Variables — pbetadiff
text: q numeric scalar [-1, 1] representing quantile threshold difference proportions. alpha_t positive numeric scalar giving first shape parameter Beta distribution treatment group. alpha_c positive numeric scalar giving first shape parameter Beta distribution control group. beta_t positive numeric scalar giving second shape parameter Beta distribution treatment group. beta_c positive numeric scalar giving second shape parameter Beta distribution control group. lower.tail logical scalar; TRUE (default), function returns \(P(\pi_t - \pi_c \le q)\), otherwise \(P(\pi_t - \pi_c > q)\).

title: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference Between Two Independent Beta Variables — pbetadiff
text: numeric scalar [0, 1].

title: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference Between Two Independent Beta Variables — pbetadiff
text: upper-tail probability obtained via convolution formula: $$P(\pi_t - \pi_c > q)   = \int_0^1 F_{\mathrm{Beta}(\alpha_c, \beta_c)}(x - q)\,              f_{\mathrm{Beta}(\alpha_t, \beta_t)}(x)\, dx$$ \(f_{\mathrm{Beta}(\alpha_t, \beta_t)}\) density \(\pi_t\) \(F_{\mathrm{Beta}(\alpha_c, \beta_c)}\) CDF \(\pi_c\). Boundary cases handled automatically pbeta (\(0\) \(x - q \le 0\), \(1\) \(x - q \ge 1\)), integrating [0, 1] safe. single-integral convolution replaces equivalent double-integral formulation based Appell's F1 hypergeometric function, yielding result lower computational cost pbeta dbeta implemented compiled C code.

title: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference Between Two Independent Beta Variables — pbetadiff
code: # P(pi_t - pi_c > 0.2) with symmetric Beta(0.5, 0.5) priors pbetadiff(0.2, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, lower.tail = FALSE) #> [1] 0.3377403  # P(pi_t - pi_c > -0.1) with informative priors pbetadiff(-0.1, 2, 1, 3, 4, lower.tail = FALSE) #> [1] 0.8783  # P(pi_t - pi_c > 0) with equal priors -- should be approximately 0.5 pbetadiff(0, 1, 1, 1, 1, lower.tail = FALSE) #> [1] 0.5  # Lower tail: P(pi_t - pi_c <= 0.1) with symmetric priors pbetadiff(0.1, 2, 2, 2, 2, lower.tail = TRUE) #> [1] 0.6181518

what: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference of Two Independent t-Distributed Variables via Monte Carlo Simulation — ptdiff_MC
title: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference of Two Independent t-Distributed Variables via Monte Carlo Simulation — ptdiff_MC
text: Calculates cumulative distribution function (CDF) difference two independent non-standardised t-distributed random variables using Monte Carlo simulation. Specifically, computes \(P(T_t - T_c \le q)\) \(P(T_t - T_c > q)\), \(T_k \sim t(\mu_k, \sigma_k^2, \nu_k)\) \(k \\{t, c\}\).

title: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference of Two Independent t-Distributed Variables via Monte Carlo Simulation — ptdiff_MC
code: ptdiff_MC(nMC, q, mu_t, mu_c, sd_t, sd_c, nu_t, nu_c, lower.tail = TRUE)

title: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference of Two Independent t-Distributed Variables via Monte Carlo Simulation — ptdiff_MC
text: nMC positive integer giving number Monte Carlo draws. Typical values range 10,000 (quick estimates) 100,000 (high precision). Larger values reduce Monte Carlo error cost computation time. q numeric scalar representing quantile threshold. mu_t numeric scalar vector giving location parameter t-distribution treatment group. mu_c numeric scalar vector giving location parameter t-distribution control group. sd_t positive numeric scalar vector giving scale parameter t-distribution treatment group. sd_c positive numeric scalar vector giving scale parameter t-distribution control group. nu_t numeric scalar giving degrees freedom t-distribution treatment group. Must greater 2 finite variance. nu_c numeric scalar giving degrees freedom t-distribution control group. Must greater 2 finite variance. lower.tail logical scalar; TRUE (default), function returns \(P(T_t - T_c \le q)\), otherwise \(P(T_t - T_c > q)\).

title: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference of Two Independent t-Distributed Variables via Monte Carlo Simulation — ptdiff_MC
text: numeric scalar vector [0, 1].  mu_t, mu_c, sd_t, sd_c vectors length \(n\), vector length \(n\) returned.

title: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference of Two Independent t-Distributed Variables via Monte Carlo Simulation — ptdiff_MC
text: algorithm proceeds follows: Generate nMC x n matrix standard t draws \(T_t\) (\(\nu_t\) degrees freedom), scale shift column sd_t[] mu_t[]. Repeat \(T_c\) \(\nu_c\) degrees freedom. Compute nMC x n difference matrix \(D = T_t - T_c\). Return colMeans(D > q) estimated \(P(T_t - T_c > q)\) parameter set. operations matrix-based (R-level loop parameter sets), performance scales well \(n\). However, note matrix size nMC x n, memory usage grows linearly nMC \(n\). \(n\) large (e.g., nsim x n_scenarios pbayesdecisionprob1cont), memory requirements can become prohibitive; cases prefer CalcMethod = 'MM'. Monte Carlo error approximately \(\sqrt{p(1 - p) / \mathrm{nMC}}\); near \(p = 0.5\) roughly \(0.5 / \sqrt{\mathrm{nMC}}\).

title: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference of Two Independent t-Distributed Variables via Monte Carlo Simulation — ptdiff_MC

text: Calculates cumulative distribution function (CDF) difference two independent non-standardised t-distributed random variables using Moment-Matching approximation. Specifically, computes \(P(T_t - T_c \le q)\) \(P(T_t - T_c > q)\), \(T_k \sim t(\mu_k, \sigma_k^2, \nu_k)\) \(k \\{t, c\}\).

title: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference of Two Independent t-Distributed Variables via Moment-Matching Approximation — ptdiff_MM
text:
difference \(D = T_t - T_c\) approximated single non-standardised t-distribution \(t(\mu^*, {\sigma^*}^2, 
u^*)\) whose parameters determined matching first two even moments \(D\): \(\mu^* = \mu_t - \mu_c\). \({\sigma^*}^2\) obtained second-moment equation. \(
u^*\) obtained fourth-moment equation. approximation requires \(
u_t > 4\) \(
u_c > 4\) finite fourth moments. exact normal limit (\(
u \infty\)) works well practice \(
u > 10\). reduces single call pt(), orders magnitude faster numerical integration method (ptdiff_NI) fully vectorised.

text: q numeric scalar representing quantile threshold. mu_t numeric scalar vector giving location parameter t-distribution treatment group. mu_c numeric scalar vector giving location parameter t-distribution control group. sd_t positive numeric scalar vector giving scale parameter t-distribution treatment group. sd_c positive numeric scalar vector giving scale parameter t-distribution control group. nu_t numeric scalar giving degrees freedom t-distribution treatment group. title: Cumulative Distribution Function of the Difference of Two Independent t-Distributed Variables via Numerical Integration — ptdiff_NI
text:
upper-tail probability obtained via convolution formula: $$P(T_t - T_c > q)   = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty}     F_{t(\mu_c, \sigma_c^2, 
u_c)}(x - q)\,     f_{t(\mu_t, \sigma_t^2, 
u_t)}(x)\, dx$$ \(f_{t(\mu_t, \sigma_t^2, 
u_t)}\) density \(T_t\) \(F_{t(\mu_c, \sigma_c^2, 
u_c)}\) CDF \(T_c\). integral evaluated adaptive Gauss-Kronrod quadrature via stats::integrate. input parameters vectors, mapply applies scalar integration function across parameter sets. Advantages: Exact within numerical precision. Handles arbitrary parameter combinations. Computational note: method substantially slower Moment-Matching approximation (ptdiff_MM) calls integrate() per parameter set. large-scale simulation (many parameter sets), prefer CalcMethod = 'MM' pbayesdecisionprob1cont.

what: Generate Random Samples from a Dirichlet Distribution — rdirichlet
title: Generate Random Samples from a Dirichlet Distribution — rdirichlet
text: Generates random samples Dirichlet distribution using Gamma representation: \(Y_i \sim \mathrm{Gamma}(\alpha_i, 1)\) independently \(= 1, \ldots, K\), \((Y_1 / S, \ldots, Y_K / S) \sim \mathrm{Dirichlet}(\alpha_1, \ldots, \alpha_K)\), \(S = \sum_{=1}^{K} Y_i\).

title: Generate Random Samples from a Dirichlet Distribution — rdirichlet
code: rdirichlet(n, alpha)

title: Generate Random Samples from a Dirichlet Distribution — rdirichlet
text: n positive integer specifying number random vectors generate. alpha numeric vector length \(K \ge 2\) containing positive concentration parameters Dirichlet distribution. elements must strictly positive.

title: Generate Random Samples from a Dirichlet Distribution — rdirichlet
text: numeric matrix dimensions n x K row one random draw Dirichlet distribution, elements [0, 1] row summing 1. n = 1, numeric vector length K returned.

title: Generate Random Samples from a Dirichlet Distribution — rdirichlet
text: Dirichlet distribution multivariate generalisation Beta distribution commonly used conjugate prior multinomial proportions Bayesian statistics. probability density function : $$f(x_1, \ldots, x_K) =   \frac{\Gamma\!\left(\sum_{=1}^{K} \alpha_i\right)}        {\prod_{=1}^{K} \Gamma(\alpha_i)}   \prod_{=1}^{K} x_i^{\alpha_i - 1}$$ \(x_i > 0\) \(\sum_{=1}^{K} x_i = 1\). Key properties: marginal follows Beta distribution: \(X_i \sim \mathrm{Beta}\!\left(\alpha_i,\,         \sum_{l \neq } \alpha_l\right)\). \(E[X_i] = \alpha_i / \sum_{l=1}^{K} \alpha_l\). Components negatively correlated unless one component dominates. Implementation steps: Generate independent \(Y_i \sim \mathrm{Gamma}(\alpha_i, 1)\) \(= 1, \ldots, K\). Normalise: \(X_i = Y_i / \sum_{l=1}^{K} Y_l\).

title: Generate Random Samples from a Dirichlet Distribution — rdirichlet
code: # Example 1: Generate 5 samples from Dirichlet(1, 1, 1) - uniform on simplex samples <- rdirichlet(5, c(1, 1, 1)) print(samples) #>           [,1]      [,2]       [,3] #> [1,] 0.5734462 0.2324977 0.19405611 #> [2,] 0.5605910 0.3467968 0.09261223 #> [3,] 0.3984420 0.1310575 0.47050046 #> [4,] 0.5574038 0.2393656 0.20323054 #> [5,] 0.7532899 0.1471133 0.09959677 rowSums(samples)  # Each row should sum to 1 #> [1] 1 1 1 1 1  # Example 2: Generate samples with unequal concentrations samples <- rdirichlet(1000, c(2, 5, 3)) colMeans(samples)  # Expected values: approximately c(0.2, 0.5, 0.3) #> [1] 0.1957181 0.4990865 0.3051954  # Example 3: Sparse Dirichlet (small alpha values) samples <- rdirichlet(100, c(0.1, 0.1, 0.1, 0.1)) head(samples)  # Most weight concentrated on one component #>              [,1]         [,2]         [,3]         [,4] #> [1,] 9.699471e-01 1.626595e-04 6.162727e-11 2.989021e-02 #> [2,] 3.884496e-03 9.956347e-01 4.808343e-04 9.754816e-17 #> [3,] 9.841950e-01 2.341152e-03 7.885939e-14 1.346385e-02 #> [4,] 6.498627e-10 1.940916e-01 8.059073e-01 1.065467e-06 #> [5,] 9.989880e-01 2.176733e-06 4.774144e-09 1.009798e-03 #> [6,] 9.839987e-02 6.097275e-03 8.955029e-01 1.741122e-11  # Example 4: Concentrated Dirichlet (large alpha values) samples <- rdirichlet(100, c(100, 100, 100)) colMeans(samples)  # Concentrated around c(1/3, 1/3, 1/3) #> [1] 0.3313643 0.3342281 0.3344076  # Example 5: Bayesian update with Jeffreys prior for 4 categories prior_alpha     <- c(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5) observed_counts <- c(10, 5, 8, 7) posterior_samples <- rdirichlet(1000, prior_alpha + observed_counts) colMeans(posterior_samples)  # Posterior mean #> [1] 0.3269486 0.1735236 0.2659320 0.2335958
